  release_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number issued upon successful inspection, authorizing shipment or installation. Format: RC-YYYYNNNN. Required before goods can be shipped to site.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection release certificate was issued and materials were authorized for shipment or installation.']
  shipment_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was formally authorized to ship the inspected materials to the construction site following successful inspection release.']
  technical_specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical specification document or drawing number against which the inspection was performed.']
  test_report_document_number varchar [note: 'Document control number for laboratory or field test reports (e.g., NDT reports, hydrostatic test reports, performance test reports).']
  witness_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the client organization or third-party entity that witnessed the inspection, if witness was required.']
  witness_representative_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual representative from the witness organization who attended the inspection.']

  Note: 'Vendor quality compliance and documentation records encompassing Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT), pre-shipment inspections, and all vendor-submitted technical documents/submittals required under a PO or framework agreement. Captures inspection order number, vendor, material/equipment tag, inspection type (FAT, SAT, third-party inspection, dimensional check), inspection date, inspector name, inspection result (pass, conditional pass, fail), punch list items, release certificate number, and associated vendor documents (material data sheets, mill certificates, test reports, ITP submissions, O&M manuals, as-built drawings) with document number, revision, submission date, review status (under review, approved, approved with comments, rejected), document type, and Aconex transmittal reference. Required before goods are shipped to site. SSOT for vendor quality evidence and technical submittals in procurement.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_document {
  vendor_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor-submitted document record. Primary key for the vendor document entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract or framework agreement under which this document submission is required. Links to the contract master data.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset to which this document relates. Links to the equipment master data. Applicable for O&M manuals, test reports, and as-built drawings.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this vendor document is associated. Links to the project master data.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.document_register. Business justification: Vendor‑supplied documents are stored in the document register; linking provides audit trail of vendor documentation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or product to which this document relates. Links to the material master data. Applicable for material data sheets, mill certificates, and test reports.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order under which this document was submitted. Links to the PO that governs the document submission requirement.']
  submitted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Links vendor document submission to the responsible employee; supports document control and accountability.']
  superseded_by_document_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newer vendor document that supersedes this document. Used to maintain document version history and ensure users reference the latest approved version.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier who submitted this document. Links to the vendor master data.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was formally approved for use in the project. Applicable when review_status is approved or approved_with_comments.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or authority who granted final approval of this document.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document meets all contractual, technical, and regulatory compliance requirements. True if compliant, False if non-compliant or deviations exist.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the document content, indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions. Aligns with organizational data governance policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor document record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the document by business function: technical, quality, compliance, commercial, safety, or environmental.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format or media type of the submitted document. Common formats include PDF, DWG (AutoCAD), DXF, DOCX, XLSX, JPG, PNG, ZIP, or native CAD formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|dwg|dxf|docx|xlsx|jpg|png|zip|native_cad — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or reference number assigned to this vendor document by the vendor or the document management system. Used for tracking and correspondence.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or name of the vendor document, summarizing its content and purpose.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor document by its purpose and content. Common types include material data sheets, mill certificates, test reports, ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) submissions, O&M (Operations and Maintenance) manuals, and as-built drawings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: material_data_sheet|mill_certificate|test_report|itp_submission|o&m_manual|as_built_drawing|warranty_certificate|compliance_certificate|technical_specification|quality_plan — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this document becomes invalid or superseded. Applicable for time-limited certificates, warranties, or test reports with validity periods.']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the document file in megabytes. Used for storage management and system performance monitoring.']
  itp_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Inspection and Test Plan to which this document submission is linked. Used to track ITP compliance and hold point releases.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the primary language of the document content. Examples: EN (English), FR (French), ES (Spanish), AR (Arabic).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this vendor document record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor document record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  linked_drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the engineering drawing or BIM model to which this document is related. Used to establish traceability between vendor documents and design deliverables.']
  ncr_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any Non-Conformance Report raised against this document or the materials/equipment it describes. Links to the NCR record for quality tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context related to this vendor document. Used for internal communication and audit trail.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Number of pages in the document. Applicable for PDF and printed document formats.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for why the document was rejected. Applicable when review_status is rejected or resubmit_required.']
  required_submission_date date [note: 'Contractual or planned date by which the vendor was required to submit this document. Used to track compliance with submission schedules.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years this document must be retained per contractual, regulatory, or organizational policy requirements. Used for records management and archival planning.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed comments, feedback, or observations provided by the reviewer during the document review process. May include technical corrections, clarifications, or conditions of approval.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the document review was completed and a decision (approved, rejected, etc.) was made.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the internal review and approval of this document should be completed. Used to manage review cycle times and SLA compliance.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the document in the review and approval workflow. Tracks whether the document is pending review, under review, approved, approved with comments, rejected, or requires resubmission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_review|under_review|approved|approved_with_comments|rejected|resubmit_required|superseded|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team who conducted the technical or quality review of this document.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of the document. Tracks changes and updates to the document over time. Common formats include numeric (1, 2, 3) or alphanumeric (A, B, C, Rev01).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor submitted this document to the project team or document management system.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the vendor document was submitted into the document management system. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Aconex or document management system transmittal under which this document was formally submitted. Links to the transmittal record for audit trail.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element or code to which this document is assigned. Used for project cost control and document organization.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this vendor document record in the system.']

  Note: 'Vendor-submitted technical documents and submittals required under a PO or framework agreement, including material data sheets, mill certificates, test reports, ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) submissions, O&M manuals, and as-built drawings. Captures document number, vendor, PO reference, document type, revision, submission date, review status (under review, approved, approved with comments, rejected), and Aconex transmittal reference.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_bid {
  procurement_bid_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the Bid association']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the subcontractor firm']
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the purchase requisition']
  amount decimal [note: 'Monetary amount offered by the subcontractor for the requisition']
  evaluation_score decimal [note: 'Score assigned by the evaluation team to the bid']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bid was submitted']

  Note: 'Represents a bid submitted by a subcontractor firm in response to a purchase requisition. Each record links one purchase_requisition to one subcontractor firm and captures bid-specific data.. Existence Justification: A purchase requisition is issued by a project team and multiple subcontractor firms can submit bids for that requisition. Each subcontractor can bid on many different purchase requisitions. The bid itself carries data such as the bid amount, evaluation score, and submission timestamp, which are managed as a distinct business entity.'
}

Table procurement.approval_workflow {
  approval_workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approval_workflow']
  parent_approval_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on approval_workflow (parent_approval_workflow_id)']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that triggers the workflow\'s approval process.']
  approval_time_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours to complete the approval process.']
  auto_approval boolean [note: 'True if the workflow can be auto‑approved under defined conditions.']
  auto_approval_condition varchar [note: 'Condition expression that enables automatic approval.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the workflow in procurement systems.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance rules applicable to the workflow.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the workflow for budgeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the workflow record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the threshold amount.']
  default_approver_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role that is the default approver for this workflow.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Business department that owns the workflow.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the workflow purpose and scope.']
  documentation_type varchar [note: 'Allowed format of required documentation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the workflow is retired; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the workflow becomes active for use.']
  escalation_policy varchar [note: 'Policy that defines how approvals are escalated when thresholds are not met.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'True if the workflow is archived and no longer active.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workflow must be used for the associated procurement process.']
  is_parallel_approval boolean [note: 'True if multiple approvers can act concurrently.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the workflow record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the workflow record.']
  max_approval_level varchar [note: 'Highest hierarchical level required to approve a request.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the workflow.']
  requires_documentation boolean [note: 'True if supporting documentation is required for approval.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the workflow.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the workflow.']
  step_count varchar [note: 'Total number of approval steps defined in the workflow.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workflow (e.g., standard, custom, ad‑hoc).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the workflow definition for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the workflow record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for approval_workflow. Referenced by approval_workflow_id.'
}

  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the service price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the service expires or is discontinued (nullable).']

  name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the service offering.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the service.']
  price_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard price of the service before taxes.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the service.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the service.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Defined SLA terms for the service.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service provided.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the service.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the service is measured or billed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard warranty period provided with the service.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service. Referenced by service_id.'
}

Table material.master {
  master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for master']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Required for material master creation audit; compliance reports need the employee who created each material record.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Required for Procurement\'s Approved Supplier List process; links each material to the subcontractor approved to supply it, enabling sourcing decisions and compliance checks.']
  substitution_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved substitute material.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier for this material.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material record was approved for use.']
  conformance_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the compliance certificate was issued.']
  conformance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the material’s compliance certificate (e.g., ISO, ASTM).']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of one unit of material in the specified currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material master record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for cost fields.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Length×Width×Height dimensions in centimeters, formatted as LxWxH.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the material batch expires.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the material is classified as hazardous under OSHA/EPA regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order to receipt.']
  leeds_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is eligible for LEED credit consideration.']
  leeds_credit_applicability varchar [note: 'LEED credit categories the material can contribute to.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material record.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for quality control and recall management.']
  manufacturer_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the manufacturer’s country.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the material.']
  master_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, including typical applications.']
  master_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique code or catalogue number for the material.']
  material_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the material\'s source.']
  max_order_qty varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase.']
  min_order_qty varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  minimum_performance_criteria varchar [note: 'Minimum performance thresholds the material must meet (e.g., compressive strength).']
  reorder_point_qty varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Buffer stock kept to protect against demand variability.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the material can be stored before it expires.']
  specification_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or class of the material as defined by the governing standard.']
  specification_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard governing the material (e.g., ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC).']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, segregation).']
  substitution_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the client/engineer approval document for the substitution.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'Engineering or client‑approved justification for the substitution.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑assigned part number for the material.']
  technical_specification varchar [note: 'Full technical specification document reference or summary.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory and consumption tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the material record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the material record for change control.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume of a single unit of material in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of a single unit of material in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all construction materials and consumables used across projects. Captures material identity, detailed technical specifications (applicable standards — ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC — grade/class, minimum performance criteria, specification code, specification revision), classification (concrete, steel, MEP components, aggregates, timber, formwork, etc.), unit of measure, approved equivalent substitutes with engineering justification, LEED compliance flags, LEED credit applicability, hazardous material indicators, shelf life, storage requirements, and supplier cross-references. Manages specification lifecycle (draft, approved, superseded) and material qualification status. Records approved substitutions with reason, client/engineer approval reference, cost impact, and affected BOQ lines. This is the SSOT for material identity, technical specifications, and approved substitutes in the construction enterprise — all transactions reference this record.'
}

Table material.warehouse {
  warehouse_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warehouse record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Some warehouses are owned or leased by a client; the FK enables asset ownership and liability tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or department responsible for warehouse oversight.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or project to which the warehouse is attached.']
  access_control_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to control entry to the warehouse.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the warehouse.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum usable floor area in square metres.']
  capacity_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total weight the warehouse can safely store, expressed in metric tonnes.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the hazardous‑material certification expires.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the warehouse is located.']
  climate_control_type varchar [note: 'Specific climate control method applied when temperature_controlled is true.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the warehouse location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the warehouse became operational or was officially opened.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the warehouse ceased operations or is scheduled for closure (null if still active).']
  gps_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Estimated positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates, in metres.']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  hazardous_material_certification_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of hazardous‑material certification held, if any.']
  inventory_management_system varchar [note: 'Name of the downstream system that tracks inventory within this warehouse (e.g., SAP WM, Procore).']
  is_hazardous_material_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse holds a valid hazardous‑material storage certification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating window (e.g., 08:00‑17:00).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the warehouse address.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Overall security classification of the warehouse.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the warehouse location.']
  storage_zone_details varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of internal zones, racks, bins, or yard sections.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the warehouse maintains a controlled temperature environment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse record.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible alphanumeric code used to reference the warehouse in external systems.']
  warehouse_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the warehouse, its purpose, and special characteristics.']
  warehouse_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the warehouse or laydown yard.']
  warehouse_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the warehouse.']
  warehouse_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse based on construction and regulatory characteristics.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical warehouse, laydown yard, and storage locations across construction sites and central depots. Tracks warehouse type (bonded, open yard, climate-controlled, hazmat-certified), capacity (area sqm, weight tonnes), GPS coordinates, site association, custodian, operating hours, hazardous material storage certification, and granular storage locations (rack positions, bin rows, open yard zones). Supports multi-site inventory management, material transfer logistics, and FIFO/FEFO stock management for batch-tracked materials.'
}

Table material.stock_level {
  stock_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each stock level record.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.material_master']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.warehouse']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production batch, used for traceability of concrete, steel, etc.']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity held due to quality issues, holds, or regulatory restrictions.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated to specific construction activities or contracts.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of material in the base currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock level record was first created.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is considered unusable or non‑compliant.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity that has been dispatched but not yet received at the location.']
  last_movement_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent stock transaction affecting this record.']
  last_movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the most recent stock transaction.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the stock level record.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage site where the stock resides.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for a lot of material, often used for quality tracking.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or catalogue number for the material.']
  max_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper threshold for inventory to avoid over‑stocking.']
  min_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lowest permissible inventory level before stock is considered understocked.']
  quality_inspection_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently undergoing quality inspection or testing.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total amount of material physically present in the location.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment request.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity earmarked for pending work packages or purchase orders.']
  safety_stock decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer quantity maintained to protect against demand variability or supply delays.']
  stock_level_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock quantity.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'External identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity fields.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand inventory position for each material at each warehouse/storage location. Captures unrestricted stock, quality-inspection stock, blocked stock, reserved stock, in-transit quantities, and committed (reserved-for-activity) quantities. Tracks reorder point, minimum/maximum stock levels, safety stock thresholds, and last movement date. Updated in real-time by stock movements (receipts, issues, transfers, returns). Critical for BOQ reconciliation, preventing site stoppages due to material shortages, and supporting procurement trigger automation.'
}

Table material.stock_movement {
  stock_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stock_movement']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Links goods receipt to the activity that triggered the material purchase, enabling receipt‑to‑schedule reconciliation and progress tracking.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Goods receipts are tied to client orders; needed for delivery confirmation and contract compliance.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the receipt.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the receipt inspection.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Goods receipt must capture which subcontractor delivered the material for invoicing, compliance, and traceability per construction site delivery logs.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for Inventory Valuation: goods receipt creates a GL entry to record inventory value in the general ledger.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock movement records must be linked to the originating purchase order for audit trails, cost allocation, and receipt verification.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or warehouse where material is received.']
  accounting_entry_posted boolean [note: 'True when the financial posting for the receipt has been completed.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the material physically arrived at the site.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the received material.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the received material.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the receipt expense is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the supplier\'s delivery note accompanying the shipment.']
  destination_warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or site where the material is being received.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date as per the purchase order.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transportation cost associated with delivering the material.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the freight cost.']
  goods_receipt_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the receipt is from a purchase, internal transfer, or return.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the received material before taxes and discounts.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection for the receipt.']
  is_critical_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as critical for project execution.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the material is hazardous and subject to special handling.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for materials that are tracked by lot.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Master data identifier for the material (e.g., steel grade, concrete mix).']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and any discounts.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accounting entry was posted.']
  quality_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the quality certificate attached to the material batch.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of material physically received, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the receipt (e.g., GR‑2023‑000123).']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Overall processing status of the goods receipt.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the goods receipt was posted in the system.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or notes about the receipt.']
  source_warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or location from which the material originated.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or site location where the material is stored.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the goods receipt.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to the receipt.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the received quantity (e.g., kilograms, meters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the goods receipt record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the receipt record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of materials physically received at site or warehouse against a Purchase Order (PO). Captures GR date, PO reference, delivery note number, supplier, material, quantity received, unit of measure, batch/lot number, storage location, receiving inspector, and GR status (partial, complete, over-delivery). Triggers quality inspection workflow and updates stock levels. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MIGO GR posting and Procore material delivery logs.'
}

Table material.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the goods issue transaction.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Primavera activity identifier linked to the material consumption.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Required for tracking consumable material issues to specific equipment for cost allocation, compliance, and operational reporting.']
  batch_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used for end‑to‑end traceability of the material batch.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Material issues are charged to a specific client; the link supports client cost tracking and invoicing.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the issue.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost Allocation: material issue costs are charged to a cost center for project accounting and financial reporting.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Materials Issue process records which craft worker receives material for job tasks, needed for labor cost allocation and safety compliance.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material being issued.']
  primary_goods_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who requested the material issue.']
  return_issue_goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the goods issue record that reverses this transaction, if any.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier that provided the material.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or stock location from which the material was issued.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the goods issue.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the issue was approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by users regarding the issue.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issued material complies with project specifications and safety standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the goods issue record was first created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the delivery note accompanying the material receipt.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is no longer usable (if applicable).']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the goods issue record.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the issued material before taxes or discounts.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the material for handling and storage.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'True if a post‑issue inspection is mandated for the material.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required inspection.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed.']
  is_returned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issued material was later returned to inventory.']
  issue_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the goods issue for tracking and reconciliation.']
  issue_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for issuing the material.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material was physically issued from inventory.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material (e.g., grade, size).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and any discounts.']
  quantity_issued decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material issued, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the material was received into the warehouse before issuance.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating purchase order or requisition.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Applicable tax amount for the issued material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is measured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the goods issue record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element to which the material is allocated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of materials issued from warehouse/stock to a WBS element, cost center, or work front for construction consumption. Captures issue date, WBS/cost code, material, quantity issued, unit of measure, batch/lot, issuing warehouse, requestor, and issue reason. Drives material cost allocation to project activities and updates stock levels. Supports EVM cost tracking and BOQ consumption reconciliation.'
}

Table material.stock_transfer {
  stock_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the stock transfer transaction.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Tracks material transfers that are initiated for a specific construction project in the Project Transfer Log.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Material Transfer SOP records the crew responsible for moving stock between warehouses, supporting logistics accountability and cost tracking.']
  destination_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage location receiving the material.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material being transferred (e.g., concrete, steel, MEP component).']
  source_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage location from which the material is transferred.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material was actually received at the destination warehouse.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for traceability of the material (e.g., concrete batch, steel roll).']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics provider or carrier responsible for moving the material.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional notes or remarks about the transfer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock transfer record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to related documents such as transfer orders, packing lists, or customs paperwork.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the material is expected to arrive at the destination.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of material moved in the transfer, expressed in the unit specified by unit_of_measure.']
  stock_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer (e.g., draft, planned, in‑transit, completed, cancelled).']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the stock transfer for tracking and communication.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the material movement, such as site reallocation or surplus return.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material movement was initiated or recorded.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the transfer.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity (kilograms, cubic meters, pieces, tons, pounds, gallons).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the stock transfer record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of material movements between warehouses, sites, or storage locations within the enterprise. Captures transfer date, source warehouse, destination warehouse, material, quantity, batch/lot, transfer reason (site reallocation, project closure, surplus return), transport reference, and transfer status (in-transit, completed, cancelled). Supports multi-site material balancing and inter-project material sharing.'
}

Table material.batch_lot {
  batch_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the batch or lot record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Batch/lot traceability often requires knowing the client for warranty, quality and regulatory reporting.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project using the material.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: Required: Batch lot compliance records need to point to the Regulatory Authority whose regulation the batch satisfies; standard practice in construction.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Assigns a responsible employee for each batch/lot; required for traceability and quality incident investigations.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site where the material is applied.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the warehouse holding the material.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned batch number used to identify a specific production run.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the batch/lot.']
  certificate_of_conformance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the certificate confirming material meets specifications.']
  cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the batch/lot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch/lot record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disposing of excess or waste material.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material should not be used.']
  inspection_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the last inspection met acceptance criteria.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or safety inspection.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability when a batch is split into multiple lots.']
  lot_traceability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot is subject to full chain‑of‑custody tracking.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that produced the material.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the material, including grade or specification.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of material represented by the batch/lot.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or observations.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the material batch was manufactured.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of material in the batch expressed in the unit of measure.']
  quarantine_status varchar [note: 'Current quarantine state of the batch/lot.']
  receipt_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the goods receipt document.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the batch was received at the construction site or warehouse.']
  safety_data_sheet_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the SDS document for hazardous materials.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (e.g., warehouse aisle) where the batch is stored.']
  supplier varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that supplied the material to the construction site.']
  test_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material batch passed required quality tests.']
  test_results_date date [note: 'Date the material tests were performed.']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of test outcomes for the batch (e.g., compressive strength).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity field.']
  unit_volume decimal(12,3) [note: 'Volume per unit of material (e.g., cubic meters per bag).']
  unit_weight decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight per unit of material (e.g., kg per piece).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch/lot record.']

  Note: 'Batch and lot traceability master for materials requiring full chain-of-custody tracking — concrete pours (batch plant ticket), structural steel heats, rebar coils, MEP cable drums, and chemical admixtures. Captures batch number, lot number, manufacturer, production date, expiry date, certificate of conformance reference, test results summary, quarantine status, and linked goods receipt. Supports FAT/SAT traceability and NCR investigations.'
}

Table material.conformance_certificate {
  conformance_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for conformance_certificate']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project where the material is used.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Links certificate to the inspector who performed the test; needed for HSE compliance and audit trails.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material to which this certificate applies.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.bid_submission. Business justification: REQUIRED: Bids must attach material conformance certificates to demonstrate compliance with client specifications and regulatory requirements.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the certificate was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or authority that approved the certificate for use.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate number assigned by the issuing authority or lab.']
  certificate_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title or description of the certificate (e.g., "Structural Steel Grade A Certification").']
  certificate_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certificate document.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination based on test results.']
  conformance_certificate_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the certificate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certificate expires or becomes invalid.']
  humidity_recorded decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity during the material test (%).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate is currently active and usable.']
  issuing_lab varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory or authority that issued the certificate.']
  location varchar [note: 'Site or warehouse location code associated with the certificate.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of the material (e.g., steel, concrete, MEP component).']
  measured_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the measured value (e.g., MPa for strength).']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Primary quantitative measurement from the test (e.g., compressive strength).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the certificate.']
  related_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file path or URL) to the digital copy of the certificate.']
  result_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of test outcomes, observations, and any deviations.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for updates to the certificate.']
  temperature_recorded decimal(8,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the material test was conducted (°C).']
  test_date date [note: 'Date on which the material test was performed.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the test (e.g., compression, tensile, chemical analysis).']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the material test indicating pass or fail.']
  test_standard varchar [note: 'Standard used for testing (e.g., ASTM C39, ISO 9001).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certificate record.']

  Note: 'Material conformance and test certificate records for construction materials — mill certificates for structural steel, concrete mix design approvals, aggregate gradation reports, MEP component type-test certificates, and LEED-compliant material declarations. Captures certificate number, issuing lab/authority, material reference, batch/lot, test standard (ASTM, BS, ISO), test results, pass/fail status, expiry date, and document reference. Mandatory for QA/QC ITP compliance and regulatory handover packages.'
}

Table material.material_boq_line {
  material_boq_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the BOQ line item.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Required for BOQ line to be assigned to the scheduled activity that will consume the material, enabling cost‑tracking, schedule integration, and material requirement reports.']
  boq_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent Bill of Quantities (BOQ) record to which this line belongs.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Required for BOQ Budgeting: each BOQ line is associated with a cost code to track planned vs. actual costs.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Needed for BOQ line allocation; assigns a subcontractor responsible for delivering the material, used in cost tracking and contract award reports.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or item that this BOQ line represents.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Needed for BOQ generation to ensure each line item references the approved sustainable material data (recycled content, EPD) as required by green certification specifications.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change order that introduced this line, if applicable.']
  contract_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that is contractually committed in the BOQ.']
  is_change_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line originates from a change order (true) or the original BOQ (false).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the material or work item represented by the line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the BOQ, used for sorting and display.']
  material_boq_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOQ line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned quantity of the material as defined in the BOQ line, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOQ line record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOQ line record.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the line after each amendment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that supplied the BOQ line (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Procore).']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated line value (contract_quantity × unit_rate) in the project currency.']
  trade_discipline varchar [note: 'Construction trade or discipline (e.g., concrete, steel, MEP) associated with the line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., m3, ton, pcs).']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of measure for the material.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element code linking the line to the project hierarchy.']

  Note: 'Bill of Quantities (BOQ) line item representing material and work quantities baselined for a project, used as the material domain\'s reference for take-off reconciliation, consumption tracking, and procurement planning. Captures BOQ item code, description, unit of measure, contract quantity, unit rate, total value, WBS element, trade/discipline, and revision history. While contractual BOQ ownership may reside in the contract domain, this product serves as the material domain\'s operational view for MTO reconciliation, material consumption variance analysis, and progress measurement against planned quantities.'
}

Table material.mto_line {
  mto_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mto_line']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the BIM model version containing the material definition.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with this MTO line.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for MTO Cost Planning: material take‑off quantities are allocated to cost centers for cost estimation and forecasting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the specific material.']
  mto_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent MTO document to which this line belongs.']
  actual_received_date date [note: 'Date when the material was received on site.']
  actual_received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material actually received on site.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the MTO line.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line was approved.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a change order that modifies this MTO line.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Applicable certification or standard (e.g., ISO9001, ASTM) that the material meets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for cost estimates.']
  design_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material calculated from design drawings or BIM models.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline associated with the material.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the engineering drawing that defines the material requirement.']
  estimated_unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of material for procurement planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is no longer usable or compliant.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is critical to project schedule or safety.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record has been logically deleted.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order to delivery for the material.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the MTO header.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  material_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed specification or grade of the material as defined in the BOQ.']
  mto_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MTO line.']
  net_required_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total material quantity required after applying wastage factor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks about the MTO line.']
  procurement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the procurement process for this material line.']
  quantity_uom_factor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Factor to convert between alternative units of measure for the material.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the material must be on site to meet construction schedule.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the drawing or BIM model for this line.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Link to the safety data sheet document for the material.']
  site_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the construction site or warehouse where material will be used or stored.']
  supplier_preferred varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the preferred supplier for this material.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost calculated as net_required_quantity multiplied by estimated_unit_price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the material quantities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the MTO line record.']
  variance_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Difference between total_estimated_cost and actual cost incurred.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between net_required_quantity and actual_received_quantity.']
  wastage_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage factor applied to account for material loss during construction.']

  Note: 'Material Take-Off (MTO) line item derived from engineering drawings and BIM models, representing the estimated material quantities required for construction. Captures MTO item, material master reference, design quantity, wastage factor, net required quantity, unit of measure, drawing/BIM model reference, discipline (civil, structural, MEP), revision number, and MTO status (draft, approved, issued-for-procurement). Bridges design domain and procurement/inventory planning.'
}

Table material.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for requisition']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved or rejected the requisition.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Client‑initiated material requisitions need client_account_id for billing, cost allocation and compliance reporting.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the construction project to which the requisition belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Budget Control: material requisitions are budgeted and approved against a specific cost center.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Requisition may be directed to a specific subcontractor; linking enables automated purchase order routing and subcontractor performance tracking.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Master data identifier of the material or component being requested.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order generated when stock is insufficient.']
  requisition_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or subcontractor who created the requisition.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical construction site or location where the material is needed.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location identifier from which the material will be drawn.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the managerial or engineering approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Associated change order identifier if the requisition results from a scope change.']
  compliance_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to any regulatory or safety document attached to the requisition (e.g., MSDS number).']
  cost_estimate_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of the requested material before taxes and discounts.']
  cost_estimate_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected net cost after tax and any discounts.']
  cost_estimate_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated tax component applicable to the material cost.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for the cost estimate.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date on which the material was actually issued to the site or received from a supplier.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Progress state of the material issue or purchase fulfillment.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is an emergency (e.g., safety‑critical) request.']
  is_stock_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requested material is currently available in warehouse inventory.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the material is needed, supporting approval decisions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information supplied by the requester.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority indicating the urgency of the material request.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of material requested, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the requisition record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the requisition record.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition was initially submitted by the site team.']
  requester_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or trade responsible for the material request.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the material must be available on site to avoid schedule impact.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the requisition (e.g., RQ‑2024‑00123).']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition.']
  safety_review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the safety compliance review required for certain hazardous materials.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to the material cost.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the requested quantity (e.g., kilograms, cubic meters).']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element that the material request is charged to.']

  Note: 'Site or project team request for materials to be issued from warehouse stock or procured. Captures requisition number, requesting WBS/work front, required material, quantity, required-by date, priority, justification, approval status (pending, approved, rejected), approver, and fulfilment status. Triggers either a goods issue (if stock available) or a purchase requisition in procurement. Supports site operations planning and material demand management.'
}

Table material.physical_inventory {
  physical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the physical inventory count record.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material being counted.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the count record.']
  audit_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the count record.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  count_date date [note: 'Date on which the inventory count was performed.']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to each inventory count event, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the count is a full inventory, a cycle count, or a spot check.']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material physically counted during the inventory event.']
  counter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or system that performed the count.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inventory count record was initially created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the material, if applicable (e.g., chemicals, adhesives).']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Specific sub‑location or aisle within the warehouse where the count was performed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier when batch tracking is not used.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material (e.g., concrete grade, steel type).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the counter.']
  physical_inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count record.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the count results were posted to the financial period for valuation.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item was recounted due to a discrepancy.']
  system_book_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material recorded in the ERP system before the physical count.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the material quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inventory count record.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between system book quantity and counted quantity (counted_quantity - system_book_quantity).']
  variance_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the quantity variance based on standard cost.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the warehouse or storage location where the count took place.']

  Note: 'Physical stock count event record for warehouse cycle counts and annual inventory audits. Captures count date, warehouse, count type (full, cycle, spot), material, system book quantity, physically counted quantity, variance quantity, variance value, count status (open, counted, posted), counter name, and recount flag. Supports inventory accuracy management, shrinkage tracking, and financial period-end stock valuation.'
}

Table material.specification {
  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for specification']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Specification defines technical details for a material; linking to material_master removes duplicate type and grade attributes and connects the spec to its parent material.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Required: Material specifications are tied to Regulatory Obligations that define performance and safety standards; essential for compliance verification.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.tender. Business justification: REQUIRED: Tender documents cite specific material specifications that suppliers must meet; linking enables traceability of spec compliance.']
  applicable_project_type varchar [note: 'Project categories where this specification may be applied.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the specification.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of regulatory or client compliance requirements tied to the specification.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the material per unit of measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost per unit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the specification becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the specification expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Guidelines for safe handling and transport of the material.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this specification is the default for its material type.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time from order to delivery for the material.']
  leeds_credit_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the specification contributes to a LEED credit (yes) or not (no).']
  material_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the material (e.g., concrete, steel, MEP).']
  min_compressive_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum compressive strength required for the material, expressed in megapascal.']
  min_modulus_of_elasticity_gpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum modulus of elasticity required, expressed in gigapascal.']
  min_tensile_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum tensile strength required for the material, expressed in megapascal.']
  min_yield_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum yield strength required for the material, expressed in megapascal.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic safety data sheet (SDS) for the material.']
  specification_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the specification (e.g., SPEC-00123).']
  specification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the specification purpose, scope, and application.']
  specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material specification.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Specific standard code (e.g., ASTM C150, ISO 9001).']
  standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing standard body (e.g., ASTM, ISO, BS, ACI).']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions required (e.g., temperature, humidity).']
  substitute_specification_code varchar [note: 'Code of an alternative specification that can be used in place of this one.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for this material specification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the specification.']
  warranty_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or exclusions that apply to the warranty.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of warranty coverage for the material, expressed in months.']

  Note: 'Technical specification master for construction materials defining approved material standards, grades, and performance requirements for a project or enterprise. Captures specification code, material category, applicable standard (ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC), grade/class, minimum performance criteria, approved equivalent substitutes, LEED credit applicability, and specification status (draft, approved, superseded). Links design requirements to procurement and QA/QC inspection criteria.'
}

Table material.approved_material_list {
  approved_material_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each approved material list record.']
  approving_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Replaces free‑text approving engineer with FK; required for project approval records and engineer accountability.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the approved material list belongs.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alternative material approved as a substitution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the material received formal approval.']
  approved_material_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the approved material entry.']
  approved_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of the material approved for use on the project.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the material lot.']
  client_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code from the client/owner confirming approval.']
  compliance_certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the compliance certificate.']
  compliance_certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date the compliance certificate was issued.']
  compliance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the certificate confirming material compliance.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approved material record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for cost values.']
  environmental_rating varchar [note: 'Sustainability certification or rating applicable to the material.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material approval is no longer valid.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Standard grade or specification level of the material (e.g., ASTM A36, C30/37).']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Operational guidance for safe handling and installation of the material.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the hazardous material, if applicable.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  is_primary_material boolean [note: 'Indicates if the material is a primary (non‑substitutable) component of the design.']
  is_substituted boolean [note: 'Flag showing whether this entry represents a substitution for another approved material.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for quality and regulatory tracking.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that produces the material.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the material (e.g., concrete, structural steel, MEP component).']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the material as used in project documentation.']
  material_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the material was manufactured.']
  material_origin_state varchar [note: 'State or province of manufacture, if applicable.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned model or part number identifying the specific material variant.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks related to the approval.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the project during which the material was approved.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the material.']
  specification varchar [note: 'Detailed technical specification or data sheet reference for the material.']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions required for the material (e.g., temperature, humidity).']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material may be substituted with an equivalent.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for quantity tracking of the material.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage ends.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of the manufacturer warranty expressed in months.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Project-level Approved Material List (AML) / Approved Vendor List for materials — the authoritative register of materials and brands approved by the client/engineer for use on a specific project. Captures material category, approved manufacturer, product model/grade, approval date, approving engineer, client approval reference, substitution status, and expiry date. Mandatory for contract compliance and submittal management. Prevents use of non-compliant materials on site.'
}

Table material.wastage {
  wastage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wastage']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract under which the material was procured.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Waste records are allocated to a client for environmental reporting, cost recovery and contractual penalties.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the wastage belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Waste Cost Tracking: waste records must be posted to the responsible cost center for cost control and reporting.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Wastage tracking logs the worker generating waste, required for waste cost attribution and regulatory reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material type that was wasted.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Required: Waste records must be linked to the specific Regulatory Obligation governing disposal; mandatory for reporting and audit.']
