  regulatory_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory requirement, standard, or code that mandates this inspection. Examples: OSHA 1926.550 for cranes, ASME Section VIII for pressure vessels, manufacturer maintenance manual reference.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this inspection record originated. SAP PM: SAP Plant Maintenance module. Intelex: HSE Management system. HCSS HeavyJob: Field operations system. Procore: Construction management platform. Manual entry: paper-based inspection digitized.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of inspection, relevant for outdoor equipment inspections where environmental factors may affect inspection validity or equipment condition assessment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a statutory, regulatory, or internal safety inspection performed on a piece of equipment, and the master register of all resulting compliance certificates issued. Captures inspection type (pre-start check, periodic statutory inspection, OSHA compliance check, crane load test, lifting gear inspection, pressure vessel test, FAT/SAT), inspection date, inspector name and certification, pass/fail outcome, defects identified, corrective actions required, next inspection due date. For certificates: captures certificate type (crane load test certificate, pressure vessel certificate, lifting gear certificate, OSHA compliance certificate, insurance certificate), certificate number, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, and document storage reference. SSOT for all equipment inspection events AND asset-level compliance certificates. Supports OSHA compliance, insurance requirements, equipment fitness-for-use certification, pre-mobilization compliance checks, and regulatory audit trails. Distinct from quality ITP inspections (owned by quality domain) and operator certifications (which track people, not assets).'
}

Table equipment.rental_agreement {
  rental_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment rental agreement. Primary key for the rental agreement record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Rental agreements are a type of contract; linking enables compliance, billing, and performance reporting against the governing agreement.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Rental agreements require approval by a procurement officer; linking to employee enables approval audit and compliance reporting.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment unit being rented. Links to the equipment master record in the equipment domain.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this rental equipment is assigned. Links to the project master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rental agreement expenses are allocated to a cost center for cost tracking and billing.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant hire company or equipment supplier providing the rental equipment. Links to the supplier master record.']
  actual_demobilization_date date [note: 'Actual date when the equipment was demobilized and removed from the project site. Used for final billing and cost reconciliation.']
  actual_mobilization_date date [note: 'Actual date when the equipment was delivered and mobilized to the project site. May differ from planned rental start date.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the rental agreement was formally approved and authorized for execution.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which rental charges are invoiced by the supplier (daily, weekly, monthly, or milestone-based).']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or digital contract document stored in the document management system (e.g., Aconex document ID).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rental agreement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rental agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daily_hire_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed daily rental rate for the equipment in the contract currency. Used when billing is on a daily basis.']
  damage_waiver_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Optional damage waiver fee paid to limit contractor liability for equipment damage during the rental period.']
  demobilization_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time charge for dismantling and removing the equipment from the project site. Invoiced at rental completion.']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rented equipment including make, model, capacity, and specifications (e.g., \'Liebherr LTM 1200-5.1 Mobile Crane, 200-ton capacity\').']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rented equipment by type. Used for fleet management and cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crane|excavator|bulldozer|loader|concrete_pump|generator|compressor|scaffolding|formwork|tower_crane|mobile_crane — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fuel_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fuel costs are included in the hire rate (True) or billed separately (False).']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance certificate covering the rented equipment. Used for compliance verification.']
  insurance_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of insurance coverage requirements for the rented equipment, including liability limits and coverage types.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rental agreement record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  late_return_penalty_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily penalty rate charged by the supplier if equipment is returned after the agreed rental end date.']
  maintenance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for routine maintenance and repairs during the rental period (supplier, contractor, or shared).']
  minimum_hire_period_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the equipment must be hired as stipulated in the rental agreement. Used for cost commitment calculation.']
  mobilization_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time charge for transporting and setting up the equipment at the project site. Invoiced at rental commencement.']
  monthly_hire_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed monthly rental rate for the equipment in the contract currency. Used when billing is on a monthly basis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or remarks related to the rental agreement.']
  operator_supplied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rental supplier provides a qualified operator with the equipment (True) or if the contractor provides their own operator (False).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the supplier (e.g., \'Net 30 days\', \'Net 45 days\', \'2/10 Net 30\'). Governs invoice payment schedule.']
  rental_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the rental agreement, typically used in contracts and invoices. Format: RA-NNNNNN.']
  rental_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the rental period ends and equipment is demobilized. Nullable for open-ended rentals.']
  rental_po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number issued to the supplier for this rental agreement. Links the rental agreement to the procurement transaction.']
  rental_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rental period begins and hire charges commence. Typically aligned with equipment mobilization date.']
  rental_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rental agreement. Tracks the agreement from draft through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|active|on_hire|demobilized|completed|cancelled|disputed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Refundable security deposit held by the supplier to cover potential damage or late return penalties.']
  site_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or site address where the rented equipment will be deployed. Used for logistics and mobilization planning.']
  total_committed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total committed cost for the rental agreement including minimum hire period charges, mobilization, and demobilization. Used for budget commitment and accrual.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'WBS element code for detailed project cost allocation and tracking. Links rental costs to specific work packages.']
  weekly_hire_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed weekly rental rate for the equipment in the contract currency. Used when billing is on a weekly basis.']

  Note: 'Master record governing the rental or hire of external equipment from a plant hire company or equipment supplier. Captures rental supplier, equipment description and type, rental start and end dates, agreed daily/weekly/monthly hire rate, minimum hire period, mobilization and demobilization charges, insurance requirements, operator-supplied flag, rental PO reference, and total committed rental cost. Distinct from procurement POs (owned by procurement domain) — this is the equipment-domain SSOT for rental fleet management and cost accrual.'
}

Table equipment.equipment_mobilization {
  equipment_mobilization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment mobilization or demobilization event.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Mobilization costs are billed to equipment mobilization contracts; the FK ties each mobilization event to its contract agreement.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset being mobilized or demobilized.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project associated with this mobilization event.']
  dispatch_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Logistics dispatch is performed by a specific employee; tracking supports operational reporting and liability management.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor or vendor responsible for transporting the equipment.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date when the equipment arrived at the destination location.']
  actual_dispatch_date date [note: 'Actual date when the equipment departed from the origin location.']
  actual_transit_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual duration in hours that the equipment was in transit from origin to destination.']
  cost_allocation_code varchar [note: 'Cost center or Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) code to which the mobilization cost is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the mobilization record was first created in the system.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the location to which the equipment is being mobilized (project site, yard, storage facility).']
  destination_site_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the destination site or facility.']
  dispatch_condition varchar [note: 'Assessed physical and operational condition of the equipment at the time of dispatch from the origin location.']
  dispatch_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes or observations regarding the equipment condition at dispatch, including any pre-existing damage or defects.']
  dispatch_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch sign-off was completed.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance in kilometers between the origin and destination locations.']
  estimated_transit_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated duration in hours for the equipment to be transported from origin to destination.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment movement event: mobilization (to site), demobilization (from site), inter-site transfer, or relocation.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is covered by insurance during the mobilization transport.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the insurance coverage applicable to this mobilization event.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the mobilization record was last updated in the system.']
  mobilization_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the mobilization event, typically formatted as MOB-YYYYNNNN.']
  mobilization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mobilization event in the workflow.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the location from which the equipment is being mobilized (yard, warehouse, previous site).']
  origin_site_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the origin site or facility.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number(s) issued by the governing authority for this transport.']
  permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether special permits (oversize load, road permits, environmental permits) are required for this mobilization.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Type of permit(s) required for the transport, such as oversize load permit, road use permit, or environmental permit. Multiple permits may be listed.']
  planned_arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the equipment is expected to arrive at the destination location.']
  planned_dispatch_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the equipment is planned to depart from the origin location.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason for the mobilization event, such as project start, equipment replacement, or maintenance requirement.']
  receipt_condition varchar [note: 'Assessed physical and operational condition of the equipment upon receipt at the destination location.']
  receipt_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes or observations regarding the equipment condition at receipt, including any damage incurred during transport.']
  receipt_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the receipt sign-off was completed.']
  receipt_signed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the site manager or authorized personnel who signed off on the equipment receipt at the destination.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional comments, notes, or special instructions related to the mobilization event.']
  transport_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for transporting the equipment, including freight, permits, and handling charges.']
  transport_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transport cost.']
  transport_method varchar [note: 'Method or mode of transportation used to move the equipment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the mobilization or demobilization event of a piece of equipment to or from a project site. Tracks event type (mobilization, demobilization, inter-site transfer), planned and actual transport dates, origin and destination locations, transport contractor, transport cost, permits required (oversize load, road permits), equipment condition at dispatch and receipt, and sign-off by site manager. Supports site logistics planning, cost allocation, and equipment tracking across the fleet.'
}

Table equipment.fuel_transaction {
  fuel_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel transaction record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Fuel supply is frequently contracted; associating each transaction with the governing agreement enables expense allocation and audit.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or fleet vehicle that received fuel. Links to the equipment master data.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which this fuel transaction is allocated for cost tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment operator who confirmed or authorized the fuel transaction. Links to workforce or employee master data.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Fuel consumption is charged to a cost code for cost allocation and EVM reporting.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Fuel transactions for equipment used by Subcontractor are billed to that Subcontractor, needed for cost recovery and fuel consumption reporting.']
  fuel_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel point, fuel station, or bowser from which the fuel was issued. May be an on-site fuel depot or external vendor station.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Links fuel transactions to the material master for standardized fuel type, pricing, and regulatory reporting.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or location where the equipment was operating at the time of fueling.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external fuel vendor or supplier if fuel was purchased from a third-party station. Null for internal fuel depots.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element for detailed project cost allocation and tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the fuel transaction. Indicates whether the transaction has been reviewed and approved by a supervisor or cost controller.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel transaction was approved. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the fuel transaction. Null if auto-approved or pending approval.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions in kilograms resulting from the fuel consumed. Calculated based on fuel type and quantity for ISO 14001 environmental reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel transaction record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fuel_card_number varchar [note: 'Fuel card or fleet card number used for the transaction. Masked or tokenized for security. Used for reconciliation with vendor invoices.']
  fuel_point_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the fuel point or bowser where the fuel was issued.']
  hour_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Hour meter reading of the equipment at the time of fueling. Used to calculate fuel consumption per operating hour and detect anomalies.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number associated with the fuel transaction. Used for accounts payable reconciliation.']
  is_emergency_refuel boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this was an emergency or unplanned refueling event. True indicates emergency; False indicates scheduled refueling.']
  is_theft_suspected boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether fuel theft or wastage is suspected based on consumption patterns or anomaly detection. True indicates suspected theft; False indicates normal transaction.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel transaction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  odometer_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer reading of the fleet vehicle at the time of fueling. Used for mileage-based fuel consumption analysis. Applicable to vehicles with odometers.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the equipment operator who received the fuel on behalf of the equipment.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number if the fuel transaction was part of a formal procurement process. Used for contract compliance and spend tracking.']
  quantity_issued decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of fuel issued to the equipment in the specified unit of measure. Typically measured in litres or gallons.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the fuel transaction record originated (e.g., HCSS HeavyJob, SAP PM, Viewpoint Vista, fuel card provider system).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fuel transaction in the source system. Used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  tank_capacity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the equipment fuel tank capacity filled during this transaction. Used to detect partial fills and optimize refueling schedules.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the fuel transaction calculated as quantity issued multiplied by unit cost. Expressed in the transaction currency.']
  transaction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the fuel transaction. May include reasons for emergency refueling, discrepancies, or special circumstances.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number or receipt number issued at the time of fueling. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fuel transaction in its lifecycle. Completed indicates finalized and posted; pending indicates awaiting approval; cancelled indicates voided; disputed indicates under review.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel was issued to the equipment. Represents the actual business event time of fueling.']
  unit_cost decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of fuel at the time of transaction. Expressed in the transaction currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the fuel quantity issued. Common units include litres, gallons, or kWh for electric charging.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of fuel issued to a piece of equipment or fleet vehicle. Captures fuel type (diesel, petrol, LPG), quantity issued (litres), unit cost, total cost, issuing fuel point or bowser, date and time of issue, hour meter or odometer reading at fueling, project and cost code allocation, and operator confirmation. Enables fuel consumption tracking, cost-per-hour analysis, carbon emissions reporting (ISO 14001), and detection of fuel wastage or theft.'
}

Table equipment.operator_certification {
  operator_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operator certification record. Primary key for the operator certification entity.']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: Operator certification is tied to an equipment category; replace free‑text category with FK to asset_category to enforce consistency.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator in the workforce domain who holds this certification. Links to the workforce master data to identify the individual operator.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital document or scanned copy of the physical certification document stored in the document management system. Links to Aconex or BIM 360 document repository.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the operator completed the certification assessment or examination. Represents the formal evaluation event.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified assessor or examiner who conducted the certification assessment and validated the operators competency.']
  capacity_rating varchar [note: 'The maximum capacity or tonnage rating of equipment the operator is certified to operate, such as cranes up to 100 tons or forklifts up to 5 tons. Defines operational limits for equipment assignment.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or license number issued by the certifying body. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification document.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'The proficiency or skill level represented by the certification, such as basic, intermediate, advanced, or master operator. Indicates the complexity of equipment and operations the operator is qualified to perform. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: basic|intermediate|advanced|master|trainee|journeyman|supervisor — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification. Indicates whether the certification is valid and active, expired, suspended, revoked, or pending renewal. Critical for HSE pre-start checks and operator assignment validation.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The type or nature of the certification, such as operator license, competency card, safety certification, or manufacturer-specific certification.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, authority, or institution that issued the certification. Examples include NCCCO, OSHA-approved training providers, manufacturer training centers, or government licensing authorities.']
  certifying_body_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation or registration number of the certifying body, validating that the issuing organization is authorized to provide certifications.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred to obtain or renew this certification, including training fees, examination fees, and administrative charges. Used for workforce development cost tracking.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount. Enables multi-currency cost tracking for international operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certification record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  endorsements varchar [note: 'Additional endorsements, specializations, or restrictions associated with the certification, such as night operations, confined spaces, or specific attachment types. Comma-separated list of endorsement codes or descriptions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Operators must renew or recertify before this date to maintain valid certification status.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was originally issued to the operator. Represents the start of the certification validity period.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country in which the certification was issued. Important for cross-border projects and international operator mobility.']
  issuing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional jurisdiction within the issuing country where the certification was granted. Relevant for jurisdictions with state-level licensing requirements.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the certification was renewed or recertified. Tracks the certification maintenance history.']
  medical_clearance_date date [note: 'The date on which the operator received medical clearance or passed the required health examination for this certification. Nullable if medical clearance is not required.']
  medical_clearance_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical clearance expires and must be renewed. Nullable if medical clearance is not required or does not expire.']
  medical_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical examination or health clearance is required as part of the certification or renewal process. True if medical clearance is mandatory, false otherwise.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this certification record. Provides audit trail for data changes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certification record was last modified in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  next_renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the operator must complete renewal requirements to maintain continuous certification. Used for proactive renewal planning and compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the certification. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields.']
  practical_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score or grade achieved by the operator in the practical hands-on assessment component of the certification. Typically expressed as a percentage or numeric score.']
  reciprocity_agreements varchar [note: 'List of countries, states, or jurisdictions where this certification is recognized under reciprocity or mutual recognition agreements. Enables cross-jurisdictional operator deployment.']
  renewal_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of the requirements the operator must fulfill to renew the certification, such as refresher training hours, re-assessment, continuing education credits, or medical examination.']
  restrictions varchar [note: 'Any limitations or restrictions placed on the certification, such as daylight operations only, supervised operation required, or specific site conditions. Defines operational boundaries for the operator.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this certification record originated, such as SAP SuccessFactors, Intelex, or HCSS HeavyJob. Enables data lineage tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this certification record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with source systems.']
  sponsor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that sponsored or paid for the operators certification training and examination. May be the construction company, a subcontractor, or the operator themselves.']
  theory_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score or grade achieved by the operator in the theoretical or written examination component of the certification. Typically expressed as a percentage or numeric score.']
  training_hours_completed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of training hours completed by the operator to achieve this certification. Demonstrates the depth of training and preparation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was last verified with the certifying body or through an independent verification process.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the certification verification process, indicating whether the certification has been independently verified with the issuing body. Critical for compliance and audit purposes.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, system, or organization that performed the certification verification. Provides audit trail for verification activities.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this certification record in the system. Provides audit trail for data entry.']

  Note: 'Master record of an equipment operator\'s certification, licence, or competency qualification for a specific equipment type or class. Captures operator reference (linked to workforce domain), equipment category certified for (e.g., tower crane, mobile crane, excavator, forklift), certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, certification status (valid, expired, suspended), and renewal requirements. Supports OSHA compliance, HSE pre-start checks, and operator-to-equipment assignment validation. Owned by equipment domain as it governs equipment operation rights.'
}

Table equipment.telematics_reading {
  telematics_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the telematics reading record. Primary key for this IoT/telemetry transactional event.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the heavy equipment or fleet vehicle that generated this telematics reading. Links to the equipment master data asset.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this equipment is currently assigned. Derived from fleet assignment or geofence mapping. Enables project-level utilization and cost allocation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator assigned to this equipment at the time of reading, if operator identification is captured via RFID, keypad, or mobile app login. Enables operator performance tracking and accountability.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction site where this equipment is currently located. Derived from GPS coordinates and geofence mapping. Enables site-level fleet visibility.']
  battery_voltage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current battery voltage of the equipment. Low voltage may indicate battery health issues or electrical system faults.']
  coolant_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engine coolant temperature in degrees Celsius. High temperatures may indicate overheating or cooling system faults.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or OEM telematics platform that provided this reading (e.g., Caterpillar Product Link, Komatsu KOMTRAX, John Deere JDLink, AEMP API, IoT Platform). Enables multi-vendor telematics integration and data lineage tracking.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation above sea level in meters at the time of reading. Useful for terrain analysis and site operations in mountainous or variable-elevation projects.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative engine operating hours recorded by the equipment at the time of this reading. Critical metric for utilization tracking, maintenance scheduling, and asset lifecycle management.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine revolutions per minute at the time of reading. Indicates operational intensity and can signal abnormal operating conditions or maintenance needs.']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) or J1939 fault code reported by the equipment at the time of reading. Empty if no fault detected. Enables predictive maintenance and early fault detection.']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the fault code, if available from the OEM telematics system. Assists maintenance teams in diagnosing issues.']
  fault_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the reported fault code. Critical faults may trigger immediate maintenance alerts or equipment shutdown protocols.']
  fuel_consumed_liters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative fuel consumed in liters since the last reset or equipment initialization. Used for cost tracking and environmental reporting.']
  fuel_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current fuel tank level as a percentage of total capacity. Enables fuel consumption monitoring, refueling planning, and theft detection.']
  geofence_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the geofence zone that the equipment is currently within, if applicable. Null if equipment is outside all defined geofences. Enables site boundary compliance and theft detection.']
  geofence_status varchar [note: 'Status of the equipment relative to the assigned geofence at the time of reading. Triggers alerts for unauthorized movement or equipment theft.']
  gps_accuracy_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters. Lower values indicate higher precision. Used to assess reliability of location data for geofence and tracking analytics.']
  hydraulic_pressure_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hydraulic system pressure in bar. Applicable to equipment with hydraulic systems (excavators, cranes, concrete pumps). Abnormal pressure may indicate system faults.']
  idle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative idle time in minutes (engine running but equipment not performing work). Key metric for utilization optimization and fuel efficiency analysis.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this telematics reading was ingested into the lakehouse platform. Used for data pipeline monitoring and latency analysis.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the equipment at the time of reading, in decimal degrees. Enables real-time fleet tracking and geofence compliance monitoring.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the equipment at the time of reading, in decimal degrees. Enables real-time fleet tracking and geofence compliance monitoring.']
  odometer_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled in kilometers. Applicable to mobile equipment and fleet vehicles. Used for maintenance scheduling and asset utilization tracking.']
  operational_state varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment at the time of reading. Derived from engine status, movement, and sensor data. Enables real-time fleet status dashboards.']
  payload_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current payload weight in kilograms, if the equipment is equipped with onboard weighing systems (e.g., haul trucks, loaders). Used for production tracking and overload prevention.']
  reading_quality varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for this telematics reading. Suspect or invalid readings may result from sensor faults, connectivity issues, or data transmission errors. Used for data quality filtering in analytics.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the telematics reading was captured by the onboard device. Represents the real-world event time of the measurement, distinct from transmission or ingestion timestamps.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this reading in the source telematics system or IoT platform. Used for data reconciliation and deduplication.']
  speed_kmh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Current speed of the equipment in kilometers per hour at the time of reading. Used for safety monitoring, route optimization, and geofence compliance.']
  telematics_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the onboard telematics unit or IoT device that transmitted this reading (e.g., Caterpillar Product Link, Komatsu KOMTRAX, John Deere JDLink device serial number).']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the telematics reading was transmitted from the device to the IoT platform or AEMP API gateway. May differ from reading_timestamp due to connectivity delays.']

  Note: 'IoT/telemetry transactional record capturing real-time or near-real-time data transmitted from GPS and telematics devices fitted to heavy equipment and fleet vehicles, aligned with ISO 15143-3 (AEMP telematics standard) and OEM-agnostic integration patterns. Captures timestamp, GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude, elevation), engine hours, engine RPM, fuel level, idle time, fault codes (DTC/J1939), speed, geofence status, and asset operational state. Sourced from onboard telematics units (e.g., Caterpillar Product Link, Komatsu KOMTRAX, John Deere JDLink) integrated via IoT platform or AEMP API. Enables real-time fleet tracking, utilization monitoring, predictive maintenance triggers, geofence compliance, and theft detection.'
}

Table equipment.asset_valuation {
  asset_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset valuation record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset being valued. Links to the equipment master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for posting asset valuation depreciation to the correct cost center in financial statements.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to allocate asset valuation entries to the appropriate GL account for depreciation expense tracking.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized from acquisition date through the valuation date. Represents the cumulative reduction in asset value due to wear, tear, and obsolescence.']
  accumulated_operating_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total operating hours logged by the equipment from acquisition through the valuation date. Used for units-of-production depreciation and lifecycle analysis.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or capitalized cost of the equipment asset at the time of acquisition, including delivery, installation, and commissioning costs.']
  appraisal_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the external appraisal report document. Used to trace valuation back to supporting documentation.']
  appraiser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the professional appraiser or valuation firm that conducted the most recent independent valuation. Null if no external appraisal has been performed.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Financial accounting classification of the asset for reporting and tax purposes (e.g., heavy equipment, vehicles, tools, generators). Aligns with chart of accounts and fixed asset subledger structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this valuation record was first created in the system.']
  current_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net book value of the asset as of the valuation date, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. This is the carrying amount on the balance sheet.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense. Straight-line allocates cost evenly over useful life, reducing balance applies a fixed percentage to declining book value, units of production bases depreciation on actual usage hours.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage, applicable for reducing balance and other rate-based depreciation methods. Null for straight-line method.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Null if the asset is still in service.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the asset was disposed. Sale indicates sold to third party, trade-in exchanged for new equipment, scrap sold for parts/metal value, donation given to charity, write-off removed with no proceeds.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal. Null if asset has not been disposed.']
  fair_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated market value of the asset if sold in an arms-length transaction. Used for insurance, disposal decisions, and rent-vs-buy analysis. May differ from book value.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the asset shows signs of impairment (carrying amount exceeds recoverable amount). True triggers impairment testing per accounting standards.']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount by which the carrying amount of the asset exceeds its recoverable amount. Recognized as an expense when impairment is identified. Null if no impairment.']
  insurance_replacement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit at current market prices. Used for insurance coverage determination and renewal valuations.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this valuation record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this valuation record was last modified.']
  next_revaluation_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic revaluation or impairment review. Used for fleet management planning and compliance with revaluation policies.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of asset ownership. Owned assets are capitalized and depreciated, leased assets may be capitalized under IFRS 16, rented assets are expensed and not valued here.']
  remaining_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated remaining economic life of the asset in years from the valuation date. Used for fleet replacement planning and rent-vs-buy decisions.']
  residual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated salvage or scrap value of the asset at the end of its useful life. The amount expected to be recovered through sale or disposal after full depreciation.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The adjustment amount applied during the most recent revaluation. Positive values indicate upward revaluation, negative values indicate impairment. Null if no revaluation has occurred.']
  revaluation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revaluation event, if the asset has been revalued to fair market value. Null if no revaluation has occurred since acquisition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this valuation record originated (e.g., SAP FI-AA, Viewpoint Vista, HCSS HeavyJob).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this valuation record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tax_depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method used for tax reporting purposes, which may differ from book depreciation. MACRS (Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System) is common in the US, Section 179 allows immediate expensing.']
  tax_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Useful life in years for tax depreciation purposes, as defined by tax regulations. May differ from book useful life.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected economic life of the asset in years, used to calculate depreciation. Determined based on manufacturer specifications, industry standards, and organizational experience.']
  valuation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this valuation record (acquisition cost, book value, depreciation, etc.).']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The effective date of this valuation snapshot. Represents the point in time when this valuation is applicable.']
  valuation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or special considerations affecting this valuation (e.g., major overhaul capitalized, damage affecting value, market conditions).']
  valuation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the valuation record, typically system-generated or following organizational numbering convention.']
  valuation_source varchar [note: 'The source or method used to determine the current valuation. Acquisition uses original cost, appraisal uses third-party professional valuation, market index uses industry pricing data, insurance quote from carrier, internal estimate by equipment managers.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the valuation record. Draft indicates preliminary valuation, active is current book value, superseded indicates replaced by newer valuation, archived for historical records.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this valuation record.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking the financial valuation and depreciation profile of owned equipment assets over their lifecycle from an equipment management perspective. Captures acquisition cost, current book value, accumulated depreciation, depreciation method (straight-line, reducing balance), useful life (years), residual value, last revaluation date, revaluation amount, insurance replacement value, and disposal proceeds (if disposed). Serves equipment-domain decisions: fleet replacement planning, rent-vs-buy analysis, insurance renewal valuations, and asset disposal timing. Finance domain owns the authoritative GL entries and fixed asset subledger (SAP FI-AA) — this product provides the equipment managers operational view of asset value for fleet management decisions.'
}

Table equipment.insurance_policy {
  insurance_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance policy record. Primary key for the insurance policy entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Equipment insurance policies are procured under contracts; linking allows compliance monitoring and claim tracking per agreement.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Insurance policy is issued for a specific asset; linking policy to asset captures ownership and removes need for policy number fields on asset.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'The name of the insurance broker or intermediary who arranged the policy on behalf of the construction company. Nullable if policy was purchased directly.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'The date when the Certificate of Insurance was issued. Nullable if certificate has not been issued. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  certificate_of_insurance_issued boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a Certificate of Insurance has been issued for this policy. COIs are required for equipment mobilization and regulatory compliance.']
  claims_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address for submitting claims documentation and correspondence with the claims adjuster.']
  claims_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the claims adjuster or contact person at the insurer for reporting and processing claims under this policy.']
  claims_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number to reach the claims contact for reporting incidents and tracking claim status.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting specific regulatory requirements met by this policy, any compliance gaps, or special conditions required by clients or governing bodies.']
  coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary amount the insurer will pay for covered losses under this policy. This is the policy limit or sum insured.']
  coverage_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the coverage amount and premium (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what assets, risks, and perils are covered under this policy. Includes covered equipment types, geographic scope, and specific inclusions or exclusions.']
  covered_asset_category varchar [note: 'The category of construction equipment and assets covered by this policy. Heavy equipment includes cranes and excavators, light equipment includes smaller machinery, fleet vehicles includes trucks and cars, tools includes hand and power tools, generators includes power generation equipment, and all equipment covers comprehensive asset types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance policy record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount the policyholder must pay out-of-pocket before the insurer pays a claim. This is the self-insured retention or excess.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'The geographic regions or territories where the insurance coverage is valid. May specify countries, states, or project sites where equipment is covered.']
  insurer_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the insurer contact for policy correspondence, claims submission, and documentation exchange.']
  insurer_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contact person or account manager at the insurance company for policy administration and claims.']
  insurer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The primary phone number to reach the insurer contact for policy inquiries, claims reporting, and emergency notifications.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the insurance company or underwriter providing the coverage. This is the carrier responsible for claims payment.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this insurance policy record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance policy record was last modified in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ownership_classification varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this policy covers only company-owned equipment, only rented equipment, or both owned and rented assets.']
  policy_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the full policy document, terms and conditions, and endorsements stored in the document management system.']
  policy_end_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance policy coverage expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended policies. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes about the policy including special terms, exclusions, endorsements, or internal administrative information.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique policy number issued by the insurer. This is the business identifier used in all correspondence and claims.']
  policy_start_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance policy coverage becomes effective and binding. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the insurance policy. Active policies provide coverage, pending policies are awaiting approval, expired policies have passed their end date, cancelled policies were terminated early, suspended policies are temporarily inactive, and renewed policies have been extended.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurance policy coverage type. All-risk plant covers comprehensive equipment damage, third-party liability covers legal claims, fleet motor covers vehicle insurance, marine transit covers equipment in transport, builders risk covers construction projects, and equipment breakdown covers mechanical failure.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total premium cost paid to the insurer for this policy coverage period. This is the price of the insurance.']
  premium_payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which premium payments are made to the insurer (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, or one-time lump sum).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this policy meets all regulatory and contractual insurance requirements for equipment operation and mobilization.']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'The date by which the policyholder must notify the insurer of intent to renew or cancel. Critical for mobilization planning and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy will automatically renew at expiration, requires manual renewal action, is non-renewable, or is currently under renewal review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this insurance policy record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista, HCSS HeavyJob).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this insurance policy record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who created this insurance policy record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record of insurance policies covering owned and rented construction equipment and fleet vehicles. Captures insurer name, policy number, policy type (all-risk plant, third-party liability, fleet motor, marine transit), coverage amount, premium amount, policy start and end dates, deductible amount, covered asset scope, and renewal status. Supports risk management, mobilization pre-checks, and regulatory compliance. Owned by equipment domain as it directly governs asset coverage — distinct from corporate insurance managed by finance.'
}

Table equipment.asset_activity_assignment {
  asset_activity_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the AssetActivityAssignment association']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the schedule activity']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the equipment asset']
  actual_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of the asset actually used in the activity']
  finish_date date [note: 'Planned finish date of the asset assignment for the activity']
  planned_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of the asset planned for the activity']
  resource_role varchar [note: 'Role of the asset in the activity (e.g., primary, support, auxiliary)']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the asset assignment for the activity']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of construction equipment assets to schedule activities. Each record links one asset to one activity and stores assignment‑specific data such as quantities, dates, and the role of the asset in the activity.. Existence Justification: Construction projects assign multiple equipment assets to a single schedule activity, and each asset can be scheduled for multiple activities over its lifecycle. The assignment is actively managed by planners and includes quantities, dates, and role information, making the relationship a distinct business entity.'
}

Table equipment.fuel_point {
  fuel_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fuel_point']
  parent_fuel_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fuel_point (parent_fuel_point_id)']
  address varchar [note: 'Street address of the fuel point installation.']
  capacity_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum fuel volume the point can hold, expressed in liters.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the fuel point is located.']
  code varchar [note: 'External code or tag used to reference the fuel point in operational systems.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the fuel point location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fuel point record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the fuel price.']
  current_level_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current amount of fuel present at the point, in liters.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the fuel point, its purpose, or special notes.']
  fuel_price_per_liter decimal(8,3) [note: 'Current price of fuel at this point, expressed per liter.']
  fuel_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Internal rating of fuel quality at this point.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the fuel point was installed and became operational.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary fuel point for its site or fleet.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the fuel point (decimal degrees).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the physical location where the fuel point is installed.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the fuel point (decimal degrees).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fuel point location or asset.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current real‑time operational condition of the fuel point.']
  safety_certification_date date [note: 'Date the fuel point received its most recent safety certification.']
  safety_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the current safety certification.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the fuel point location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fuel point.']
  supplier_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone or email) for the fuel supplier.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier providing fuel to this point.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of fuel dispensed or stored at the point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fuel point record.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal (ZIP) code for the fuel point address.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fuel_point. Referenced by fuel_point_id.'
}

Table equipment.functional_location {
  functional_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for functional_location']
  parent_functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on functional_location (parent_functional_location_id)']
  parent_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent location in the hierarchy, if any.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  area_sq_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical footprint of the location in square meters.']
  asset_count varchar [note: 'Number of equipment assets assigned to this location.']
  capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum load capacity the location can support, expressed in metric tons.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the location is situated.']
  code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique alphanumeric code used to reference the location in SAP PM and HCSS HeavyJob.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the location was officially commissioned for use.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the location resides.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the functional location record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the location was retired or taken out of service, if applicable.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the location.']
  environmental_zone varchar [note: 'Designation of the environmental zone (e.g., hazardous, clean, temperature‑controlled).']
  gps_accuracy_m double [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the location was first installed or made operational.']
  is_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is owned by the enterprise (true) or not (false).']
  is_rental boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is leased/rented (true) or owned (false).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the location (e.g., Main Plant, Building A).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the location.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department accountable for the location\'s operation and maintenance.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the location based on internal risk assessments.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the location within the equipment hierarchy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the functional location record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for functional_location. Referenced by functional_location_id.'
}

Table workforce.craft_worker {
  craft_worker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the craft worker record. Primary key for the craft worker entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Payroll & compliance reports require each worker to be linked to the employing client account for tax, insurance and HSE reporting.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Each permit designates a responsible worker (permit holder) to ensure compliance; linking enables accountability audits.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary construction project to which the craft worker is currently assigned as their home base.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Payroll & benefits integration: linking each craft worker to their HR employee record ensures accurate salary, tax, and benefits processing.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Employer assignment: linking each craft worker to the subcontractor firm that employs them, required for labor cost allocation and compliance reporting.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_party. Business justification: REQUIRED: Workers are employed by a contract party (contractor/sub‑contractor); linking supports payroll, insurance, and compliance reporting.']
  skill_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Standardize trade information by referencing skill_trade; remove redundant primary_trade_code and primary_trade_name from craft_worker.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: REQUIRED: Vendor‑Supplied Labor Tracking – each subcontractor worker must be linked to the vendor that supplies them for compliance and cost allocation.']
  craft_code varchar [note: 'Standardized craft classification code used for cost coding and labor reporting in project management systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the craft worker record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the worker\'s compensation (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  demobilization_date date [note: 'Date the craft worker was demobilized from their project assignment, if applicable.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the craft worker used for communication and system access.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the craft worker\'s designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the craft worker\'s designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the craft worker (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend).']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker\'s employment relationship with the organization: direct hire (permanent employee), agency (temporary staffing), union referral (union hall dispatch), or subcontractor (independent contractor).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the craft worker as recorded in HR systems.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the craft worker was hired or first engaged by the organization.']
  hourly_base_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard hourly wage rate for the craft worker in their primary trade classification, excluding overtime premiums and benefits.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the craft worker as recorded in HR systems.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the craft worker, if applicable.']
  mobilization_date date [note: 'Date the craft worker was mobilized to their current project assignment.']
  mobilization_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment status of the craft worker: mobilized (actively deployed to a project site), demobilized (released from project), on_leave (temporarily unavailable), or available (ready for assignment).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the craft worker record was last modified in the system.']
  osha_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the craft worker\'s OSHA safety certification, if applicable.']
  osha_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the craft worker holds valid OSHA safety training certification (e.g., OSHA 10-hour or 30-hour).']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base hourly rate for overtime hours (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double-time).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the craft worker for emergency contact and field coordination.']
  ppe_size_boots varchar [note: 'Boot size for issuing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as steel-toed safety boots.']
  ppe_size_pants varchar [note: 'Pants size for issuing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as work pants and coveralls.']
  ppe_size_shirt varchar [note: 'Shirt size for issuing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as high-visibility vests and work shirts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: XS|S|M|L|XL|XXL|XXXL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  secondary_trade_code varchar [note: 'Secondary craft trade classification code for multi-skilled workers who hold certifications in more than one trade.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Security clearance level held by the craft worker for access to restricted project sites (e.g., government, defense, critical infrastructure projects).']
  site_access_badge_number varchar [note: 'Unique badge or access card number issued to the craft worker for site entry and time tracking at construction sites.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Proficiency level of the craft worker in their primary trade: apprentice (in training), journeyman (certified tradesperson), master (advanced certification), or foreman (supervisory level).']
  supervisory_role_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the craft worker currently holds a supervisory role such as working foreman or crew lead (True) or is a non-supervisory hourly worker (False).']
  supervisory_title varchar [note: 'Job title for supervisory role if applicable (e.g., Working Foreman, Crew Lead, General Foreman).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the craft worker\'s employment or engagement was terminated, if applicable.']
  union_affiliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the craft worker is affiliated with a labor union (True) or non-union (False).']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'Local union chapter number the worker belongs to, if union-affiliated.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union the worker is affiliated with, if applicable (e.g., International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers, United Association of Plumbers).']
  worker_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the craft worker record: active (currently employed and available), inactive (temporarily not working), suspended (disciplinary or safety hold), or terminated (employment ended).']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years of experience the craft worker has in their primary trade.']

  Note: 'Master record for all craft labor personnel deployed on construction projects including hourly trade workers and working foremen/supervisors. Captures worker identity, trade classification (from skill_trade taxonomy), employment type (direct hire, agency, union referral), union affiliation and local, craft code, supervisory role flag, home project assignment, mobilization status, security clearance level, and field deployment history. SSOT for individual field worker identity in the workforce domain. Workers may hold multiple trade classifications and rotate between craft and supervisory roles across projects. Integrates with SAP SuccessFactors for HCM master data and HCSS HeavyJob for field time tracking.'
}

Table workforce.crew {
  crew_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the construction crew. Primary key for the crew entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Crew budgeting and resource allocation dashboards aggregate crew costs per client account, needing a direct account FK.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: REQUIRED: Crew allocation is planned per contract agreement; linking allows crew costs and productivity to be attributed to the agreement.']
  assigned_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Needed for Crew Equipment Allocation report, assigning primary equipment to a crew for cost allocation, scheduling, and safety compliance.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Hot‑work permits assign a specific crew; tracking crew per permit is required for safety compliance reports.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this crew is currently assigned. A crew may be reassigned between projects over its lifecycle.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for crew cost allocation reports that roll crew labor costs to the project\'s cost center, enabling accurate budgeting and variance analysis per cost center.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home cost code for this crew. Used for labor cost allocation and job costing when crew time is not charged to a specific activity cost code.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker record of the foreman or crew lead responsible for supervising this crew. The foreman is accountable for crew productivity, safety, and quality.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Crew ownership: crews are supplied by subcontractors; linking enables crew billing and performance tracking per subcontractor.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Crew safety oversight: crew supervisor (an employee) is responsible for safety briefings and incident reporting for the crew.']
  average_hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Blended average hourly labor rate for the crew, calculated across all crew members. Used for cost estimation and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified in project configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crew_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the crew used in field operations and timekeeping systems. Typically alphanumeric and unique within a project or division.']
  crew_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the crew, often reflecting the crew type, foreman name, or project assignment (e.g., \'Smith Concrete Gang\', \'MEP Crew A\').']
  crew_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the crew in its lifecycle. Active crews are deployed and working; inactive crews are not currently assigned; mobilizing/demobilizing crews are in transition; standby crews are available but not deployed; disbanded crews are permanently closed.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the crew based on trade or discipline. Defines the primary craft or work scope the crew is organized to perform. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|concrete|mep|electrical|plumbing|hvac|finishing|earthworks|piling — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  days_since_last_incident varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days since the last recordable safety incident for this crew. Used for safety milestone recognition and Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR) tracking. Null if no incidents have occurred.']
  demobilization_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the crew will be or was demobilized from the current project. Null for active crews with no planned demobilization.']
  home_location varchar [note: 'Primary site, yard, or facility where this crew is based or reports to. May be a project site, equipment yard, or regional office.']
  is_union_crew boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a union crew subject to collective bargaining agreements and union work rules. True if union, false if non-union or open shop.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  last_safety_incident_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recordable safety incident involving this crew. Used for safety performance tracking and Lost Time Injury (LTI) frequency calculations. Null if no incidents have occurred.']
  mobilization_date date [note: 'Date when the crew was mobilized and became operational on the current project or assignment. Used for crew lifecycle tracking and project staffing history.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this crew record. Used for audit trail and accountability in crew management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information about the crew, such as special skills, project-specific arrangements, or operational constraints. Used by project management and field supervision.']
  planned_crew_size varchar [note: 'Target or planned number of workers for this crew based on project staffing plans and Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) resource requirements.']
  productivity_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard or historical productivity rate for this crew, expressed in units per hour (e.g., cubic yards of concrete per hour, linear feet of pipe per hour). Unit of measure depends on crew type and work scope.']
  productivity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the productivity rate (e.g., CY/HR for cubic yards per hour, LF/HR for linear feet per hour, EA/HR for each per hour). Aligns with Bill of Quantities (BOQ) units.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Current quality performance rating for the crew based on Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) inspections, Non-Conformance Reports (NCR), and rework rates. Used for crew selection and quality incentive programs.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of skill certifications or licenses required for crew members (e.g., \'OSHA 30, Confined Space, Rigging Level II\'). Used for crew qualification verification and compliance with Inspection and Test Plans (ITP).']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Current safety performance rating for the crew based on Health Safety and Environment (HSE) inspections, incident history, and compliance with Safe Work Method Statements (SWMS). Used for crew selection and safety incentive programs.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'Standard end time for the crew\'s shift in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for timesheet validation and labor hour planning.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'Standard start time for the crew\'s shift in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for timesheet validation and site access control.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern for this crew. Day shift is typical daytime hours; night shift is overnight; swing shift is evening; rotating shifts alternate; extended shifts are longer than standard 8-hour shifts.']
  size varchar [note: 'Current number of workers assigned to this crew, including the foreman. Used for resource planning and productivity rate calculations.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union or trade association to which the majority of crew members belong, if applicable. Null for non-union crews. Relevant for labor relations, collective bargaining agreements, and jurisdictional work rules.']

  Note: 'Master record for a named field crew or gang deployed on a construction project. Captures crew name, crew type (civil, MEP, structural, concrete, etc.), foreman assignment, home cost code, project assignment, crew size, and active status. A crew is the primary unit of field labor organization in construction operations. Distinct from individual worker records.'
}

Table workforce.crew_assignment {
  crew_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for crew_assignment']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Crew scheduling assigns crews to specific activities; required for dispatch, labor tracking, and activity labor cost reporting.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project where the crew assignment is active. Links to the project entity.']
