  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Connects labor rate definitions to the cost center that applies the rates, supporting the Labor Rate Master report used in payroll processing.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the labor cost code that categorizes this rate for job costing and financial reporting. Links to the WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) and SAP cost elements.']
  labor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the labor agreement or collective bargaining agreement under which this rate is defined. Links to union contracts, prevailing wage determinations, or project-specific labor agreements.']
  labor_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_cost_code. Business justification: Link labor_rate to labor_cost_code to associate each rate with its cost code definition, enabling removal of duplicate cost code attributes from labor_rate.']
  primary_labor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this labor rate record in the source system. Used for audit trail and data governance.']
  apprentice_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable ratio of apprentices to journeymen for this trade classification, as defined by union agreements or prevailing wage law (e.g., 1:3 means one apprentice per three journeymen). Nullable if not applicable.']
  base_hourly_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The straight-time hourly wage rate paid to the worker for regular hours (typically up to 8 hours per day or 40 hours per week). Excludes fringe benefits and burden.']
  certified_payroll_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate is subject to certified payroll reporting requirements under prevailing wage law (True) or not (False). Used to trigger compliance workflows in HCSS HeavyJob and Viewpoint Vista.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this labor rate record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency projects and international joint ventures.']
  double_time_hourly_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The hourly wage rate for double-time hours, typically 2.0x the base rate for hours worked on Sundays, holidays, or beyond a threshold (e.g., over 12 hours per day). Nullable if not applicable.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this labor rate expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended rates. Used to manage rate changes and contract renewals.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this labor rate becomes effective. Used for rate escalation tracking and historical cost analysis.']
  escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of any contractual escalation provisions that adjust this rate over time (e.g., annual CPI adjustment, fixed percentage increase, renegotiation trigger). Nullable if no escalation applies.']
  fringe_benefit_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The hourly cost of fringe benefits (health insurance, pension, vacation, training funds, etc.) as defined by the labor agreement or prevailing wage determination. Added to base wage to calculate total compensation.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic or contractual jurisdiction where this rate applies (e.g., Cook County IL, State of California, Federal Davis-Bacon). Used to enforce prevailing wage compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this labor rate record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental data refresh and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or clarifications regarding this labor rate (e.g., project-specific adjustments, negotiated exceptions, compliance notes).']
  overhead_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage markup applied to cover indirect costs and general overhead (field supervision, small tools, consumables, field office, etc.). Used in bid pricing and Guaranteed Maximum Price (GMP) calculations. Nullable if not applicable.']
  overtime_hourly_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The hourly wage rate for overtime hours, typically 1.5x the base rate for hours beyond 8 per day or 40 per week, as defined by labor agreements or prevailing wage law.']
  payroll_burden_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage markup applied to gross wages to cover employer-paid payroll taxes (FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers compensation insurance, general liability insurance). Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 35.50 for 35.5%).']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily per diem allowance for meals and incidental expenses when workers are mobilized away from their home location. Nullable if not applicable. Used in labor mobilization cost calculations.']
  prevailing_wage_determination_number varchar [note: 'The official reference number of the government-issued prevailing wage determination (e.g., Davis-Bacon wage decision number) that mandates this rate. Nullable for non-prevailing-wage projects.']
  profit_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The profit margin percentage applied to the fully burdened labor cost for bid pricing. Used in EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) and GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price) contracts. Nullable if not applicable.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the labor rate. Typically a concatenation of trade classification, skill level, and agreement reference used in estimating and job costing systems.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor rate record. Active rates are used in cost estimation and job costing. Expired or superseded rates are retained for historical cost analysis and audit trails.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate basis: union (collective bargaining agreement), prevailing_wage (government-mandated), open_shop (non-union competitive), project_specific (negotiated for a single project), or market_rate (regional benchmark).']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Additional hourly premium paid for non-standard shifts (e.g., night shift, swing shift). Nullable if not applicable. Used in 24/7 operations and accelerated project schedules.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'The skill or seniority level within the trade classification. Determines the applicable wage scale and supervisory responsibility.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this labor rate record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista, HCSS HeavyJob, Procore). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this labor rate record in the source operational system. Used for traceability and incremental data synchronization.']
  subsistence_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily subsistence allowance for lodging and living expenses when workers are required to stay overnight away from home. Nullable if not applicable. Used in remote project cost planning.']
  total_loaded_hourly_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The all-in hourly cost including base wage, fringe benefits, payroll burden, overhead, and profit. This is the rate used for project cost estimation, Bill of Quantities (BOQ) pricing, and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.']
  trade_classification varchar [note: 'The craft or trade classification for which this rate applies (e.g., Carpenter, Electrician, Pipefitter, Ironworker, Heavy Equipment Operator, Laborer). Aligns with union jurisdictions and prevailing wage schedules.']
  travel_zone varchar [note: 'The geographic travel zone or radius from the union hall or home base that determines eligibility for travel pay, per diem, and subsistence. Nullable if not applicable.']
  union_local varchar [note: 'The union local or labor organization that governs this rate (e.g., IBEW Local 134, Carpenters Local 1). Nullable for non-union or open-shop rates.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining the all-in labor rates (base wage, fringe benefits, burden, overhead) for each trade classification under a specific labor agreement or project. Captures trade classification, effective date range, straight-time rate, overtime rate, double-time rate, fringe benefit rate, payroll burden percentage, and total loaded rate. Used for project cost estimation, bid pricing (BOQ), and job cost control.'
}

Table workforce.agency_labor_order {
  agency_labor_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the agency labor order record.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the labor hire agency or staffing firm supplying the workers.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project for which the agency labor is requested.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost code for labor cost allocation and tracking.']
  primary_agency_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or project manager who requested the agency labor order.']
  site_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific site or location where the agency workers will be deployed.']
  skill_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Reference standardized skill_trade for trade classification, removing free-text trade_classification column.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element to which the agency labor cost will be charged.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the agency workers assignment concluded.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the agency workers commenced work on the project.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the agency labor order was approved.']
  bill_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Hourly billing rate charged by the agency per worker.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the agency labor order was cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or explanation for the cancellation of the agency labor order.']
  craft_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the craft discipline requested.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agency labor order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bill rate and pay rate.']
  estimated_end_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the agency workers assignment is expected to conclude.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date when the agency completed fulfillment of the order by supplying the requested workers.']
  hse_orientation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HSE site orientation is required for the agency workers before starting work.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agency labor order record was last modified or updated.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Agency markup percentage applied on top of the pay rate to arrive at the bill rate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General notes or comments related to the agency labor order.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the agency labor order was placed or submitted to the agency.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the agency labor order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agency labor order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|in_fulfillment|fulfilled|partially_fulfilled|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]']
  pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Hourly pay rate paid to the agency worker.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number issued to the agency for this labor order.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of PPE required for the agency workers on this assignment.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of certifications or qualifications required for the agency workers (e.g., OSHA 30, rigging certification, confined space).']
  required_start_date date [note: 'Date by which the agency workers are required to begin work on the project.']
  site_access_clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether site access clearance or background check is required for the agency workers.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Required skill or experience level for the requested workers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this agency labor order record originated (e.g., HCSS HeavyJob, SAP S/4HANA, Procore).']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Additional special requirements or instructions for the agency workers (e.g., language skills, equipment operation, shift preferences).']
  union_affiliation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency workers must be affiliated with a specific union or labor organization.']
  union_local varchar [note: 'Union local chapter or jurisdiction required for the agency workers.']
  workers_fulfilled_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of workers supplied by the agency to fulfill this order.']
  workers_requested_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of workers requested from the agency for this order.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a request placed with a labor hire agency or staffing firm to supply craft workers for a project. Captures agency identity, trade classification requested, number of workers, required qualifications, required start date, project and site, bill rate, markup percentage, PO reference, fulfillment status, and workers actually supplied. Tracks the full lifecycle from request through fulfillment. Distinct from subcontractor work orders — covers temporary staffing arrangements where workers report to the GC\'s supervision.'
}

Table workforce.apprenticeship_progression {
  apprenticeship_progression_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each apprenticeship progression record.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the craft worker to whom this apprenticeship record belongs.']
  prior_apprenticeship_progression_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on apprenticeship_progression (prior_apprenticeship_progression_id)']
  actual_journeyman_completion_date date [note: 'Date the apprentice actually attained journeyman status, if applicable.']
  apprentice_to_journeyman_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of apprentices to journeymen required for prevailing‑wage project compliance.']
  apprenticeship_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual end date of the apprenticeship (nullable if ongoing).']
  apprenticeship_hours_required decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total OJT hours required to complete the apprenticeship.']
  apprenticeship_level varchar [note: 'Current apprenticeship level or period (e.g., Level 1, Year 2, Journeyman Candidate).']
  apprenticeship_period varchar [note: 'Designated period within the apprenticeship program (e.g., "Year 1", "Quarter 3").']
  apprenticeship_program_code varchar [note: 'Standard code identifying the apprenticeship program (e.g., NCCER, JATC, state program).']
  apprenticeship_program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the apprenticeship program.']
  apprenticeship_start_date date [note: 'Date the worker entered the apprenticeship program.']
  apprenticeship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the apprenticeship record.']
  apprenticeship_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., "suspended due to safety violation").']
  apprenticeship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the apprenticeship program (union‑sponsored, state‑registered, private, or company‑run).']
  certification_earned_date date [note: 'Date the required certification was awarded.']
  certification_earned_flag boolean [note: 'True if the apprentice has earned the required certification for the trade.']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the apprenticeship record with DOL and state requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the apprenticeship progression record was first created.']
  dol_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the apprenticeship meets all Department of Labor requirements.']
  expected_journeyman_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date when the apprentice is expected to achieve journeyman status.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the record.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to apprenticeship_status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations about the apprenticeship.']
  ojt_hours_accumulated decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total OJT hours the worker has logged toward the required apprenticeship hours.']
  prevailing_wage_classification varchar [note: 'Classification used for prevailing‑wage calculations (e.g., "Skilled Craft").']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the record (e.g., HCSS HeavyJob, SAP SuccessFactors).']
  sponsoring_jatc varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Joint Apprenticeship Training Committee sponsoring the apprenticeship.']
  state_apprenticeship_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the apprenticeship complies with the applicable state apprenticeship agency rules.']
  technical_instruction_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative classroom or online instruction hours completed for the apprenticeship.']
  technical_instruction_hours_required decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total classroom or online instruction hours required for the apprenticeship.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Organization that delivered the technical instruction hours.']
  union_local varchar [note: 'Union local identifier associated with the apprentice, if applicable.']
  wage_progression_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current wage progression step became effective.']
  wage_progression_step varchar [note: 'Current step in the wage progression schedule for the apprentice.']
  wage_step varchar [note: 'Current wage step associated with the apprentice\'s progression.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking a craft worker\'s progression through a registered apprenticeship program (NCCER, union JATC, or state-registered). Captures current apprenticeship level/period, accumulated OJT (On-the-Job Training) hours by trade, related technical instruction hours completed, wage progression step, sponsoring JATC (Joint Apprenticeship Training Committee), expected journey-level completion date, and compliance status with DOL (Department of Labor) or state apprenticeship agency requirements. Supports Davis-Bacon Act apprentice-to-journeyman ratio compliance, certified payroll reporting of apprentice classifications, and union reporting of hours toward journeyman upgrade. Critical for contractors participating in federal/state prevailing wage projects where apprentice utilization ratios are audited.'
}

Table workforce.carbon_reduction_participation {
  carbon_reduction_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the carbon_reduction_participation association']
  carbon_reduction_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carbon reduction initiative']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the craft worker']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the craft worker ended participation in the initiative']
  hours_contributed decimal [note: 'Number of labor hours the craft worker contributed to the initiative']
  participation_role varchar [note: 'The role of the craft worker within the initiative (e.g., installer, supervisor)']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the craft worker started participation in the initiative']

  Note: 'Represents the participation of a craft worker in a carbon reduction initiative, capturing the role, hours contributed, and the period of involvement. Each record links one craft worker to one initiative.. Existence Justification: Craft workers can be assigned to multiple carbon reduction initiatives, and each initiative can involve many craft workers. The organization actively records each worker\'s participation role, hours contributed, and the start/end dates of the involvement for compliance and reporting purposes.'
}

Table workforce.agency {
  agency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for agency']
  parent_agency_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on agency (parent_agency_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the agency.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  agency_code varchar [note: 'Business code or abbreviation used internally to identify the agency.']
  agency_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the primary function of the agency.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for payments to the agency.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number associated with the agency\'s bank account.']
  bonding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Bond amount provided by the agency for project guarantees.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the agency is located.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the agency is internal to the organization or an external partner.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the agency with regulatory requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the agency operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agency record was first created.']
  default_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for transactions with the agency.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the agency relationship becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the agency relationship ends or is scheduled to end (null if open‑ended).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for agency communications.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount covered by the agency\'s liability insurance.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or financial audit of the agency.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the agency as registered with authorities.']
  license_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the agency\'s license.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Official license number issued to the agency.']
  name varchar [note: 'Common name used to reference the agency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the agency.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the agency.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the agency.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the agency address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact at the agency.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Internal rating (1‑5) reflecting agency performance and reliability.']
  safety_certification varchar [note: 'Safety certification held by the agency.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the agency\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the agency.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency is exempt from tax reporting.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identification number for the agency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the agency record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the agency.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for agency. Referenced by agency_id.'
}

Table schedule.activity {
  activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the schedule activity.']
  change_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.change_notice. Business justification: Change Notice Management requires each affected activity to reference the change notice that altered its scope or duration.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit compliance tracking: each scheduled activity must reference the required construction permit to ensure legal start and permit status monitoring.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Needed for project status dashboards that aggregate activity progress per construction project; each activity must be directly linked to its project.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Each activity has a designated foreman/supervisor responsible for safety and execution; needed for safety incident reporting and activity oversight.']
  design_submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: Submittal Review Gate process blocks activity start until the related submittal is approved; linking provides the Submittal Status Dashboard.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: Environmental impact assessments are tied to specific construction activities (e.g., earthworks) for regulatory reporting and mitigation planning.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Cost coding of schedule activities for budgeting and earned value reporting requires linking each activity to a finance cost_code entity.']
  interface_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.interface_point. Business justification: Interface Coordination Schedule uses interface_point_id to align multidisciplinary handover dates with activity sequencing.']
  itp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.itp. Business justification: ITP‑tracking report requires each activity to reference its ITP; activity references itp_id for planning and compliance.']
  mep_coordination_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.mep_coordination_zone. Business justification: MEP Coordination Zone planning links activities to specific zones; zone_id is required for zone‑based resource allocation and clash resolution.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Activity ownership is needed for accountability, performance tracking, and audit of who is responsible for each scheduled task.']
  permit_to_work_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.permit_to_work. Business justification: Activities requiring hazardous work must reference the associated Permit‑to‑Work to manage authorization and control measures.']
  project_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the schedule baseline to which this activity belongs.']
  rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: RFI Impact Log tracks how pending RFIs delay activities; schedule needs rfi_id to calculate schedule impact days.']
  schedule_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the calendar to which the activity is assigned.']
  swms_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.swms. Business justification: During planning, each activity is linked to its approved Safe Work Method Statement to ensure compliance with safety procedures.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'FK to project.wbs_element.wbs_element_id — Core project-centric reporting spine: every schedule activity must link to a WBS element for earned value, progress reporting, and cost integration. Without this FK, schedule-to-project reporting is b']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: Activities belong to a WBS node; linking provides hierarchy and removes redundant wbs_code column.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the activity (e.g., unique activity number).']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the work to be performed.']
  activity_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the activity.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the activity.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the activity based on its nature (task‑dependent, resource‑dependent, level of effort, or milestone).']
  actual_finish_date date [note: 'Date the activity actually finished.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date the activity actually started.']
  constraint_date date [note: 'Date associated with the scheduling constraint, if applicable.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling constraint applied to the activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the activity record was first created.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'True if the activity lies on the critical path.']
  free_float_days varchar [note: 'Free float available for the activity in days.']
  lookahead_finish_date date [note: 'Finish date used in the look‑ahead planning window.']
  lookahead_start_date date [note: 'Start date used in the look‑ahead planning window.']
  original_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the activity in calendar days at creation.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current percent complete of the activity (0‑100).']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Scheduled finish date as originally planned.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date as originally planned.']
  remaining_duration_days varchar [note: 'Remaining duration in days based on current progress.']
  total_float_days varchar [note: 'Total float available for the activity in days.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the activity record.']

  Note: 'Core CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule activity representing a discrete unit of work within a project WBS. Captures activity ID, name, WBS code, activity type (task-dependent, resource-dependent, level of effort, milestone), planned/actual start and finish dates, original duration, remaining duration, percent complete, calendar assignment, constraint types (start-no-earlier-than, finish-no-later-than, mandatory), float values (total float, free float), critical path flag, activity status (not started, in progress, completed, suspended), and activity code assignments for filtering and grouping. The foundational scheduling entity from which all schedule analysis, critical path calculation, and progress measurement derives.'
}

Table schedule.activity_relationship {
  activity_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the activity relationship record.']
  primary_predecessor_activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the predecessor activity in the CPM network.']
  activity_relationship_description varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments describing the purpose or special conditions of the relationship.']
  activity_relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the relationship record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the relationship record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the relationship becomes effective in the schedule.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the relationship expires or is no longer valid (nullable).']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship lies on the project\'s critical path (true) or not (false).']
  is_driving boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this relationship drives the schedule logic (true) or is a passive link (false).']
  lag_duration decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric amount of lag (or lead if negative) applied to the relationship, expressed in the units defined by lag_time_unit.']
  lag_time_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for lag_duration (e.g., days, hours, minutes).']
  relationship_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the relationship record: exported from scheduling tool or entered manually.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Logical dependency type defining how the predecessor and successor activities are linked.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the relationship record.']

  Note: 'Logical dependency links between schedule activities defining the CPM network logic. Captures predecessor and successor activity references, relationship type (Finish-to-Start, Start-to-Start, Finish-to-Finish, Start-to-Finish), lag/lead duration, lag time unit, driving relationship flag, and relationship source (scheduling tool export, manual entry). Enables critical path calculation, schedule network analysis, and what-if scenario modeling for delay impact assessment.'
}

Table schedule.schedule_baseline {
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the schedule baseline record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Baseline approval audit requires recording the employee who approved the baseline for governance and compliance.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this baseline belongs.']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Baseline schedules are updated when new regulatory changes affect project timelines; linking captures the change that triggered baseline revision.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the baseline was formally approved for use.']
  baseline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the baseline indicating its purpose: original contract baseline, current working baseline, or supplemental revision.']
  bcws_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of work scheduled in the baseline, used for Earned Value calculations.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for creating a supplemental or revised baseline.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the baseline record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the baseline.']
  data_date date [note: 'The date representing the snapshot of schedule data used to create the baseline.']
  finish_date date [note: 'Planned finish date of the schedule baseline.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baseline is the active baseline for schedule comparisons.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date on which the baseline was last revised or re‑baselined.']
  schedule_baseline_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or notable characteristics of the baseline.']
  schedule_baseline_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the schedule baseline (e.g., "Original Contract Baseline").']
  schedule_baseline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the baseline record.']
  schedule_tool_project_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier of the project within the scheduling tool (e.g., Primavera project code).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating scheduling system that generated the baseline.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the schedule baseline.']
  total_duration_days varchar [note: 'Calculated total duration of the baseline in calendar days.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the baseline record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number indicating the order of baseline revisions.']

  Note: 'Approved project schedule baseline snapshot capturing the time-phased plan against which actual progress is measured. Stores baseline name, baseline type (original, current, supplemental), approval date, approved-by reference, baseline start and finish dates, total baseline duration, baseline data date, and scheduling tool baseline project reference. Supports EVM (Earned Value Management) calculations including BCWS (Budgeted Cost of Work Scheduled) and variance analysis. Multiple baselines per project are supported (original contract baseline, re-baselined approved revisions).'
}

Table schedule.baseline_activity {
  baseline_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the baseline activity record.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master activity definition (work breakdown element).']
  project_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the schedule baseline to which this activity belongs.']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element that groups this activity.']
  baseline_activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the activity within the baseline.']
  baseline_constraint_date date [note: 'Date associated with the constraint type, if applicable.']
  baseline_constraint_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling constraint applied to the activity in the baseline.']
  baseline_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned cost for the activity as captured in the approved baseline.']
  baseline_cost_variance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between actual cost incurred and baseline cost estimate.']
  baseline_early_finish date [note: 'Earliest possible finish date for the activity in the approved baseline.']
  baseline_early_start date [note: 'Earliest possible start date for the activity in the approved baseline.']
  baseline_is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the activity lies on the critical path of the baseline schedule.']
  baseline_late_finish date [note: 'Latest permissible finish date for the activity in the approved baseline.']
  baseline_late_start date [note: 'Latest permissible start date for the activity in the approved baseline.']
  baseline_milestone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activity is defined as a milestone in the baseline.']
  baseline_original_duration varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the activity at baseline approval, expressed in whole days.']
  baseline_percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of work completed for the activity at the baseline snapshot.']
  baseline_remaining_duration varchar [note: 'Remaining planned duration as of the latest progress update, based on the baseline.']
  baseline_resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of resources assigned to the activity in the baseline.']
  baseline_resource_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total resource units (e.g., labor hours, equipment hours) planned for the activity in the baseline.']
  baseline_schedule_variance varchar [note: 'Difference between actual start/finish and baseline dates, expressed in days.']
  baseline_total_float varchar [note: 'Amount of scheduling flexibility (float) for the activity in the baseline, expressed in days.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the baseline activity record was created in the data lake.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the activity within the baseline schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the baseline activity record.']

  Note: 'Baseline-frozen snapshot of each activity\'s planned dates, durations, and resource assignments at the time a schedule baseline was approved. Stores baseline early start, baseline early finish, baseline late start, baseline late finish, baseline original duration, baseline remaining duration, baseline percent complete, and baseline total float for each activity within a specific baseline. Enables schedule variance analysis (SV = BCWP - BCWS) and start/finish variance reporting against the approved plan.'
}

Table schedule.resource {
  resource_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for resource']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: Resource Costing & Compliance: tying resource equipment_category to the master asset_category allows accurate cost rates, depreciation tracking, and regulatory reporting on equipment usage.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Schedule resources often represent individual workers; linking enables labor allocation reports and compliance with union and safety regulations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Labor allocation report and payroll integration require linking each labor resource to the employee performing the work.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Resource allocation reports must attribute external resources to the subcontractor providing them, enabling cost and performance tracking.']
  last_assigned_activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent activity to which the resource was assigned.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: RESOURCE PLANNING: External subcontractor/vendor provides the resource; procurement contracts vendor, schedule assigns resource to activities. Linking enables traceability of vendor‑supplied labor/equ']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned availability of the resource expressed as a percent of total time.']
  billing_rate_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Rate used for billing external parties for labor resources.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Name of the work calendar associated with the resource (defines working days/hours).']
  certification_requirements varchar [note: 'Required certifications or trainings for the resource (e.g., OSHA, LEED).']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or safety compliance items applicable to the resource (e.g., OSHA training).']
  cost_account_code varchar [note: 'Accounting code used to charge resource costs.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Organizational cost centre responsible for the resource.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the resource record was first created in the system.']
  default_units_per_time decimal(10,2) [note: 'Default allocation quantity when the resource is assigned without explicit units.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the resource over its useful life.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the resource is no longer available (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the resource becomes available for scheduling.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the resource\'s environmental impact (e.g., emissions, waste).']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if the resource is provided by an external subcontractor or supplier.']
  is_overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime work is permitted for this resource.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Category of labor (e.g., skilled, unskilled, supervisory).']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the resource was last assigned to an activity.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to procure or mobilize the resource.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'Classification of material (e.g., concrete, steel, piping).']
  max_concurrent_assignments varchar [note: 'Maximum number of activities the resource can be assigned to simultaneously.']
  max_units_per_period decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the resource that can be allocated in a scheduling period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the resource.']
  overtime_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the price when the resource works overtime.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost charged for one unit of the resource, used for cost‑loaded schedules.']
  procurement_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the resource is sourced internally or from an external vendor.']
  resource_code varchar [note: 'External identifier or catalogue number for the resource.']
  resource_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the resource.']
  resource_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the resource as used in schedules.']
  resource_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the resource in the project (e.g., carpenter, electrician).']
  resource_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the resource.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the resource (e.g., labor, material, equipment, subcontractor).']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the resource based on internal assessments.']
  site_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or location where the resource is primarily used.']
  skill_set varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of skills or competencies associated with the resource.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the resource (e.g., hour, day, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the resource record.']
  utilization_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical or planned utilization of the resource expressed as a percent.']

  Note: 'Resource definitions assigned to schedule activities for resource-loaded scheduling and leveling. Captures resource name, resource type (labor, non-labor, material), unit of measure, max units per time period, default units per time, price per unit, resource calendar, overtime factor, and resource role. Sourced from the enterprise scheduling tool resource dictionary. Supports resource leveling, resource histograms, and cost-loaded schedule generation. Distinct from workforce employee records — this is the scheduling resource abstraction used for capacity planning and EVM cost loading.'
}

Table schedule.activity_resource_assignment {
  activity_resource_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the resource assignment to an activity.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project activity to which the resource is assigned.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Equipment Assignment Planning: linking each activity resource assignment to a specific asset enables the Equipment Allocation Report and ensures compliance with the daily equipment utilization schedul']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Resource assignments are charged to cost centers; linking enables accurate cost allocation and financial reporting per cost center.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posting resource assignment costs to GL accounts requires linking assignments to finance.gl_account for proper ledger entries.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_party. Business justification: Resource Assignment Report requires linking each assignment to the responsible contract party (subcontractor) for cost and compliance tracking.']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the resource (labor, equipment, material, etc.) assigned to the activity.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Tracks which subcontract provides the assigned resources, enabling accurate cost allocation and subcontractor performance analysis.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to date for the resource consumption.']
  actual_finish_date date [note: 'Date when the resource actually completed work on the activity.']
  actual_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Resource units actually consumed to date.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when the resource actually began work on the activity.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the assignment.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the resource assignment.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a change order that modified this assignment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment meets regulatory or contract compliance requirements.']
  cost_account_code varchar [note: 'Accounting code used for cost allocation of this assignment.']
  cost_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the resource (e.g., $ per hour).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created.']
  distribution_curve varchar [note: 'Shape of the resource loading over time for the assignment.']
  finish_date date [note: 'Scheduled finish date for the resource on the activity.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'True if the assignment lies on the project’s critical path.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of labor skill level for the assigned resource.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the assignment within the activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the assignment.']
  overtime_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional resource units worked as overtime.']
  overtime_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost per overtime unit of the resource.']
  planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost associated with the planned quantity of the resource.']
  planned_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount of resource units (e.g., hours, cubic meters) planned for the assignment.']
  remaining_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost minus actual cost, representing cost remaining.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned units minus actual units, representing work left.']
  resource_location varchar [note: 'Site or location where the resource is deployed for the activity.']
  resource_role varchar [note: 'Specific role the resource performs on the activity.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of the assigned resource (e.g., labor, equipment).']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for safety associated with the assignment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the resource on the activity.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., hours, cubic meters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element to which the assignment belongs.']

  Note: 'Assignment of a schedule resource to a specific activity, capturing the planned and actual resource consumption. Stores assigned resource reference, activity reference, budgeted units, actual units to date, remaining units, budgeted cost, actual cost, remaining cost, resource distribution curve (front-loaded, back-loaded, bell, linear), overtime units, and assignment status. Enables resource-loaded schedule analysis, cost-loaded CPM, and EVM BCWP (Budgeted Cost of Work Performed) calculations.'
}

Table schedule.wbs_node {
  wbs_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the WBS node.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project to which the WBS belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WBS nodes are allocated to cost centers for budgeting; linking provides cost‑center roll‑up for each WBS element.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary individual responsible for the node.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organization accountable for the node.']
  parent_wbs_wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent WBS node; null for top‑level nodes.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to date for the node.']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed days from start to finish.']
  actual_finish_date date [note: 'Date when work actually completed for the node.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when work actually commenced on the node.']
  baseline_finish_date date [note: 'Baseline finish date used for Earned Value Management comparisons.']
  baseline_start_date date [note: 'Baseline start date used for Earned Value Management comparisons.']
  budgeted_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original cost estimate for the node before any revisions.']
  change_order_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the node has been impacted by a change order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the WBS node record was created in the system.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node lies on the project\'s critical path.']
  deleted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the node has been logically deleted; false otherwise.']
  earned_value_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to calculate earned value for the node.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the node in external systems (e.g., Primavera activity ID).']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'True if the node represents a project milestone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by planners.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current progress expressed as a percentage of total planned effort.']
  planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost allocated to the node.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the node in calendar days.']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Scheduled finish date as originally planned.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date as originally planned.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned to the node.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the WBS node record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Unique hierarchical code assigned to the WBS node, used for identification and reporting.']
  wbs_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the node within the WBS hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  wbs_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the WBS node representing the scope element.']
  wbs_node_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the scope element.']
  wbs_node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WBS node.']
  wbs_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node (e.g., project summary, work package, activity, milestone).']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) hierarchy node defining the decomposition of project scope into manageable components. Captures WBS code, WBS name, WBS level, parent WBS reference, WBS type (project summary, WBS summary, work package), anticipated start and finish dates, earned value technique (fixed formula, percent complete, weighted milestones, level of effort), and WBS status. Provides the hierarchical framework for schedule organization, cost control, progress reporting, and EVM roll-up calculations.'
}

Table schedule.schedule_calendar {
  schedule_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the work calendar.']
  break_time_minutes_per_day varchar [note: 'Total minutes of mandatory break time deducted from the workday.']
  calendar_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of calendars for reporting (standard, custom, or regional).']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the calendar (e.g., "CAL‑US‑NY").']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the calendar used in scheduling tools.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the calendar: global (company‑wide), project‑specific, or resource‑specific.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calendar record was first created in the source system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the calendar ceases to be active; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the calendar becomes active for scheduling calculations.']
  holiday_exceptions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list or description of holidays and non‑working dates that override the regular schedule.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this calendar is the default for its scope.']
  max_overtime_hours_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper limit of overtime hours that can be applied in a single day.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if overtime hours may be scheduled under this calendar.']
  schedule_calendar_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the calendar.']
  schedule_calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Definition of work shifts applied to the calendar.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system (e.g., "Primavera P6").']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier (e.g., "America/New_York") that the calendar operates in.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the calendar record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the calendar definition for change tracking.']
  work_days_per_week varchar [note: 'Number of days in a week that are considered working days.']
  work_hours_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of productive hours defined for a typical workday.']
  work_week_definition varchar [note: 'Encoded representation (e.g., JSON string) of which days of the week are working days.']

  Note: 'Work calendars defining working and non-working time periods used in schedule calculations. Captures calendar name, calendar type (global, project, resource), standard work hours per day, work week definition (working days per week), holiday exceptions, shift patterns, and time zone. Imported from the enterprise scheduling tool calendar configuration. Determines activity duration calculations, float computations, and resource availability windows. Critical for multi-site international projects operating across different regional calendars and public holiday schedules.'
}

Table schedule.progress_update {
  progress_update_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the schedule progress update record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this progress update belongs.']
  actual_finish_date date [note: 'Actual finish date of the activity or work package as of this reporting period.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual start date of the activity or work package as of this reporting period.']
  actual_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual quantity of work units performed (e.g., labor hours, cubic meters).']
  bcwp decimal(12,2) [note: 'Earned Value (BCWP) in project currency.']
  bcws decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned Value (BCWS) in project currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the progress update record was initially created in the system.']
  critical_activity_count varchar [note: 'Number of activities currently on the critical path.']
  critical_path_completion_date date [note: 'Estimated completion date of the critical path at this reporting period.']
  forecast_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date for project completion based on current performance trends.']
  is_critical_path_changed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the critical path has changed compared to the previous reporting period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations related to the progress update.']
  path_drift_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the critical path is on schedule, drifting, or off track.']
  percent_complete_duration decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of duration completed (0-100).']
  percent_complete_units decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work units completed (0-100).']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the reporting period within the project schedule.']
  progress_update_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the progress update record.']
  remaining_duration varchar [note: 'Remaining duration in days for the activity or work package.']
  remaining_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Remaining quantity of work units to be performed.']
  reporting_date date [note: 'Date for which the progress update snapshot is recorded.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which progress updates are generated for the project.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by this update.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by this update.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reporting period (e.g., pending, completed, overdue).']
  spi decimal(5,3) [note: 'Schedule Performance Index calculated as BCWP divided by BCWS.']
  sv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Schedule Variance (BCWP minus BCWS) in project currency.']
  sv_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Schedule Variance expressed as a percentage of BCWS.']
  total_float decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total float (slack) in days for the critical path.']
  update_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data for this progress update (e.g., field report, Primavera P6 import, HCSS HeavyJob).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the progress update record.']

  Note: 'Periodic schedule progress update record capturing actual performance data, EVM metrics, and critical path state at a specific data date. Stores actual start/finish dates, remaining duration, percent complete (duration, units, physical), actual/remaining units, update source (field report, P6 import, HCSS HeavyJob), reporting period definition (period number, dates, frequency, status), EVM metrics (BCWP, BCWS, SPI, SV, SV%, forecast completion date), and critical path snapshot (project completion date, total float, critical activity count, path drift indicator). Represents the single transactional record of schedule state per reporting period. Enables schedule performance trending, SPI reporting, and critical path monitoring over time.'
}

Table schedule.lookahead_plan {
  lookahead_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lookahead plan record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this lookahead plan belongs.']
  primary_lookahead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the superintendent or foreman responsible for this lookahead period.']
  project_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the schedule baseline referenced by the lookahead plan.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'True if any change orders are expected within the lookahead window.']
  constraint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified constraint.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Primary constraint type affecting the planned work.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lookahead plan record was created in the system.']
  crew_ready_flag boolean [note: 'True if the necessary crew is confirmed available for the lookahead period.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'True if any activity in the lookahead is on the project critical path.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the plan.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the lookahead period.']
  equipment_ready_flag boolean [note: 'True if required equipment is confirmed available for the lookahead period.']
  horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks covered by the lookahead window.']
  is_lps_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the lookahead follows the Last Planner System methodology.']
  material_ready_flag boolean [note: 'True if all required materials are confirmed available for the lookahead period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments or observations related to the lookahead plan.']
  pending_activities varchar [note: 'Number of activities still pending readiness.']
  percent_plan_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of planned activities that are ready to be executed (0‑100).']
  plan_date date [note: 'Date on which the lookahead plan was generated.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the lookahead plan, used in project documentation.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lookahead plan.']
  planned_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for the activities in the lookahead period.']
  ppc_actual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual Percent Plan Complete achieved during the lookahead period.']
  ppc_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Percent Plan Complete (PPC) for the lookahead period.']
  readiness_status varchar [note: 'Overall readiness status of the work front for execution.']
  ready_activities varchar [note: 'Number of activities marked as ready for execution.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level associated with the lookahead plan.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the underlying master schedule used for this lookahead.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the lookahead period.']
  total_activities varchar [note: 'Total number of activities scheduled in the lookahead window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lookahead plan record.']
  weather_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if adverse weather conditions are expected to impact the lookahead activities.']
  work_front varchar [note: 'Name or code of the work front or zone covered by the lookahead plan.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code representing the specific site zone for the plan.']

  Note: 'Short-interval look-ahead schedule (typically 3-week or 6-week rolling window) used for near-term construction planning and crew coordination. Captures look-ahead period dates, horizon (weeks), responsible superintendent/foreman, work front or zone, constraint identification, and readiness status (materials, permits, crew, equipment confirmed). Supports Last Planner System (LPS) and Percent Plan Complete (PPC) tracking. Bridges the gap between the master CPM schedule and daily site execution. The header record for lookahead_activity line items.'
}

Table schedule.lookahead_activity {
  lookahead_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lookahead activity record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of primary equipment assigned to the activity.']
  cpm_activity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CPM activity in Primavera P6 that this lookahead activity maps to.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Lookahead planning assigns specific crews to upcoming activities; required for crew dispatch, resource leveling, and labor budgeting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the foreman responsible for the activity.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of subcontractor responsible for the activity, if any.']
  lookahead_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent look-ahead plan.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of primary material required for the activity.']
  project_change_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the related change order, if applicable.']
  actual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the activity.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual duration of the activity in hours.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual end time recorded when work finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual start time recorded when work began.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity is associated with a change order.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current commitment status of the activity.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for the activity.']
  cost_variance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between estimated and actual cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lookahead activity record was created.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Number of crew members planned to work on the activity.']
  equipment_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of equipment units planned for the activity.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the activity is on the project critical path.']
  is_equipment_intensive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity relies heavily on equipment.']
  is_labor_intensive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity is primarily labor intensive.']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity is a project milestone.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Work location or zone code where the activity will be performed.']
  lookahead_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the activity within the look‑ahead plan.']
  material_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned quantity of material required (units).']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the milestone when the activity represents a milestone.']
  non_completion_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for non‑completion when status is not completed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes or comments for the activity.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Planned execution date for the activity within the look-ahead window.']
  planned_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned duration of the activity in hours.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the activity.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the activity.']
  ppc_contribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the activity contributes to Percent Plan Complete (PPC).']
  required_permits varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of permits required for the activity.']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required for the crew (e.g., apprentice, journeyman, specialist).']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Safety risk classification for the activity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  variance_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Difference between planned and actual duration.']
  weather_constraint_description varchar [note: 'Description of weather constraints (e.g., rain, temperature).']
  weather_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if weather conditions affect the activity.']

  Note: 'Individual planned task within a look-ahead plan, representing a specific work item committed for execution within the short-interval planning window. Captures parent look-ahead plan reference, linked CPM activity reference, planned execution date, planned crew size, planned equipment, planned material requirements, responsible foreman, work location/zone, commitment status (planned, committed, completed, not completed), reason for non-completion code, and PPC (Percent Plan Complete) contribution flag. Enables weekly work plan tracking and constraint removal workflows.'
}

Table schedule.schedule_milestone {
  schedule_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the schedule milestone. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit a predefined role, treated as OTHER.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract to which the milestone is tied.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the milestone belongs.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: Milestone reconciliation report aligns contract milestones with schedule milestones for payment certification and performance monitoring.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Milestones are owned by a responsible foreman; linking supports accountability, progress reporting, and compliance audits.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role responsible for delivering the milestone.']
  payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_payment_certificate. Business justification: Payment certification requires linking schedule milestones to the contract payment certificate for audit, retention release, and compliance reporting.']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: Milestones are part of the WBS hierarchy; linking removes redundant wbs_code.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date the milestone was actually achieved; null if not yet achieved.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Original baseline date from the approved project schedule before any re‑baselines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'True if the milestone lies on the project\'s critical path, affecting overall project duration.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Most recent forecasted date for milestone completion based on progress and risk analysis.']
  ld_exposure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone triggers liquidated damages if missed.']
  ld_rate_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged per day of delay when the LD exposure flag is true.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical site or geographic location where the milestone is to be achieved.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Date originally scheduled for the milestone according to the baseline CPM schedule.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the milestone based on impact and probability of delay.']
  schedule_milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and scope of the milestone.']
  schedule_milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the milestone as defined in the contract or project plan.']
  schedule_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the milestone reflecting progress and risk.']
  schedule_milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone (e.g., contract‑driven, internal project, client‑required, or regulatory).']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the milestone within the overall schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned (or baseline) date and actual/forecast date.']

  Note: 'Key project milestone records representing contractually significant or internally critical schedule events with zero duration. Captures milestone name, milestone type (contract milestone, internal milestone, client milestone, regulatory milestone), planned date, forecast date, actual achieved date, milestone status (not started, at risk, achieved, missed), contract reference, liquidated damages (LD) exposure flag, LD rate per day, and milestone owner. Derived from contract schedule requirements and master CPM schedule milestone activities. Enables contract compliance tracking, LD exposure monitoring, and executive schedule reporting.'
}

Table schedule.schedule_eot_claim {
  schedule_eot_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the EOT claim record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Links EOT claim to the client account responsible for the delay, required for claim justification and regulatory reporting of party responsibilities.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the claim relates.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: EOT claim creation audit needs the employee identifier to ensure traceability and accountability.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., contractor or subcontractor) submitting the claim.']
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the baseline schedule against which the delay is measured.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the claim was approved (or partially approved) by the employer.']
  approved_extension_days varchar [note: 'Number of days approved by the employer after claim evaluation.']
  claim_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the claim, used in contract administration and communications.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the claim within the contract administration workflow.']
  claim_submission_date date [note: 'Date the claim was formally submitted to the employer or client.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delay event triggering the claim, aligned with contract clauses such as FIDIC Sub‑Clause 8.4.']
  claimed_extension_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the claimant requests to extend the contract completion date.']
  contract_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractual clause (e.g., FIDIC Sub‑Clause 8.4) that governs the claim.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  delay_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the delay event as recorded in the project schedule.']
  delay_event_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the excusable delay event that caused the time extension request.']
  delay_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the delay event as recorded in the project schedule.']
  impact_on_schedule varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of how the delay affects overall project schedule performance.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delayed activity lies on the project\'s critical path (true) or not (false).']
  justification_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., document number or URL) to supporting documentation submitted with the claim.']
  supporting_schedule_analysis_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the schedule analysis (e.g., critical path report) that substantiates the claim.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the claim record.']

  Note: 'Extension of Time (EOT) claim record documenting a formal request to extend the contract completion date due to excusable delay events. Captures claim reference number, claim type (employer risk, force majeure, concurrent delay, neutral event), delay event description, delay start and end dates, claimed extension days, approved extension days, claim status (draft, submitted, under review, approved, rejected, partially approved), contract clause reference (FIDIC Sub-Clause 8.4), supporting schedule analysis reference, and claim submission date. Critical for contract administration and schedule recovery planning.'
}

Table schedule.delay_event {
  delay_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delay event record.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Party held responsible for the delay.']
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_baseline. Business justification: Delay events affect a specific schedule baseline and may be tied to an EOT claim; linking creates proper relationships and removes string reference columns.']
  schedule_eot_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_eot_claim. Business justification: Link delay events to the EOT claim they support, enabling traceability.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: DELAY CLAIMS: Delay events often stem from vendor delivery or performance; linking to vendor supports root‑cause analysis and EOT claim justification.']
  activity_ids_impacted varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of activity identifiers that are affected by the delay.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the delay event record was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the delay event record.']
