  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the cost impact amount.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'System user who created the delay event record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delay event record was first created in the system.']
  delay_category varchar [note: 'Legal/contractual classification of the delay.']
  delay_duration_calendar_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days the event delayed the schedule.']
  delay_duration_working_days varchar [note: 'Total number of working days the event delayed the schedule, accounting for non‑working days.']
  delay_event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the cause and nature of the delay.']
  delay_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the delay event record.']
  eot_claim_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the linked Extension of Time claim.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delay event ended or is expected to end.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the delay event.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delay event began.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the delay event such as weather, design change, employer instruction, utility conflict, permit delay, labor dispute, material shortage, or force majeure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|design_change|employer_instruction|utility_conflict|permit_delay|labor_dispute|material_shortage|force_majeure — promote to reference product]']
  impact_on_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary cost impact associated with the delay.']
  impact_on_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delay impacts the project critical path (true/false).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'System user who last modified the delay event record.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Actions taken to mitigate or recover from the delay.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the delay event.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the delay event.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the delay\'s impact on schedule and cost.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the delay event data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delay event record.']

  Note: 'Discrete delay event record capturing a specific occurrence that caused or is causing schedule delay. Stores event name, event type (weather, design change, employer instruction, utility conflict, permit delay, labor dispute, material shortage, force majeure), event start date, event end date, impacted activities, delay duration (calendar days, working days), responsibility party (employer, contractor, third party, neutral), delay category (excusable compensable, excusable non-compensable, non-excusable), linked EOT claim reference, and mitigation measures taken. Supports delay analysis (as-planned vs as-built, time impact analysis, windows analysis).'
}

Table schedule.schedule_risk {
  schedule_risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the schedule risk entry. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit predefined role categories, classified as OTHER.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Risk management processes assign a responsible employee; linking enables tracking mitigation responsibility.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Risk register links each schedule risk to the underlying regulatory obligation that creates the risk, enabling compliance‑driven risk mitigation.']
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_baseline. Business justification: Schedule risks are evaluated against a specific baseline; linking provides context.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: RISK MANAGEMENT: Schedule risk register tracks vendor‑related performance risks; linking to vendor allows risk owner identification and contractual mitigation.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk was formally assessed.']
  contingency_reserve_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of schedule contingency set aside for this risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the risk becomes active or applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the risk is no longer considered active, if applicable.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions and strategies to reduce the probability or impact of the risk.']
  monte_carlo_p50_date date [note: 'Projected completion date at the 50th percentile from Monte Carlo simulation.']
  monte_carlo_p80_date date [note: 'Projected completion date at the 80th percentile from Monte Carlo simulation.']
  monte_carlo_p85_date date [note: 'Projected completion date at the 85th percentile from Monte Carlo simulation.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit responsible for the risk.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role or title of the risk owner (e.g., Project Scheduler, Construction Manager).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used to rank the risk for mitigation planning.']
  probability_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the likelihood that the risk will materialize.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Chosen strategy for responding to the risk.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk based on its origin or domain.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the schedule risk, including cause and context.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days the schedule could be delayed if the risk occurs.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score combining probability and schedule impact to prioritize risks.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the risk originated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primavera|sap|procore|bim360|viewpoint|hcsh|aconex|salesforce|successfactors|intelex — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the risk in the source system.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the schedule risk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk record.']

  Note: 'Schedule risk register entry identifying threats and opportunities that may impact project schedule performance. Captures risk ID, risk title, risk description, risk category (design, procurement, construction, external, weather, regulatory), probability rating, schedule impact (days), risk score, risk status (identified, assessed, mitigated, closed, realized), risk owner, mitigation plan, contingency reserve days, Monte Carlo simulation P50/P80/P85 completion date outputs, and risk response type (avoid, mitigate, transfer, accept). Supports quantitative schedule risk analysis (QSRA) and schedule contingency management.'
}

Table quality.itp {
  itp_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Inspection and Test Plan record. Primary key for the ITP entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Contract‑Based QA: ITPs are defined per contract to ensure compliance; linking to agreement enables contract‑level audit and performance tracking.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this ITP applies. Links the quality control framework to the parent project.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Required for Contract Responsibility Report linking ITP contractor to subcontractor record for compliance and billing.']
  permit_to_work_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.permit_to_work. Business justification: ITP execution is performed under a Permit‑to‑Work; linking ensures the ITP is authorized and aligns with safety permitting processes.']
  qc_inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: ITP approval workflow requires tracking the internal QC inspector responsible for the plan, enabling accountability and audit trails.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Subcontractor ITP: Subcontractors submit ITPs under a specific subcontract; FK ties ITP to the subcontract for responsibility and schedule management.']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: ITPs are created from specific technical specifications; FK ensures correct acceptance criteria.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed acceptance criteria and tolerances that must be met for the work to pass inspection. Defines the pass/fail thresholds for quality verification.']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of applicable quality, design, and construction standards governing this ITP (e.g., \'ISO 9001, ACI 318, ASME B31.3, IBC 2018\'). Defines the regulatory and technical framework.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the ITP was formally approved by the client or authorized party. Marks the effective date for use in quality control.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the ITP document in the quality management workflow. Governs whether the ITP can be used for active inspections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|superseded|obsolete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role who granted final approval for the ITP document, authorizing its use for quality control activities.']
  client_inspector_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the client or third-party inspector responsible for witnessing or reviewing inspections per this ITP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ITP record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data governance.']
  defect_liability_period_days varchar [note: 'Duration in days of the defects liability period applicable to work covered by this ITP. Defines the warranty period for quality defects.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline or trade to which this ITP applies. Enables filtering and assignment of ITPs by technical specialty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|hvac|instrumentation|piping|architectural|geotechnical — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the ITP document and related inspection records are stored (e.g., Aconex folder path, SharePoint URL, physical file reference).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this ITP becomes effective and must be applied to the specified work activities. May differ from approval date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this ITP expires or is superseded by a new revision. Nullable for ITPs that remain valid until explicitly superseded.']
  fat_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Factory Acceptance Test is required as part of this ITP. Applicable to equipment and prefabricated components.']
  hold_point_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ITP includes mandatory hold points where work cannot proceed until inspection approval is obtained. Critical for compliance and risk management.']
  inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency or sampling rate for inspections defined in this ITP (e.g., \'100% inspection\', \'Every 10th unit\', \'Daily\', \'Per shift\'). Defines the intensity of quality control.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed scope of inspection activities covered by this ITP, including specific items, locations, and phases to be inspected.']
  itp_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the ITP document, typically following a project-specific numbering convention. Used for external reference and document control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ITP record was last modified in the system. Tracks the most recent update for audit and version control purposes.']
  material_test_certificate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether material test certificates must be submitted and verified as part of this ITP. Common for structural materials and critical components.']
  ndt_method_required varchar [note: 'Type of non-destructive testing method required by this ITP, if applicable. Critical for structural integrity verification in construction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|visual|ultrasonic|radiographic|magnetic_particle|liquid_penetrant|eddy_current — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role who prepared the ITP document. Part of the document control and accountability trail.']
  qc_inspector_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the contractor QC inspector responsible for verifying compliance with this ITP before submission to the client.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, notes, or special instructions related to this ITP. Captures context or clarifications not covered in other fields.']
  review_point_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ITP includes review points where inspection records and test results must be submitted for review by the client or engineer.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role who reviewed the ITP document for technical accuracy and completeness before submission for approval.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the current revision of the ITP document. Used for version control and ensuring the latest approved version is in use.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision number or letter of the ITP document, tracking version history and changes. Critical for document control and traceability.']
  sat_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Site Acceptance Test is required as part of this ITP. Applicable to installed equipment and systems commissioning.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard test methods or procedures to be used for testing under this ITP (e.g., \'ASTM E165\', \'ISO 9712\', \'ACI 318 Section 5.6\').']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the ITP defining the scope of work or activity being inspected (e.g., \'Structural Steel Welding ITP\', \'Concrete Pour ITP - Foundation Slab\').']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical code identifying the work package or activity within the project WBS that this ITP governs. Enables traceability from quality plans to project scope.']
  witness_point_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ITP includes witness points where the client or third-party inspector must be notified and given the opportunity to witness the inspection.']
  work_package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the construction work package, activity, or deliverable that this ITP covers. Defines the scope of inspection and testing.']

  Note: 'Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) master record defining the structured quality control framework for a construction work package or activity. Captures the inspection scope, hold/witness/review points, applicable standards (ISO 9001, IBC, ACI), responsible parties, acceptance criteria, and linkage to the WBS. Serves as the authoritative QA/QC planning document governing all inspection activities on a project.'
}

Table quality.itp_line {
  itp_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ITP line item. Primary key for the ITP line entity.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: ITP line execution is assigned to a specific worker; required for execution accountability.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Needed for detailed ITP line responsibility tracking; ties each activity to the subcontractor performing the work.']
  itp_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ITP document that contains this line item. Links the line to its header ITP.']
  responsible_crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: ITP line often executed by a crew; linking crew provides crew‑level accountability.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Each ITP line item is associated with the WBS element where the activity occurs.']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: ITP lines are scoped to WBS nodes; linking via wbs_node_id replaces denormalized wbs_code for accurate WBS‑based inspection planning.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'The specific criteria, tolerances, or standards that must be met for the inspection or test to pass. Defines what constitutes acceptable quality.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific inspection or test activity to be performed at this hold point, witness point, or review point.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'The industry standard, code, or specification that governs this inspection activity (e.g., ASTM, ASME, ACI, AISC, BS, EN, ISO standard reference).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality manager, project manager, or authorized person who approved this ITP line item for use.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this ITP line item was formally approved for implementation.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test equipment used for this inspection must have valid calibration certificates before use.']
  client_witness_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client or their representative must be present to witness this inspection activity before work can proceed.']
  consultant_witness_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consultant or engineer must be present to witness this inspection activity before work can proceed.']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'The cost code used for tracking inspection and quality control costs associated with this ITP line item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ITP line item record was first created in the system.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection activity is on the project critical path and delays would impact overall project schedule.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this ITP line item version becomes effective and must be followed for inspections.']
  environmental_conditions varchar [note: 'Any specific environmental conditions or constraints that must be met during inspection (e.g., temperature range, humidity limits, weather restrictions, lighting requirements).']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated time in hours required to complete this inspection or test activity, including setup, execution, and documentation.']
  hold_point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection point. Hold: work cannot proceed without approval. Witness: client/consultant must be present. Review: documentation review only. Surveillance: periodic monitoring.']
  inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'How often or at what stage the inspection must be performed (e.g., 100%, first and last, every 10th unit, per batch, continuous).']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'The method or technique to be used for performing the inspection or test (e.g., visual inspection, ultrasonic testing, dimensional check, pressure test, material sampling).']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'The construction or manufacturing stage at which this inspection must be performed (e.g., pre-pour, during installation, post-weld, before backfill, at completion).']
  itp_line_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this ITP line item in its lifecycle. Planned: not yet ready. Ready: prerequisites met. In Progress: inspection underway. Completed: inspection finished. Waived: inspection waived by authority. Cancelled: no longer required.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent ITP document. Defines the order of inspection activities.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is mandatory per contract requirements or regulatory compliance, or if it is optional/recommended.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ITP line item record was last modified or updated.']
  ncr_trigger_criteria varchar [note: 'The specific conditions or findings that would trigger the issuance of a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) if this inspection fails.']
  notification_lead_time_hours varchar [note: 'The minimum advance notice in hours that must be given to client, consultant, or third-party witnesses before performing this inspection.']
  reference_document varchar [note: 'The specification, drawing, standard, or procedure document that governs this inspection activity (e.g., drawing number, specification code, industry standard reference).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to this inspection line item that do not fit in other structured fields.']
  required_documentation varchar [note: 'List of documents, certificates, or records that must be produced as evidence of inspection completion (e.g., test certificates, inspection reports, material certificates, calibration records).']
  responsible_discipline varchar [note: 'The engineering or construction discipline responsible for performing or coordinating this inspection (e.g., Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Piping, QA/QC, MEP).']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'The revision number of this ITP line item. Increments when inspection requirements, acceptance criteria, or other details are changed.']
  safety_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific health, safety, and environment (HSE) requirements, personal protective equipment (PPE), or permits required for performing this inspection activity.']
  sampling_plan varchar [note: 'The statistical sampling plan or sample size requirements for this inspection activity (e.g., AQL levels, sample quantity, sampling method).']
  sequence_dependency varchar [note: 'Description of any prerequisite inspections or activities that must be completed before this inspection can be performed.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'The date on which this ITP line item version was superseded by a newer revision. Null if this is the current active version.']
  test_equipment_required varchar [note: 'List of specialized test equipment, instruments, or tools required to perform this inspection or test (e.g., ultrasonic tester, pressure gauge, theodolite, concrete slump cone).']
  third_party_witness_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a third-party inspector, certification body, or regulatory authority must witness this inspection activity.']

  Note: 'Individual inspection or test activity line item within an ITP. Defines a specific hold point, witness point, or review point with its activity description, inspection method, acceptance criteria, frequency, responsible discipline, and required documentation. Each line drives a discrete inspection event on site.'
}

Table quality.inspection {
  inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection record. Primary key.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Inspection schedule report ties each inspection to the specific scheduled activity it validates; activity_id provides that link.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Inspection reports are contract‑driven; linking to agreement allows traceability of inspections to contractual obligations.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Equipment inspection process records which specific asset (e.g., crane, excavator) was inspected; required for compliance reports and maintenance scheduling.']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized inspection checklist template used for this inspection. Defines the verification criteria and check items.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Inspection requests originate from a client account; required for client‑billing and inspection tracking reports.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Client contact may attend or request the inspection; needed for audit of client participation.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit‑mandated inspections: inspections are scheduled to satisfy conditions of an active permit, linking inspection records to the governing permit.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project under which this inspection is performed.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Inspection logs which craft worker performed the inspection; required for QC audit reports.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Inspections are scheduled for specific crews; needed for crew‑based inspection planning.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Inspection reports must reference the exact drawing inspected for traceability and compliance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified inspector or quality control engineer who conducted the inspection. Primary party responsible for verification.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Required for Cost Allocation Report: each inspection activity is charged to a cost code for budgeting and client billing.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Inspection witness verification requires linking the subcontractor representative to the inspection record for audit trails.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Needed to link inspection results to the goods receipt that triggered the inspection, supporting receipt‑inspection traceability.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: When a quality inspection uncovers a safety hazard, an incident is logged; linking inspection to incident supports immediate corrective action and compliance documentation.']
  itp_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.itp_line. Business justification: Link inspection to its ITP line for proper hierarchy; inspection may occur multiple times per line.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for Inspection Report linking each inspection to its purchase order, enabling PO‑based compliance tracking.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Inspections are scheduled against specific WBS elements (e.g., structural components); FK supports inspection‑WBS mapping.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, photos, measurements, or other digital attachments linked to this inspection record. Provides evidence trail for QA/QC compliance.']
  checklist_template_name varchar [note: 'Name of the checklist template used (e.g., Concrete Pour Inspection Checklist, Structural Steel Welding Inspection, MEP Rough-in Checklist). Provides human-readable context.']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the checklist template used at the time of inspection. Ensures audit trail of which criteria were applied.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective action is required to address findings from this inspection. True indicates action needed; false indicates no action required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the quality management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  defects_identified varchar [note: 'Description of specific defects, non-conformances, or quality issues identified during the inspection. Forms the basis for NCR (Non-Conformance Report) creation and defect tracking through DLP (Defects Liability Period).']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the inspection activity was completed and all checklist items verified.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of inspection. Relevant for coating, painting, and other moisture-sensitive quality checks.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical inspection was conducted. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique inspection reference number used in documentation, reports, and correspondence. Typically follows project or organizational numbering conventions.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection. Scheduled indicates planned but not started; in progress indicates active inspection; pass indicates all checks met; fail indicates non-conformance detected; conditional pass indicates minor issues with conditions; cancelled indicates inspection not performed; deferred indicates postponed to later date. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_progress|pass|fail|conditional_pass|cancelled|deferred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection activity. Hold point requires mandatory approval before proceeding; witness point allows client/consultant observation; surveillance is routine monitoring; pre-pour checks concrete readiness; structural verifies load-bearing elements; MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) inspects building systems; material test validates material certificates; weld inspects welding quality; FAT (Factory Acceptance Test) is factory verification; SAT (Site Acceptance Test) is on-site verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hold_point|witness_point|surveillance|pre_pour|structural|mep|material_test|weld|fat|sat — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspector_certification varchar [note: 'Professional certification or qualification held by the inspector (e.g., AWS CWI for welding, ACI for concrete, ASNT for NDT). Validates inspector competency.']
  inspector_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspector\'s digital signature was captured as part of the inspection sign-off. True indicates signature obtained; false indicates signature pending or not required.']
  items_failed varchar [note: 'Count of checklist items that did not meet acceptance criteria and failed verification. Typically triggers NCR (Non-Conformance Report) creation.']
  items_not_applicable varchar [note: 'Count of checklist items marked as not applicable to this specific inspection instance due to scope or conditions.']
  items_passed varchar [note: 'Count of checklist items that met acceptance criteria and passed verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this inspection record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific location where inspection was performed (e.g., Building A Level 3 Column Grid C5, Fabrication Shop Bay 2, Concrete Lab). Provides spatial context for traceability.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of where the inspection was physically conducted. Site indicates construction site; factory indicates manufacturer facility for FAT; workshop indicates fabrication shop; laboratory indicates testing facility; warehouse indicates storage location; offsite indicates other external location.']
  ncr_raised boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was raised as a result of this inspection. True indicates NCR created; false indicates no NCR required.']
  ncr_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the NCR (Non-Conformance Report) raised as a result of this inspection, if applicable. Links inspection to corrective action workflow.']
  observations varchar [note: 'General observations, notes, and comments recorded by the inspector during the inspection. Captures qualitative findings, context, and professional judgment.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Final verdict of the inspection based on aggregated check item results. Pass indicates all critical items met; fail indicates non-conformance requiring corrective action; conditional pass indicates minor issues with conditions for acceptance.']
  photo_count varchar [note: 'Number of photographs captured during the inspection as visual evidence of conditions, defects, or compliance.']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up reinspection to verify corrective actions have been implemented and defects resolved.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a follow-up reinspection is required after corrective actions are completed. True indicates reinspection needed; false indicates no reinspection required.']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical specification, standard, or code against which the inspection was performed (e.g., ACI 318, AISC 360, project specification section). Defines acceptance criteria.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the inspection activity commenced on site or at the facility.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of inspection. Critical for temperature-sensitive activities such as concrete curing, welding, coating application.']
  total_check_items varchar [note: 'Total number of individual verification items in the checklist that were evaluated during this inspection.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of inspection (e.g., clear, rainy, windy, temperature). Relevant for outdoor inspections where weather may affect quality or inspection validity.']
  witness_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the witness party\'s digital signature was captured. True indicates signature obtained; false indicates signature pending or not required.']
  work_package_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the work package, activity, or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element being inspected. Links quality verification to project schedule and scope.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a physical quality inspection or checklist verification event conducted on site, at a factory, or workshop. Captures inspection date, location, inspector identity, ITP line reference, checklist template used, item-level verification results (pass/fail/N/A per check item with remarks), overall outcome (pass/fail/conditional), observations, non-conformances raised, digital sign-off, and attachments (photos, measurements). Covers all inspection types: hold-point inspections, witness-point inspections, pre-pour checks, structural inspections, MEP inspections, and any checklist-based quality verification. Each record serves as the auditable evidence trail for QA/QC compliance and may trigger NCR creation on failure.'
}

Table quality.ncr {
  ncr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the non-conformance report. Primary key for the NCR entity.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: NCR impact analysis requires knowing which activity triggered the non‑conformance; activity_id records that relationship.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: NCRs affect contract performance and liquidated damages; contract reference is required for claim and settlement processes.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: NCRs are tracked against the client account for contract compliance and client‑specific reporting.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Clients can raise non‑conformances; linking to the reporting client contact enables traceability.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project where the non-conformance was identified.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: NCR assigns a specific worker to resolve the issue; needed for corrective‑action tracking.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: NCRs are raised against a specific drawing; linking enables automated impact analysis.']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: ESG reports require aggregation of all NCRs for compliance; linking each NCR to its ESG report enables traceability and audit of non‑conformances impacting sustainability metrics.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Needed for Cost Impact Tracking: NCR corrective cost is allocated to a cost code to reflect financial impact in cost reports.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: NCR responsibility assignment report must reference the subcontractor accountable for the non‑conformance.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Required for traceability: NCR investigations often trigger safety incident investigations; linking enables root‑cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Permit Condition Violation NCR tracking: each NCR is raised when a specific permit condition is breached, required for compliance reporting.']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Regulatory NCRs often cite the violated technical specification; direct FK supports compliance reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Assigns each NCR to the responsible vendor, essential for vendor performance evaluation and corrective action.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific work package or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element affected by the non-conformance.']
  client_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the client was formally notified of the non-conformance. Null if client notification was not required.']
  client_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client must be formally notified of this non-conformance per contract requirements. Typically true for major or critical NCRs.']
  closed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who authorized closure of the NCR. Typically a QA/QC manager or project quality manager.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the NCR was formally closed after successful completion of corrective action, verification, and effectiveness review. Marks the end of the CAPA cycle.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective action was completed. Compared against target date for performance measurement.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action(s) to be taken to address the immediate non-conformance. Part of the CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process.']
  corrective_action_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or organization responsible for implementing the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_target_date date [note: 'Planned target date for completion of the corrective action. Used for tracking and escalation if deadlines are missed.']
  cost_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost impact (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NCR record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or construction discipline to which the non-conformance relates. Used for categorization and routing to appropriate technical specialists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|architectural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|hvac|instrumentation|piping|welding|painting|insulation|fireproofing|other — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Formal decision on how the non-conforming work or material will be handled. Disposition determines the corrective action path and may require client approval for concessions.']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the disposition decision. Typically a senior QA/QC manager, project manager, or client representative.']
  disposition_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the disposition was formally approved, allowing corrective action to proceed.']
  disposition_justification varchar [note: 'Technical and business justification for the selected disposition, including engineering analysis, code compliance assessment, and impact on functionality.']
  effectiveness_review_comments varchar [note: 'Comments from the effectiveness review assessing whether the corrective and preventive actions have successfully addressed the non-conformance and prevented recurrence.']
  estimated_cost_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost impact of the non-conformance including rework, material replacement, schedule delay, and potential liquidated damages. Used for cost tracking and recovery.']
  hold_release_date date [note: 'Date when the hold was released, allowing work to resume. Null if no hold was placed or if hold is still active.']
  hold_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a hold has been placed on the affected work or material, preventing further work until the NCR is resolved. True if hold is active, False otherwise.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date when the non-conformance was first discovered or identified on site. Represents the business event timestamp for the NCR initiation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NCR record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking throughout the CAPA lifecycle.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the physical location where the non-conformance was identified (e.g., Building A Level 3, Grid Line E5, Foundation Block 12).']
  ncr_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of non-conformance to support trend analysis and root cause categorization.']
  ncr_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance, including what was observed, what was expected per specifications, and the extent of the deviation.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the NCR, typically following a project-specific numbering convention. Externally visible reference number used in correspondence and documentation.']
  ncr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NCR tracking its progression from identification through closure. Reflects the workflow state in the CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) cycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|under_investigation|pending_disposition|corrective_action_in_progress|verification_pending|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of preventive actions to be taken to prevent recurrence of similar non-conformances. Addresses systemic issues identified in root cause analysis.']
  preventive_action_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or organization responsible for implementing the preventive action.']
  quantity_affected decimal(18,3) [note: 'Numeric quantity of work, material, or units affected by the non-conformance (e.g., 150 cubic meters of concrete, 25 welds, 500 linear meters of pipe).']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who identified and reported the non-conformance. Typically a QA/QC inspector, site engineer, or subcontractor representative.']
  reported_by_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company that the reporting person represents (e.g., General Contractor, Subcontractor, Client, Third-Party Inspector).']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of the underlying root cause(s) of the non-conformance. May reference techniques such as 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, or Fault Tree Analysis.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days of schedule delay caused by the non-conformance and its resolution. Used for schedule recovery planning and EOT (Extension of Time) claims.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification indicating the impact of the non-conformance on safety, quality, schedule, and cost. Critical NCRs may trigger work stoppage.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity affected (e.g., m3, kg, linear meter, each, square meter).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the verification of corrective action effectiveness was performed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was effective and the non-conformance has been resolved. May include re-inspection, testing, or document review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual_inspection|dimensional_check|material_test|functional_test|document_review|third_party_inspection|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  verification_performed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who performed the verification of corrective action effectiveness. Typically a QA/QC inspector or independent verifier.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification activity indicating whether the corrective action was effective and the non-conformance is resolved.']

  Note: 'Non-Conformance Report (NCR) and corrective action record capturing formal documentation of construction deliverables, materials, or workmanship that do not meet specified requirements. Tracks NCR number, description of non-conformance, affected work package, root cause analysis, disposition (accept-as-is, rework, reject, concession), corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) with responsible parties, target and actual completion dates, verification method, effectiveness review, and closure status. Supports hold/release workflow and the full CAPA cycle required under ISO 9001 clause 10.'
}

Table quality.corrective_action {
  corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for corrective_action']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Corrective action records the worker responsible for implementing the action; needed for audit trails.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Allows budgeting of corrective actions: each corrective_action record must be linked to the cost code under which its expense is recorded.']
  linked_change_order_project_change_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Change Order (CO) generated as a result of this corrective action, if cost or schedule impacts require contract modification.']
  linked_document_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Request for Information (RFI) issued to clarify design or specification ambiguities related to this corrective action.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Non-Conformance Report that triggered this corrective or preventive action.']
  primary_corrective_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee, subcontractor, or organizational unit assigned to execute this corrective action.']
  quaternary_corrective_last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this corrective action record.']
  tertiary_corrective_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this corrective action record.']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the specific remediation steps to be taken, including scope, method, materials, and acceptance criteria.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the corrective or preventive action, typically formatted as NCR-XXXX-CA-YY for traceability.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the corrective action: open (assigned but not started), in_progress (work underway), pending_verification (awaiting QA/QC review), verified (effectiveness confirmed), closed (completed and accepted), cancelled (no longer required).']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action: corrective (addresses existing defect), preventive (prevents recurrence), containment (immediate isolation), or interim (temporary measure pending permanent fix).']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was actually completed and submitted for verification. Null if still in progress.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred to implement the corrective action, captured from job costing and procurement records.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was formally assigned to the responsible party.']
  client_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the client formally approved the corrective action plan. Null if client approval is not required or pending.']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to implement the corrective action, including labor, materials, equipment, and any rework or schedule impact costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corrective action record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation such as inspection reports, test certificates, photographs, or revised drawings stored in the document management system (e.g., Aconex, BIM 360).']
  effectiveness_review_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed comments from the effectiveness review, including evidence of resolution, lessons learned, and recommendations for process improvement.']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of the corrective action was formally reviewed, typically 30-90 days after implementation.']
  effectiveness_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness review: effective (action resolved the issue and prevented recurrence), partially_effective (issue partially resolved, additional action required), ineffective (action did not resolve the issue), pending_review (awaiting final assessment).']
  is_systemic_issue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the root cause analysis identified this as a systemic issue requiring organization-wide corrective action across multiple projects or processes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corrective action record was last updated.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of lessons learned from this corrective action, including process improvements, training needs, and recommendations for future projects.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the corrective action based on safety impact, schedule criticality, and cost exposure: critical (immediate action required), high (urgent), medium (standard timeline), low (routine).']
  recurrence_prevention_measures varchar [note: 'Specific measures implemented to prevent recurrence of the non-conformance, such as updated work instructions, additional inspections, or supplier quality audits.']
  requires_client_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action requires formal approval from the client or project owner before implementation.']
  requires_design_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action requires a formal design change, triggering BIM (Building Information Modeling) revision and engineering approval workflows.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause investigation findings that informed this corrective action, often using 5-Why, Fishbone, or Fault Tree Analysis methods.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the corrective action delayed the project schedule or affected the critical path.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action must be completed to meet project schedule and compliance requirements.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was verified by QA/QC personnel or the designated authority.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the effectiveness of the corrective action: inspection (visual check), testing (material or functional test), document_review (record verification), audit (formal QA/QC audit), site_observation (field walkdown), measurement (dimensional or performance measurement).']
  verified_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the verification of the corrective action.']

  Note: 'Corrective and preventive action record linked to an NCR. Tracks the specific remediation steps assigned, responsible party, target completion date, actual completion date, verification method, and effectiveness review outcome. Supports the CAPA (Corrective Action / Preventive Action) cycle required under ISO 9001 clause 10.'
}

Table quality.checklist {
  checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality inspection checklist template. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract that mandates or governs the use of this checklist, linking to contract-specific quality requirements.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this checklist template was created or customized, if project-specific.']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Permit condition compliance checklist: checklists verify that each permit condition is met during construction activities.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Checklist scope is defined per WBS element for quality control; linking enables WBS‑level checklist tracking.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Overall acceptance criteria for the checklist (e.g., \'All critical items must pass\', \'95% of items must pass with no critical failures\').']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the construction activity type that this checklist applies to, aligned with WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) and ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concrete_pour|rebar_placement|formwork_erection|waterproofing|structural_steel_erection|welding|mechanical_installation|electrical_installation|piping_installation|excavation|backfill|piling|painting|insulation|commissioning|other — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing technical standard, code, or specification that this checklist enforces (e.g., \'ACI 318\', \'AISC 360\', \'ASME B31.3\', \'IBC 2018\', \'ASTM C39\').']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist template in the document control workflow, indicating whether it is ready for use in field inspections.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) manager or authorized person who approved this checklist template for use.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the checklist template was formally approved for use in quality inspections.']
  average_pass_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average pass rate (percentage) for inspections conducted using this checklist, used to identify problematic activities or checklist items.']
  checklist_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the checklist template, used for reference in inspection documentation and ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) workflows.']
  checklist_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the checklist purpose, scope, and applicability, including any special instructions or prerequisites for use.']
  checklist_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the checklist template describing the inspection scope (e.g., \'Concrete Pour Inspection\', \'Rebar Placement Verification\', \'Waterproofing Application Check\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template record was first created in the system.']
  critical_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of check items in this checklist that are classified as critical and must pass for overall acceptance.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or construction discipline that this checklist is associated with, used for routing to appropriate QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) personnel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|architectural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|hvac|instrumentation|piping|geotechnical|environmental|multi_discipline — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this checklist template version becomes effective and should be used for new inspections.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this checklist template version remains effective, after which it is superseded by a newer revision.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to complete the inspection using this checklist, used for resource planning and scheduling.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger for when this checklist should be used (e.g., per concrete pour, daily for ongoing work, per material batch). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_occurrence|daily|weekly|monthly|per_batch|per_lot|as_required — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_point_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist represents a hold point where work cannot proceed until inspection is completed and accepted.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Phase of construction activity when this checklist should be applied, aligned with ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) hold points and witness points.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection method that this checklist governs (e.g., visual inspection, NDT, dimensional verification, functional testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|dimensional|non_destructive_testing|destructive_testing|functional|performance|documentation_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist is mandatory for the associated activity type per contract requirements, ITP (Inspection and Test Plan), or regulatory compliance.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who last modified this checklist template.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template record was last modified.']
  ncr_trigger_threshold varchar [note: 'Criteria that automatically trigger the issuance of an NCR (Non-Conformance Report) when inspection results fail to meet this threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this checklist template.']
  reference_documents varchar [note: 'List of supporting documents, drawings, specifications, or procedures that inspectors should reference when using this checklist.']
  required_equipment varchar [note: 'List of tools, instruments, or equipment required to perform the inspection per this checklist (e.g., \'tape measure, level, ultrasonic thickness gauge, calibrated thermometer\').']
  required_qualifications varchar [note: 'Qualifications, certifications, or competencies required for the inspector who will use this checklist (e.g., \'AWS CWI\', \'ACI Level 1\', \'ASNT NDT Level II\').']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the checklist was issued, used to ensure inspectors are using the latest approved version.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier for the checklist template, incremented when checklist content is updated to reflect specification changes or lessons learned.']
  safety_requirements varchar [note: 'HSE (Health Safety and Environment) requirements and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) needed when performing inspections using this checklist.']
  total_check_items varchar [note: 'Total number of individual verification items or questions included in this checklist template.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this checklist template has been used in actual field inspections, used for analytics and continuous improvement.']
  witness_point_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist represents a witness point where the client or third-party inspector must be notified and given opportunity to witness the inspection.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who originally created this checklist template.']

  Note: 'Quality inspection checklist template defining the structured set of verification items for a specific construction activity type (e.g., concrete pour, rebar placement, waterproofing, structural steel erection). Captures checklist name, revision, activity type, applicable standard, approval status, and ordered check items with acceptance criteria and inspection method. Used as the governing template when conducting inspections — each inspection references a checklist to ensure consistent verification coverage.'
}

Table quality.checklist_execution {
  checklist_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the completed checklist execution instance. Primary key for this quality inspection record.']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master checklist template that was executed. Links to the standard ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) or inspection checklist definition.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project under which this quality inspection was performed.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or subcontractor whose work is being inspected.']
  permit_to_work_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.permit_to_work. Business justification: Checklist execution on site requires a Permit‑to‑Work; linking records the permit governing the execution for safety compliance.']
  primary_checklist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified QA/QC inspector who performed the checklist execution. Must be a certified quality professional.']
  site_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction site or facility where the inspection took place.']
  tertiary_checklist_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA/QC manager or authorized person who approved the checklist execution results.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Execution of a checklist is performed against a specific WBS element on site.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the checklist execution: draft (in progress), submitted (awaiting review), approved (accepted), rejected (not accepted), or revision required.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist execution was formally approved by the QA/QC manager.']
  compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of checklist items that passed inspection, excluding N/A items. Formula: (pass_count / (total_items - na_count)) * 100.']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions or remediation work required to address failed inspection items.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist execution record was first created in the system.']
  critical_failure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any critical or safety-related inspection items failed, requiring immediate stop-work or remediation.']
  defects_identified varchar [note: 'Summary description of defects, non-conformances, or quality issues identified during the inspection.']
  document_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this checklist execution to the project document management system for archival and retrieval.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the quality inspection checklist was executed on site.']
  execution_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspector completed the checklist execution and submitted the results.']
  execution_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for this checklist execution, often formatted as project-discipline-sequence (e.g., PRJ001-QC-0123).']
  execution_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspector began the checklist execution on site.']
  fail_count varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items that failed inspection criteria and require corrective action.']
  humidity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of inspection, relevant for coating, painting, and material testing activities.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Project lifecycle stage when the inspection was conducted: pre-construction, during construction, post-construction, commissioning, or DLP (Defects Liability Period).']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of quality inspection performed: ITP (Inspection and Test Plan), FAT (Factory Acceptance Test), SAT (Site Acceptance Test), material test, weld inspection, concrete test, structural inspection, MEP (Mechanical Electrical Plumbing) inspection, final inspection, or defect inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITP|FAT|SAT|material_test|weld_inspection|concrete_test|structural_inspection|MEP_inspection|final_inspection|defect_inspection — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector who executed the checklist for display and reporting purposes.']
  inspector_remarks varchar [note: 'General observations, comments, or notes recorded by the inspector during the checklist execution. Provides context for inspection results.']
  inspector_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspector\'s digital signature was captured as part of the checklist sign-off process.']
  inspector_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspector digitally signed off on the completed checklist execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist execution record was last updated or modified.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Physical location on site where the inspection was performed (e.g., \'Building A - Level 2 - Grid C3\', \'North Wing Foundation\', \'Substation Area\').']
  na_count varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items marked as not applicable to this specific inspection instance.']
  ncr_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection resulted in the generation of one or more NCRs (Non-Conformance Reports) for failed items.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the checklist execution: pass (all items compliant), fail (one or more critical failures), conditional pass (minor defects requiring remediation), or not applicable.']
  pass_count varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items that passed inspection criteria.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in Celsius at the time of inspection, critical for concrete curing, welding, and coating applications.']
  total_items varchar [note: 'Total number of inspection items in the executed checklist.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of inspection, relevant for outdoor construction activities and material testing (e.g., \'Clear, 25°C\', \'Light rain, 18°C\').']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the witness who co-signed the inspection for display and reporting purposes.']
  witness_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection requires a witness signature from client, consultant, or third-party inspector per contract requirements.']
  witness_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the witness\'s digital signature was captured as part of the checklist sign-off process.']
  witness_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the witness digitally signed off on the completed checklist execution.']
  work_activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific construction work activity or deliverable being inspected (e.g., \'Foundation concrete pour - Grid A1 to A5\', \'HVAC ductwork installation - Level 3\').']

  Note: 'Completed instance of a quality inspection checklist executed on site for a specific work activity. Records the execution date, inspector, work location, WBS reference, each checklist item result (pass/fail/N/A), overall outcome, remarks, and digital sign-off. Provides the auditable evidence trail for QA/QC compliance.'
}

Table quality.test_certificate {
  test_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_certificate']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Material test certificates are contract‑required evidence of compliance; contract link supports audit and payment certification.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project for which the material was tested. Links the certificate to the project quality records.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Material test certificates need a FK to the supplying subcontractor for traceability and warranty management.']
  itp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.itp. Business justification: Test certificates are often issued for ITP items and may be associated with NCRs; linking enables direct lookup.']
  material_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_catalog. Business justification: Links material test certificates to the material master record, enabling traceability of test results to specific catalog items.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: Test certificates may also be linked to NCRs for traceability.']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the physical material sample that was tested. Links the certificate to the specific specimen extracted from the batch or lot.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Sustainable material certification relies on test certificates; the FK ties each certificate to the material record, supporting material compliance verification in sustainability assessments.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Material test certificates are linked to the WBS element using the material, supporting compliance reporting.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accreditation authority that certified the laboratory (e.g., UKAS, A2LA, NABL, NATA).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the test certificate was formally approved and released for use in construction quality records.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the quality manager, engineer, or authorized signatory who reviewed and approved the test certificate.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier batch number identifying the production lot from which the sample was taken. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certificate validity expires, if applicable. Some certificates have time-limited validity for regulatory or contractual reasons.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the certificate was officially issued by the laboratory or supplier. This is the principal business event timestamp for the certificate lifecycle.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique certificate number issued by the testing laboratory or supplier. This is the business identifier printed on the physical or digital certificate document.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test certificate in the quality management workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|superseded|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test certificate indicating the source and nature of testing. MTC (Material Test Certificate) from supplier, laboratory test from independent lab, factory test (FAT - Factory Acceptance Test), site test (SAT - Site Acceptance Test), third-party test from accredited body, or supplier certificate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test certificate record was first created in the quality management system.']
  delivery_lot_number varchar [note: 'Delivery or shipment lot number identifying the specific material consignment from which the sample was taken. Links certificate to goods receipt.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the test certificate document stored in the document management system (e.g., Aconex, BIM 360, Procore).']
  heat_number varchar [note: 'Steel mill heat number for steel and rebar materials, identifying the specific furnace melt. Essential for metallurgical traceability.']
  issuing_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name of the testing laboratory or organization that performed the tests and issued the certificate.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation certificate number issued by the national or international accreditation body (e.g., ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation number). Validates the laboratory\'s competence.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test certificate record was last updated in the quality management system.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material being tested, including grade, specification, and any relevant characteristics (e.g., Grade 60 Rebar, M30 Concrete, AC-20 Bitumen).']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of construction material being tested. Covers primary materials used in infrastructure and building construction projects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concrete|steel|aggregate|bitumen|soil|asphalt|geotextile|cement|rebar|structural_steel|weld|paint|coating — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Overall determination of whether the tested material meets the specification requirements. Conditional pass indicates minor deviations requiring engineering review.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number linking the tested material to the procurement transaction. Enables traceability from test certificate back to supplier contract and delivery.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments from the testing laboratory or quality engineer regarding the test results, sample condition, or special circumstances.']
  sampling_date date [note: 'Date when the material sample was collected from the batch, delivery, or construction site for testing.']
  sampling_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the material sample was collected (e.g., site name, warehouse, delivery truck, production line, grid reference).']
  specification_requirement varchar [note: 'Required values or acceptance criteria per project specification, contract, or standard (e.g., minimum compressive strength 30 MPa, yield strength 420 MPa min).']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory technician or engineer who performed the testing and signed the certificate.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory or field testing was performed on the sample.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Specific testing procedure or method applied (e.g., compression test, tensile test, slump test, sieve analysis, chemical analysis).']
  test_parameters varchar [note: 'List of specific properties or characteristics measured during testing (e.g., compressive strength, yield strength, elongation, gradation, moisture content). Stored as structured text or JSON.']
  test_results varchar [note: 'Measured values and outcomes for each test parameter. Stored as structured text or JSON containing parameter-value pairs with units.']
  test_standard varchar [note: 'Industry or regulatory test standard followed during testing (e.g., ASTM C39, BS EN 12390, AASHTO T22, ISO 6892). Defines the test methodology and acceptance criteria.']
  work_package_code varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) code or work package identifier indicating where the tested material will be used in the project.']

  Note: 'Material test certificate and laboratory test result record capturing certified testing outcomes for construction materials (concrete, steel, aggregates, bitumen, soil, asphalt, geotextiles). Stores certificate number, sample ID, material type, batch/heat number, sampling date and location, test standard (ASTM, BS, EN, AASHTO), test parameters and measured values, pass/fail determination, issuing laboratory, laboratory accreditation number, and traceability to purchase order and delivery lot. Covers both third-party MTC documents received from suppliers and in-house/independent lab test results for site-sampled materials.'
}

Table quality.weld_record {
  weld_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the weld record. Primary key for weld inspection and traceability documentation.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Weld records are subject to contract specifications and warranties; linking to agreement enables contract‑level quality assurance.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Welding records require linking the welding machine (asset) to ensure equipment maintenance, certification, and traceability of weld quality.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Carbon accounting tracks emissions per welding activity; linking weld records to the corresponding carbon emission entry provides granular emission data for reporting.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project under which this weld was performed.']
