  transaction_date date [note: 'The business date on which this retention ledger transaction occurred (withholding or release event).']

  Note: 'Tracks retention (retainage) balances held by clients against the contractor and held by the contractor against subcontractors. Captures retention withheld per billing period, cumulative retention balance, DLP (Defects Liability Period) release schedule, partial release events, and final release upon practical completion certificate. Manages both receivable retention (client owes contractor) and payable retention (contractor owes subcontractor) in a single ledger. Critical for FIDIC and GMP contract financial management.'
}

Table finance.cash_flow_forecast {
  cash_flow_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cash flow forecast record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Required for cash flow forecasting per contract to align financing with project cash needs; contracts define payment terms used in cash flow models.']
  climate_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.climate_risk_assessment. Business justification: Cash‑flow forecasts must incorporate quantified climate‑risk financial impacts for risk‑adjusted planning.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this cash flow forecast is prepared. Null for corporate-level forecasts.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cash flow forecasts are prepared per cost center to track cash impact of cost allocations.']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Cash‑flow forecasts are prepared by a finance analyst; linking to employee enables responsibility tracking and variance analysis.']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Forecasts incorporate cash‑flow impact of specific regulatory changes; linking provides the necessary change reference.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific WBS element if the forecast is prepared at a granular work package level. Null for project-level or corporate-level forecasts.']
  advance_drawdown_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted drawdown of advance payments or mobilization advances from the client, typically secured by advance payment guarantees.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the forecast was formally approved for use in financial planning and decision-making.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the forecast, typically the project manager, finance director, or CFO.']
  bond_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for performance bonds, advance payment guarantees, and other surety instruments required by the contract.']
  bonding_capacity_utilization decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total bonding capacity utilized by this forecast, relevant for corporate-level forecasts to monitor surety capacity constraints.']
  closing_cash_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cash balance at the end of the forecast period, calculated as opening balance plus net cash flow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system.']
  credit_facility_utilization decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available credit facility or working capital line utilized to meet the peak funding requirement, relevant for corporate treasury management.']
  cumulative_cash_position decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative cash position at the end of the forecast period, representing the running total of net cash flows from project inception or corporate fiscal year start.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this forecast (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  equipment_hire_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for equipment rental and hire charges, including plant, machinery, and temporary facilities.']
  forecast_assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying the forecast, including payment term assumptions, progress rate assumptions, inflation factors, and risk contingencies.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast was prepared or last updated, representing the as-of date for the forecast assumptions.']
  forecast_granularity varchar [note: 'The time interval granularity at which cash flows are forecasted: daily for short-term liquidity management, weekly for near-term project cash planning, monthly for standard project forecasting, or quarterly for long-term corporate planning.']
  forecast_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the forecast, typically combining project code, forecast period, and version (e.g., PRJ-2024-001-V3).']
  forecast_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.']
  forecast_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast: draft for work-in-progress, submitted for review, approved for active use, superseded when replaced by a newer version, or archived for historical reference.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast scope: project-level for individual construction projects, corporate-level for enterprise-wide treasury forecasting, WBS-level for granular work package forecasts, or contract-level for specific contract cash management.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the forecast, incremented with each revision to track forecast evolution and variance analysis over time.']
  forecasted_inflow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected cash inflows for the forecast period, including client milestone payments, retention releases, advance drawdowns, and other receipts.']
  forecasted_outflow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected cash outflows for the forecast period, including subcontractor payments, material procurement, payroll, equipment hire, bond premiums, and other disbursements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was last updated, supporting audit trail and version control.']
  material_procurement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for material purchases and procurement, based on Purchase Orders (PO) and supplier payment terms.']
  milestone_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted client milestone payments expected during the period, based on contract payment schedules and progress billing milestones.']
  net_cash_flow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net cash flow for the forecast period, calculated as forecasted inflows minus forecasted outflows, indicating the expected cash position change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or explanations regarding the forecast, including significant changes from prior versions or special circumstances.']
  opening_cash_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash balance at the beginning of the forecast period, serving as the starting point for the period\'s cash flow projection.']
  payroll_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payroll disbursements for direct labor and site personnel, including wages, benefits, and statutory contributions.']
  peak_funding_requirement decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum negative cash position expected during the forecast period, indicating the highest working capital or credit facility draw required.']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team who prepared the forecast, typically the project controls team or corporate treasury analyst.']
  retention_release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted release of retention amounts held by the client, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).']
  s_curve_profile_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification of the cash flow profile shape: front-loaded for projects with high early expenditure, linear for steady cash flow, back-loaded for projects with late-stage heavy spending, or custom for non-standard profiles.']
  subcontractor_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments to subcontractors for work performed, based on subcontract payment terms and progress certificates.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between current and prior forecast, calculated as (current - prior) / prior * 100, indicating forecast volatility.']
  variance_to_prior_forecast decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the current forecast net cash flow and the previous forecast version for the same period, used for forecast accuracy tracking and trend analysis.']
  working_capital_gap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between forecasted outflows and inflows timing, representing the working capital financing need to bridge payment obligations before receipt of client payments.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking cash flow projection record for construction projects and the corporate entity, capturing forecast inflows (client milestone payments, retention releases, advance drawdowns) and outflows (subcontractor payments, material procurement, payroll, equipment hire, bond premiums) by period (weekly/monthly). Includes S-curve cash flow profile, cumulative cash position, peak funding requirement, working capital gap analysis, and variance to prior forecast. Supports both project-level forecasting for contract cash management and corporate-level treasury forecasting for bonding capacity and credit facility utilization.'
}

Table finance.financial_guarantee {
  financial_guarantee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial guarantee record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction contract for which this guarantee is issued or received.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project associated with this financial guarantee.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial guarantees are tied to a cost center for budgeting and exposure tracking.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Guarantee issuance is recorded by a responsible employee; linking supports traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of amendments or modifications made to the original financial guarantee terms.']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party entitled to call or claim against the financial guarantee (typically the client or contractor).']
  beneficiary_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary party: client, contractor, subcontractor, supplier, or joint venture (JV).']
  bonding_capacity_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount by which this guarantee reduces the organization\'s available bonding capacity with the surety.']
  call_conditions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the conditions under which the beneficiary may call or claim against the financial guarantee.']
  call_type varchar [note: 'Type of call mechanism: on-demand (unconditional), conditional (requires proof of default), or documentary (requires specific documents).']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount claimed by the beneficiary against the financial guarantee, if a call was made.']
  claim_date date [note: 'Date on which the beneficiary called or claimed against the financial guarantee, if applicable.']
  collateral_description varchar [note: 'Description of any collateral or security provided to the issuer (e.g., cash deposit, property lien, parent company indemnity).']
  collateral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issuer required collateral or security to issue the guarantee (True) or not (False).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the financial guarantee meets all contractual and regulatory compliance requirements (True) or not (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the financial guarantee record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the face value amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the financial guarantee becomes binding and enforceable.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the financial guarantee expires and is no longer enforceable, unless extended or called prior.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier or document number from the source system or issuing institution.']
  face_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary value of the financial guarantee that can be claimed by the beneficiary.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which guarantee premium expenses or liabilities are posted.']
  guarantee_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the issuing bank or surety for this financial guarantee instrument.']
  guarantee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the financial guarantee: active (in force), expired (past expiry date), called (claim made), released (obligation discharged), cancelled (terminated early), or pending (awaiting issuance).']
  guarantee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial guarantee instrument: performance bond, advance payment guarantee, retention bond, bid bond, parent company guarantee, or letter of credit.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the financial guarantee was issued and became effective.']
  issuer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank, surety company, or parent company that issued the financial guarantee.']
  issuer_type varchar [note: 'Type of institution issuing the guarantee: bank, surety company, parent company, or insurance company.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the financial guarantee.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of the person who last modified the financial guarantee record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the financial guarantee record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the financial guarantee.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total premium or fee paid to the issuer for providing the financial guarantee.']
  premium_payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which premium payments are made: one-time, annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly.']
  premium_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual premium rate expressed as a percentage of the face value amount.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the financial guarantee was formally released and the obligation discharged.']
  risk_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current financial risk exposure represented by this guarantee, used for financial risk reporting and management.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this financial guarantee data originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista).']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element code linking the guarantee to a specific project phase or deliverable.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of the person who created the financial guarantee record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all financial instruments and guarantees issued or received in connection with construction contracts — performance bonds, advance payment guarantees, retention bonds, bid bonds, parent company guarantees, and letters of credit. Captures instrument type, issuing bank/surety, beneficiary, face value, currency, issue date, expiry date, call conditions, amendment history, premium/fee schedule, and current status (active/expired/called/released). Critical for bonding capacity management, surety relationship tracking, FIDIC/EPC contract compliance, and financial risk exposure reporting.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for profit_center']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on profit_center (parent_profit_center_id)']
  actual_profit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual profit realized by the profit center.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the profit center\'s physical address.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the profit center for the fiscal period.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the profit center\'s primary office.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated cost center code used for internal cost allocation.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Direct costs attributable to the profit center\'s revenue.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the profit center\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the profit center\'s purpose and scope.']
  ebit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Earnings before interest and taxes for the profit center.']
  ebitda decimal(18,2) [note: 'Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the profit center.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the profit center became effective for reporting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the profit center ceased to be effective; null if still active.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center in external ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Viewpoint).']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for the profit center\'s financial reporting.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center\'s results are consolidated into higher‑level reporting.']
  last_reported_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial report for the profit center.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the profit center.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number for the profit center.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the profit center\'s address.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'Business category classification of the profit center.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to identify the profit center in external systems.']
  profit_center_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the profit center within the organization.']
  profit_center_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the manager responsible for the profit center.']
  profit_center_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the owner or sponsor of the profit center.']
  profit_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Profit margin expressed as a percentage of revenue.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the profit center operates.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the profit center reports financial results.']
  revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the profit center.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the profit center\'s earnings.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of profit center indicating its organizational role.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profit center record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS code linking the profit center to project budgeting structures.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for profit_center. Referenced by profit_center_id.'
}

Table finance.billing_period {
  billing_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_period']
  prior_billing_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_period (prior_billing_period_id)']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any post‑close adjustments to the period.']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code that groups periods into a billing cycle (e.g., monthly cycle code).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing period record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for amounts in this period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description of the billing period.']
  due_date date [note: 'Standard payment due date for invoices generated in this period.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the period definition is effective for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the period definition remains valid (null for open‑ended).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter number (1‑4) for the billing period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the billing period belongs.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period has been adjusted after initial posting.']
  is_provisional boolean [note: 'Indicates if the period is provisional (e.g., before final approval).']
  payment_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due for this period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the billing period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the billing period (e.g., “2024‑01”, “Q1‑2024”).']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the period within the fiscal year.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the billing period.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing period length.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_period. Referenced by billing_period_id.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for company_code']
  parent_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on company_code (parent_company_code_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the company location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business area classification for reporting.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts used by the entity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the company is located.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category describing the legal relationship of the entity within the corporate group.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the legal entity for accounting and reporting.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the entity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the company location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the company code record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for reporting for this company.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the company\'s primary activities and scope.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date the entity was legally dissolved, if applicable.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the company code became effective for accounting purposes.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the company code ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partner or regulatory systems to reference this entity.']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting framework applied to the entity\'s financial statements.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the entity was legally incorporated.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (e.g., NAICS) describing the primary business activity.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is included in group financial consolidation.']
  legal_form varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the entity.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company entity.']
  parent_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the immediate parent legal entity, if any.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the company address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary person to contact for the company.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center to which the entity reports.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the entity belongs for strategic reporting.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the company location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the company entity.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the legal entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the company code record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for company_code. Referenced by company_code_id.'
}

Table finance.chart_of_accounts {
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for chart_of_accounts']
  parent_chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on chart_of_accounts (parent_chart_of_accounts_id)']
  account_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the depth of the account within the chart hierarchy (e.g., 1 = top‑level, 2 = sub‑account).']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the account as displayed in financial statements and reports.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the account, used in financial postings and reporting.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Category of the account defining its role in the balance sheet or income statement.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the account to a cost center for internal cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the chart of accounts record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the account is denominated.']
  description varchar [note: 'Extended textual description providing context or usage notes for the account.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the account becomes valid for posting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the account is retired or becomes invalid; null if still active.']
  financial_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code that maps the account to external financial reporting standards (e.g., IFRS, GAAP).']
  is_budgetable boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in budgeting and forecasting processes.']
  is_cash_account boolean [note: 'True if the account represents cash or cash equivalents.']
  is_consolidation_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used in corporate consolidation reporting.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for external parties (e.g., vendors, customers).']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account records intercompany transactions.']
  normal_balance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the start of the fiscal period, expressed in the account\'s currency.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the account to a profit center for profitability analysis.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account (e.g., active for posting, inactive for archival).']
  tax_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for tax reporting and compliance purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the chart of accounts record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for chart_of_accounts. Referenced by chart_of_accounts_id.'
}

Table finance.commitment_item {
  commitment_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commitment_item']
  commitment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent commitment (header) to which this line belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commitment items are charged to a cost center; replace the existing code with a proper foreign key.']
  parent_commitment_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on commitment_item (parent_commitment_item_id)']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with this commitment line.']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the resource (e.g., material, equipment, labor) associated with this commitment line.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor or subcontractor linked to this commitment line.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the commitment line before taxes or discounts.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commitment line (e.g., budgeted, actual, forecast, change order).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commitment line record was first created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the commitment line item.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the commitment line becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the commitment line expires or is scheduled to end (nullable if open‑ended).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the commitment.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the resource committed (units defined by unit_of_measure).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commitment line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the commitment line record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element code linking the commitment line to project hierarchy.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for commitment_item. Referenced by commitment_item_id.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payment runs are executed for a specific cost center; replace the string code with a foreign key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who triggered or approved the run.']
  prior_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (prior_payment_run_id)']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the payment run.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or subcontractor receiving the payments.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2024‑03) for financial reporting.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run finished processing.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run actually started processing.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the payment run.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run received final approval.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch identifier used by the banking partner or ERP system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the payment run.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or context of the payment run.']
  error_flag boolean [note: 'True if any payment line in the run failed or was rejected.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error when error_flag is true.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate applied when the run involves foreign currency.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment run was generated automatically by the system.']
  number_of_payments varchar [note: 'Total count of individual payment transactions included in the run.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'System or interface through which the payment run was initiated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payments.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the payment run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the payment run was executed.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the run is scheduled, ad‑hoc, or recurring.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment run was scheduled to occur.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payment run.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage used to calculate total_tax_amount.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all payment line amounts before taxes and deductions.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after taxes and deductions; the amount actually paid out.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applied to the payment run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.commitment {
  commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commitment']
  parent_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on commitment (parent_commitment_id)']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier used for financial reporting.']
  amount_committed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value pledged by the commitment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the commitment received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the commitment.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the budget line that this commitment draws from.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for a change order that modifies the original commitment.']
  commitment_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification indicating whether the commitment is capital‑expenditure or operational‑expenditure.']
  commitment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or comments about the commitment.']
  commitment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the commitment by the originating system (e.g., SAP, Vista).']
  commitment_owner_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for the commitment.']
  commitment_owner_role varchar [note: 'Role of the employee who owns the commitment.']
  commitment_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or process that created the commitment record.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the financial commitment indicating its nature.']
  commitment_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for amendments to the commitment.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center charging the commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commitment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the committed amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the commitment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the commitment becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the commitment ends or expires; null for open‑ended commitments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the commitment is booked.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart‑of‑accounts code to which the commitment is posted.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commitment contains confidential information.']
  is_retention boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commitment includes a retention amount.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent update to the record.']
  original_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original commitment when this record represents an amendment.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the commitment is allocated.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the committed amount held as retention, if applicable.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating contract, purchase order, or agreement.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the originating document that generated the commitment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commitment.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑form text explaining the reason for the current status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the commitment record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor or subcontractor linked to the commitment.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS element associated with the commitment for detailed budgeting.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for commitment. Referenced by commitment_id.'
}

Table bid.firm_profile {
  firm_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for firm_profile']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Required for subcontractor ESG compliance; the firm profile stores reference to the latest ESG report submitted for regulatory and client sustainability reporting.']
  annual_revenue_band varchar [note: 'Banded annual revenue range of the subcontracting firm, used for financial capacity assessment during prequalification. Exact revenue figures are not captured to reduce sensitivity; banding provides sufficient granularity for risk tiering.']
  bonding_capacity_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate bonding capacity in US dollars as certified by the firm\'s surety provider. Determines the maximum contract value the firm can be awarded. Critical for large EPC and GMP contract prequalification.']
  company_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued company registration or incorporation number assigned by the relevant corporate registry (e.g., state secretary of state, Companies House). Used for legal identity verification and compliance checks.']
  contractor_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the firm\'s primary contractor license. Triggers compliance alert workflow when approaching expiry. Firms with expired licenses cannot be awarded new subcontracts.']
  contractor_license_number varchar [note: 'Primary state or jurisdiction contractor license number held by the firm. Required for legal compliance on construction projects. Multiple licenses may exist; this captures the primary license for the firm\'s home jurisdiction.']
  contractor_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the jurisdiction that issued the primary contractor license. Used to validate the firm\'s legal authority to perform work in a given state.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the jurisdiction in which the subcontracting firm is legally incorporated or registered (e.g., USA, AUS, GBR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subcontracting firm profile record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR data retention compliance.']
  dbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds a valid Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (DBE) certification under the US DOT program. Required for compliance reporting on federally funded highway, airport, and transit projects.']
  diversity_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the firm\'s most current MBE/WBE/DBE diversity certification. Triggers renewal notification workflow. Expired certifications cannot be counted toward project diversity spend goals.']
  emr decimal(5,3) [note: 'Experience Modification Rate (EMR) — a workers\' compensation insurance metric reflecting the firm\'s historical safety performance relative to industry average. An EMR below 1.0 indicates better-than-average safety record. Used as a key HSE prequalification criterion per OSHA guidelines.']
  emr_reference_year varchar [note: 'The policy year to which the reported EMR value applies. EMR is recalculated annually; this field identifies the vintage of the current EMR on file.']
  firm_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subcontracting firm within the enterprise\'s approved subcontractor registry. Drives eligibility for bid invitations, trade package awards, and payment processing.']
  geographic_coverage_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions or states where the firm is licensed and operationally capable of performing work (e.g., \'CA,TX,NV,AZ\'). Used for trade package sourcing and bid list construction.']
  headquarters_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the firm\'s registered headquarters or principal place of business. Used for correspondence, legal notices, and geographic coverage analysis.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City of the firm\'s registered headquarters or principal place of business.']
  headquarters_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the firm\'s registered headquarters country.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the firm\'s registered headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code of the firm\'s registered headquarters.']
  insurance_gl_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the firm\'s General Liability (GL) insurance certificate on file. Firms with expired GL insurance are blocked from site mobilization. Tracked separately from workers\' compensation and professional indemnity.']
  insurance_wc_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the firm\'s Workers\' Compensation (WC) insurance certificate on file. Required for all firms deploying labor on construction sites. Expiry triggers compliance hold on site access.']
  is_union_shop boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontracting firm operates as a union shop (True) or open/merit shop (False). Determines eligibility for union-mandated project requirements and prevailing wage projects.']
  iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds a current ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Used as an HSE prequalification criterion.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds a current ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Used as a quality prequalification criterion for projects requiring certified QA/QC systems.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subcontracting firm profile record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and Silver layer incremental load processing.']
  leed_accredited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) accreditation or employs LEED-accredited professionals. Relevant for green building projects requiring LEED-certified subcontractors.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the subcontracting firm as recorded with the relevant government or corporate registry. Used for contract execution, compliance verification, and financial transactions.']
  mbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds a valid Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) certification. Used for diversity spend tracking, DBE/MBE/WBE compliance reporting on federally funded projects, and supplier diversity program management.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code identifying the firm\'s primary industry sector. Used for regulatory reporting, diversity spend tracking, and market segmentation analytics.']
  prequalification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the firm\'s current prequalification approval expires. Triggers renewal workflow in the subcontractor management system. Firms with expired prequalification cannot be awarded new trade packages.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Current prequalification standing of the subcontracting firm against the enterprise\'s vendor qualification criteria. Only firms with \'approved\' status are eligible for trade package award. Managed through the subcontractor prequalification process.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the primary contact at the subcontracting firm. Used for RFQ/RFP issuance, bid invitations, and contract correspondence via Procore and Aconex.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact or account manager at the subcontracting firm. Used for bid invitations, RFQ communications, and contract administration correspondence.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the primary contact at the subcontracting firm, including country and area code.']
  primary_trade_classification varchar [note: 'The principal construction trade or specialty discipline the firm is classified under (e.g., MEP - Mechanical Electrical Plumbing, Civil Works, Structural Steel, Concrete, Roofing, Earthworks, Specialty). Determines which trade packages the firm is eligible to bid on. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mep_mechanical|mep_electrical|mep_plumbing|civil|structural_steel|concrete|earthworks|roofing|glazing|fit_out|specialty — promote to reference product]']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code for the subcontracting firm. Used for legacy regulatory reporting, insurance underwriting, and cross-referencing with older procurement systems.']
  single_project_bond_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum bonding capacity for a single project in US dollars, as distinct from the aggregate bonding capacity. Used to assess the firm\'s eligibility for individual trade package awards.']
  state_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the firm is incorporated or registered (e.g., CA, TX, NY). Relevant for US-based entities for licensing and lien law compliance.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal or national tax identification number for the firm, such as the Employer Identification Number (EIN) in the US or Australian Business Number (ABN) in Australia. Required for IRS/ATO reporting, subcontractor payment processing, and 1099/W-9 compliance.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) name used by the subcontracting firm in day-to-day commercial activities, which may differ from the registered legal entity name.']
  trir decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR) — the number of OSHA-recordable incidents per 200,000 man-hours worked. A key HSE performance indicator used in subcontractor prequalification and ongoing performance evaluation.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name or code of the labor union(s) the firm is affiliated with (e.g., IBEW, UA, Laborers International). Relevant for union project requirements, prevailing wage compliance, and collective bargaining agreement adherence.']
  wbe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firm holds a valid Women-Owned Business Enterprise (WBE) certification. Used for diversity spend tracking and compliance reporting on projects with WBE participation goals.']
  years_in_business varchar [note: 'Number of years the subcontracting firm has been in continuous operation since its founding or incorporation date. Used as a stability and experience indicator in prequalification scoring.']

  Note: 'Master profile of each subcontracting firm engaged or seeking engagement on construction projects. Captures legal entity name, ABN/EIN/company registration number, trade classifications (MEP, civil, structural, specialty), business registration details, bonding capacity, union affiliations, geographic coverage areas, minority/women-owned business enterprise (MBE/WBE/DBE) certifications, NAICS/SIC codes, annual revenue band, EMR (Experience Modification Rate), and financial standing indicators. This is the SSOT for subcontractor firm identity, distinct from the procurement domain\'s vendor master which governs purchase order relationships.'
}

Table bid.subcontractor_prequalification {
  subcontractor_prequalification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subcontractor prequalification record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Needed for Project‑Specific Subcontractor Prequalification process, enabling tracking of which subcontractors are qualified for each construction project.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor firm being prequalified.']
  primary_subcontractor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement or project management officer who conducted the prequalification evaluation and made the approval recommendation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification was formally approved by the approving authority. Null for rejected or pending applications.']
  bonding_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum bonding capacity available to the subcontractor, indicating the largest contract value they can bond. Critical for determining eligibility for high-value trade packages.']
  bonding_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bonding limit amount.']
  certification_requirements_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontractor has provided all required industry certifications (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, trade-specific licenses) as part of the prequalification.']
  conditional_approval_requirements varchar [note: 'List of conditions or remedial actions the subcontractor must fulfill to convert conditional approval to full approval (e.g., submit updated insurance certificates, provide additional references).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prequalification record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the prequalification approval becomes valid and the subcontractor is eligible to bid on trade packages.']
  eligible_project_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of project types or sectors for which this prequalification is valid (e.g., commercial, infrastructure, energy, residential). Used to filter RFQ distribution.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification evaluation was completed by the evaluating officer.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations recorded by the evaluating officer during the assessment, including strengths, weaknesses, and areas of concern.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the prequalification approval expires and requalification is required. Nullable for rejected or conditional statuses.']
  financial_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Evaluation score assessing the subcontractor\'s financial strength, liquidity, and ability to fund project work. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  financial_statements_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontractor\'s financial statements (balance sheet, P&L, cash flow) have been reviewed and verified by the evaluating officer or finance team.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries where the subcontractor is prequalified to operate, based on licensing, local presence, and regulatory compliance.']
  insurance_adequacy_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Evaluation score assessing whether the subcontractor\'s insurance coverage (general liability, workers compensation, professional indemnity) meets project requirements. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prequalification record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_contract_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maximum contract value.']
  maximum_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum trade package contract value the subcontractor is prequalified to bid on, based on financial capacity and bonding limits.']
  minimum_passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Threshold score required for prequalification approval. Subcontractors scoring below this threshold are rejected or granted conditional approval.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite weighted score aggregating all evaluation criteria (financial, safety, performance, technical, bonding, insurance). Used to determine approval decision. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  past_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Evaluation score reflecting the subcontractor\'s track record on previous projects, including quality of work, schedule adherence, and client satisfaction. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  prequalification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this prequalification assessment, used in correspondence and RFQ invitations.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prequalification record, determining whether the subcontractor is eligible to receive RFQs and bid invitations.']
  reference_check_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reference checks with previous clients or general contractors have been completed and verified as part of the evaluation process.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the prequalification was rejected, including specific criteria failures or compliance gaps. Null for approved applications.']
  requalification_trigger varchar [note: 'Business event or condition that requires the subcontractor to undergo requalification before expiry date (e.g., major safety incident, financial distress, change in ownership, significant performance failure).']
  safety_record_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Evaluation score based on the subcontractor\'s historical safety performance, including TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, and HSE compliance. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the subcontractor submitted their prequalification application package.']
  technical_capability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Evaluation score assessing the subcontractor\'s technical expertise, workforce qualifications, equipment availability, and ability to execute complex trade packages. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  trade_category varchar [note: 'Primary trade or specialty category for which the subcontractor is being prequalified (e.g., MEP, civil works, structural steel, concrete, electrical, plumbing, HVAC).']

  Note: 'Prequalification assessment records for subcontractor firms seeking approval to bid on trade packages. Tracks submission date, evaluation criteria scores (financial capacity, safety record, past performance, bonding limits, insurance adequacy), prequalification status (approved, conditional, rejected, expired), expiry date, evaluating officer, and requalification triggers. Governs which firms are eligible to receive RFQs and bid invitations.'
}

Table bid.trade_package {
  trade_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade package. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Package Allocation Report requires linking each trade package to its master agreement to track contract value per package.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent construction project to which this trade package belongs.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project team member responsible for managing this trade package.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor to whom this trade package was awarded.']
  green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.green_certification. Business justification: Trade packages often mandate a specific green building certification; linking allows verification that the subcontractor’s work meets the required certification.']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: Associates each trade package with its budget line, supporting budget control and earned value analysis.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WBS element that this trade package is associated with for cost and schedule control.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the subcontractor completed all work under this trade package.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the subcontractor commenced work on this trade package.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the trade package was formally awarded to the selected subcontractor.']
  awarded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final contract value at which the trade package was awarded to the subcontractor.']
  bid_closing_date date [note: 'Deadline date by which subcontractors must submit their bids for this trade package.']
  bid_out_date date [note: 'Date when the trade package was issued to subcontractors for competitive bidding.']
  bonding_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether performance and payment bonds are required from the subcontractor for this trade package.']
  budget_allowance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocation reserved for this trade package in the project cost plan.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of contract pricing mechanism for this trade package (Lump Sum, Unit Price, Cost Plus, Guaranteed Maximum Price, Time and Materials).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade package record was first created in the system.']
  csi_masterformat_code varchar [note: 'CSI MasterFormat classification code for the scope of work (e.g., 03 30 00 for Cast-in-Place Concrete).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this trade package (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration in calendar days for the execution of this trade package.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Pre-bid estimated value of the trade package based on quantity take-offs and market rates.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Summary of insurance coverage requirements that the awarded subcontractor must maintain (e.g., general liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade package record was last updated.']
  liquidated_damages_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily rate of liquidated damages applicable if the subcontractor fails to complete the trade package by the planned completion date.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this trade package.']
  number_of_bidders_invited varchar [note: 'Count of subcontractors invited to submit bids for this trade package.']
  number_of_bids_received varchar [note: 'Count of valid bids received from subcontractors by the bid closing date.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the trade package (e.g., Structural Steel Erection, HVAC Installation, Electrical Fit-Out).']
  package_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of this trade package relative to other packages in the project (Critical, High, Medium, Low).']
  package_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the trade package used in bidding and contract documents.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trade package in the bidding and award process.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which payment is due to the subcontractor after invoice approval.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subcontractor to complete all work under this trade package.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subcontractor to commence work on this trade package.']
  prequalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subcontractors must be prequalified before being invited to bid on this trade package.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to procure this trade package (Open Tender, Selective Tender, Negotiated, Single Source, Framework Agreement).']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each payment withheld as retention until satisfactory completion of the trade package.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for this trade package based on complexity, value, and schedule criticality.']
  scope_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the scope of work included in this trade package, including deliverables, exclusions, and boundaries.']
  trade_discipline_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the trade discipline (e.g., MEP, CIVIL, STRUCT, ARCH, ELEC, HVAC, PLUMB).']
  uniformat_code varchar [note: 'UniFormat II classification code for elemental cost planning (e.g., B2010 for Exterior Walls).']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the defects liability period (DLP) or warranty period after completion.']

  Note: 'Defines a discrete scope-of-work package to be competitively bid and awarded to a subcontractor — e.g., structural steel erection, HVAC installation, electrical fit-out, piling works, or facade cladding. Captures trade discipline code, CSI MasterFormat/UniFormat classification, package reference number, associated WBS elements, scope narrative, estimated value, budget allowance, bid-out date, bid closing date, number of bidders invited, award date, and package status (draft, bid-out, evaluation, awarded, closed). The SSOT for trade package definitions that drive the subcontractor bidding and award process.'
}

Table bid.contract_agreement {
  contract_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract_agreement']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent construction project under which this subcontract is executed.']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Contract Management process assigns an internal employee as contract manager; linking enables manager accountability and reporting.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor party executing the work under this agreement.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps each subcontractor contract to a GL account for expense recognition and financial reporting of contract costs.']
  sustainability_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_plan. Business justification: Contracts include sustainability obligations; linking to the sustainability plan records the plan the subcontractor must follow for the project.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the subcontractor achieved substantial completion and received acceptance from the general contractor.']
  advance_payment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the contract value paid as an advance payment to the subcontractor upon contract signing or NTP, subject to advance payment guarantee.']
  contract_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the subcontract value and payments are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the subcontract agreement expires or is scheduled for final closeout, including all warranty and retention obligations.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the subcontract agreement, typically referencing the trade package or scope.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the subcontract agreement, used in correspondence and documentation.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the subcontract agreement was formally executed and signed by both the general contractor and the subcontractor.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subcontract agreement: draft (under negotiation), pending_approval (awaiting internal sign-off), awarded (signed but not started), active (work in progress), suspended (temporarily halted), completed (work finished), terminated (cancelled), closed (final settlement complete). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|awarded|active|suspended|completed|terminated|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of subcontract pricing structure: lump-sum (fixed price), unit-rate (measured work), cost-plus (reimbursable), GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price), time-and-materials, or design-build.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subcontract agreement record was first created in the system.']
  current_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total contract value including all approved change orders, variations, and adjustments to date.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Primary method for resolving disputes under this subcontract: negotiation, mediation, arbitration, litigation, or DAB (Dispute Adjudication Board) as per FIDIC.']
  dlp_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration in days of the Defects Liability Period (also known as warranty period) during which the subcontractor is responsible for rectifying defects, typically 12-24 months.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'Calculated or actual end date of the Defects Liability Period, after which final retention is released and warranty obligations conclude.']
  executed_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original signed contract value in the contract currency, representing the baseline commitment before any change orders or variations.']
  fidic_conditions_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the FIDIC contract conditions edition and color book (e.g., Red Book 1999, Yellow Book 2017) governing the contractual framework and dispute resolution.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law under which the subcontract is interpreted and enforced (e.g., State of New York, England and Wales, UAE Civil Code).']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the subcontractor is contractually required to maintain specific insurance coverage (e.g., general liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subcontract agreement record was last updated or modified.']
  ld_cap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum total liquidated damages expressed as a percentage of the contract value (typically 10-20%), beyond which no further LDs are applied.']
  ld_rate_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily liquidated damages amount deductible from the subcontractor for each day of delay beyond the planned completion date, as stipulated in the contract.']
  ntp_date date [note: 'Date on which the general contractor issued the Notice to Proceed, authorizing the subcontractor to commence work on site.']
  payment_application_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the subcontractor submits payment applications for work completed: monthly (calendar month), bi-weekly (every two weeks), milestone-based (upon achieving defined milestones), or completion-based (upon final completion).']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice submission or payment application approval to payment due date (e.g., Net 30, Net 45).']
  performance_bond_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the contract value that the performance bond must cover (typically 5-10%).']
  performance_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the subcontractor is required to provide a performance bond or bank guarantee to secure contract performance.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Contractually agreed date by which the subcontractor must achieve substantial completion of the scope of work.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Contractually agreed date for the subcontractor to begin work on site, typically aligned with or following the NTP date.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or clarifications related to the subcontract agreement.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each payment withheld by the general contractor as retention (typically 5-10%) to ensure performance and defect rectification, released after DLP completion.']
  scope_of_work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the work scope, deliverables, and responsibilities assigned to the subcontractor under this agreement.']
  subcontractor_representative_name varchar [note: 'Name of the subcontractor representative or project manager designated as the primary point of contact for this agreement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the subcontract was terminated by either party, applicable only if contract_status is terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the reason for contract termination (e.g., default by subcontractor, convenience termination by contractor, force majeure).']
  trade_package_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the trade package or scope-of-work category (e.g., MEP, civil works, structural steel, concrete, finishes).']

  Note: 'Subcontract agreement record governing the legal and commercial relationship between the general contractor and a subcontractor for a specific trade package. Captures contract number, contract type (lump-sum, unit-rate, cost-plus, GMP), executed contract value, retention percentage, payment terms, NTP date, planned completion date, DLP duration, LD (liquidated damages) rate, FIDIC conditions reference, and contract status. Distinct from the contract domain which owns head contracts with clients.'
}

Table bid.payment_application {
  payment_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment application record. Primary key for the payment application entity.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this payment application is submitted.']
  contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontract agreement governing this payment application.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed to allocate each subcontractor payment to the appropriate cost center for cost reporting and internal chargeback.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineer, architect, or project manager who certified this payment application.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor submitting this payment application.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for posting subcontractor payment applications to the general ledger; the finance team needs the GL account to record the expense per contract.']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: Links payment applications to the specific project budget line they consume, enabling budget variance tracking.']
  amount_certified_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount certified and approved by the certifying engineer or project manager for payment in this application, which may differ from the amount claimed if adjustments are made.']
  amount_claimed_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount claimed by the subcontractor for work performed during this billing period before retention and back-charges.']
  amount_previously_certified decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount certified and approved in all previous payment applications prior to this one.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Sequential business identifier for the payment application (e.g., Pay App #5). Externally-known reference number used in correspondence and approvals.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment application in the approval and payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|certified|approved|paid|disputed|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  back_charges_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total back-charges deducted from this payment application for costs incurred by the general contractor to correct deficiencies, delays, or damages caused by the subcontractor.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this payment application.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this payment application.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the payment application was certified and approved by the certifying engineer, architect, or project manager.']
  contract_value_original decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original total value of the subcontract agreement before any change orders or modifications.']
  contract_value_revised decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total value of the subcontract including all approved change orders (CO) and modifications to date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment application record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payment application (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AUD).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment application is currently under dispute between the subcontractor and general contractor or owner.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the payment application is contested (e.g., quality issues, scope disagreement, quantity discrepancy).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment application record was last updated or modified.']
  lien_waiver_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a conditional or unconditional lien waiver has been received from the subcontractor for this payment application.']
  liquidated_damages_deducted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Liquidated damages amount deducted from this payment for failure to meet contractual milestones or completion dates.']
  materials_stored_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative value of materials delivered to site and properly stored but not yet incorporated into the work, eligible for payment per contract terms.']
  net_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount payable to the subcontractor after all retention, back-charges, liquidated damages, and other deductions are applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this payment application, including clarifications, special conditions, or reviewer observations.']
  other_deductions decimal(18,2) [note: 'Other miscellaneous deductions applied to this payment application (e.g., insurance adjustments, warranty holdbacks, disputed amounts).']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date when payment was issued to the subcontractor.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Contractual date by which payment must be made to the subcontractor after certification, typically 30 days from certification per standard payment terms.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Financial system reference number or check number for the payment transaction (e.g., EFT reference, check number).']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment application indicating the nature of the claim (progress payment for work completed, retention release at milestone, final payment at project closeout, mobilization advance, or stored materials payment).']
  percent_complete_certified decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of contract work completion certified by the project engineer or quantity surveyor, which may differ from the claimed percentage after field verification.']
  percent_complete_claimed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of contract work completion claimed by the subcontractor in this payment application, calculated from Schedule of Values (SOV) line items.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each payment withheld as retention per contract terms (e.g., 10% standard retention rate).']
  retention_release_milestone varchar [note: 'The contractual milestone triggering retention release (practical completion, DLP expiry, final completion, or partial release per contract terms).']
  retention_released_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of previously withheld retention released to the subcontractor in this payment application, typically upon reaching practical completion or DLP (Defects Liability Period) expiry milestone.']
  retention_withheld_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of retention withheld from the current billing period payment based on the retention percentage.']
  retention_withheld_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative retention amount withheld from all payment applications to date.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the subcontractor submitted the payment application to the general contractor or project owner. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the payment application was submitted into the system.']
  supporting_documents_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required supporting documentation (lien waivers, certified payroll, insurance certificates, progress photos) has been submitted with this payment application.']
  total_earned_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount earned by the subcontractor through this billing period (work completed plus materials stored).']
  work_completed_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative value of all work completed from contract start through the end of this billing period, based on Schedule of Values (SOV) breakdown.']

  Note: 'Progress payment application (pay app) submitted by a subcontractor for work completed during a billing period, encompassing the full payment lifecycle from submission through certification to payment. Captures application number, billing period, payment type (progress, retention release, final), schedule of values breakdown by WBS/BOQ line, percent complete claimed, amount claimed, previously certified amount, certified amount, retention withheld, retention released, back-charges applied, net amount due, submission date, certification date, certifying engineer, payment due date, release milestone (practical completion, DLP expiry), and application status (submitted, under review, certified, paid, disputed). This is the SSOT for all subcontractor payment events including progress claims, interim certificates, retention releases, and final payments.'
}

Table bid.insurance_certificate {
  insurance_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance certificate record. Primary key.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this insurance certificate is required. Enables project-level compliance reporting.']
