  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor who submitted this insurance certificate. Enables tracking of all certificates by subcontractor across multiple contracts.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontract that this insurance certificate covers. Links the certificate to the specific scope-of-work agreement requiring insurance compliance.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Insurance compliance verification is performed by a designated HR/compliance employee; needed for regulatory reporting.']
  additional_insured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the general contractor is named as an additional insured on the policy. Critical for liability protection and contract compliance.']
  aggregate_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount the insurer will pay for all claims during the policy period. Distinct from per-occurrence coverage limits.']
  certificate_holder_address varchar [note: 'The address of the certificate holder. Used for correspondence and verification purposes.']
  certificate_holder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the entity to whom the certificate is issued, typically the general contractor or project owner. Confirms the certificate is issued for the correct party.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number issued by the insurance provider. This is the externally-known identifier used for verification and claims.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'The current compliance status of the insurance certificate. Drives site access decisions and compliance alerts.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding compliance issues, exceptions, or special conditions related to the insurance certificate. Used for audit trails and issue resolution.']
  coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total coverage limit provided by the insurance policy. Represents the maximum amount the insurer will pay for covered claims.']
  coverage_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which the coverage amount is denominated. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance certificate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deductible amount that the subcontractor must pay before insurance coverage applies. Impacts risk assessment and claims processing.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the digital copy of the insurance certificate document. Enables quick access for verification and audits.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance coverage becomes active. Used to determine if coverage is in force for site access and contract compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance coverage expires. Critical for compliance monitoring and triggering renewal alerts.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the insurance company that issued the policy. Used for verification and claims processing.']
  insurer_rating varchar [note: 'The financial strength rating of the insurance company (e.g., A.M. Best rating). Used to assess the reliability and solvency of the insurer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance certificate record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  non_compliance_reason varchar [note: 'The specific reason why the certificate is marked as non-compliant. Drives corrective action and communication with the subcontractor.']
  per_occurrence_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount the insurer will pay for a single claim or occurrence. Used to assess adequacy of coverage for high-risk activities.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The unique policy number assigned by the insurer. Used for verification and claims tracking.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'The type of insurance policy covered by this certificate. Determines the risk category and compliance requirements for the subcontractor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general_liability|workers_compensation|professional_indemnity|contractors_all_risk|employers_liability|auto_liability|umbrella|pollution_liability — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_non_contributory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontractor\'s insurance is primary and non-contributory to the general contractor\'s coverage. Ensures the subcontractor\'s policy pays first.']
  renewal_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'The date when a renewal reminder was sent to the subcontractor. Used to track proactive compliance management efforts.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date when the subcontractor submitted the insurance certificate to the general contractor. Used for compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance certificate was verified by the general contractor or compliance team. Confirms authenticity and coverage adequacy.']
  waiver_of_subrogation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy includes a waiver of subrogation in favor of the general contractor. Prevents the insurer from pursuing claims against the GC.']

  Note: 'Insurance compliance certificates submitted by subcontractors as a condition of contract execution and ongoing site access. Tracks policy type (general liability, workers compensation, professional indemnity, contractor\'s all-risk), insurer name, policy number, coverage amount, effective date, expiry date, certificate status (valid, expiring, expired, non-compliant), and the subcontract it covers. Drives compliance alerts for expiring coverage.'
}

Table bid.performance_scorecard {
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subcontractor performance scorecard evaluation record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Performance Scorecard evaluates subcontractor against a specific contract agreement, needing agreement_id to associate scores with that contract.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this subcontractor performance is being evaluated.']
  evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Scorecard evaluations are conducted by a specific employee; linking enables traceability and performance analytics.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor being evaluated in this performance scorecard.']
  performance_esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Scorecards evaluate subcontractor performance against ESG metrics; storing the ESG report used for assessment enables traceability.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the performance scorecard was formally approved by authorized management.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the performance scorecard document.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the senior manager or PMO director who approved the final performance scorecard.']
  bid_eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current bid eligibility status resulting from this performance evaluation: eligible for all future bids, restricted to certain project types, or ineligible for future bidding.']
  change_order_disputes varchar [note: 'Number of disputed or contested change orders raised by or against the subcontractor during the evaluation period.']
  commercial_conduct_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the subcontractor\'s commercial conduct including responsiveness to instructions, cooperation, documentation quality, and change order management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'The date on which the performance scorecard assessment was completed and recorded.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the performance evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the performance evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  evaluator_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments and observations from the evaluator providing additional context, specific incidents, or qualitative assessment details.']
  evaluator_role varchar [note: 'The role or title of the person who conducted the evaluation (e.g., Project Manager, Construction Manager, PMO Director).']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which follow-up actions or corrective measures must be completed and reviewed.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether follow-up actions, meetings, or corrective action plans are required based on this performance evaluation.']
  hse_compliance_violations varchar [note: 'Number of HSE compliance violations or safety infractions recorded during the evaluation period.']
  hse_lti_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Lost Time Injuries recorded for the subcontractor\'s workforce during the evaluation period.']
  hse_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the subcontractor\'s overall HSE compliance and safety performance during the evaluation period.']
  hse_trir decimal(7,4) [note: 'Total Recordable Incident Rate calculated as (Number of OSHA recordable incidents × 200,000) / Total hours worked. Industry standard HSE metric.']
  improvement_areas varchar [note: 'Detailed description of specific areas where the subcontractor needs to improve performance, including corrective actions required.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance scorecard record was last updated or modified.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall categorical performance rating summarizing the subcontractor\'s performance across all evaluation dimensions.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted overall performance score (0-100) calculated from individual category scores (schedule, quality, HSE, productivity, commercial conduct).']
  prequalification_impact varchar [note: 'Impact of this performance scorecard on the subcontractor\'s prequalification status for future projects: automatic renewal, conditional renewal, requires detailed review, or disqualification.']
  productivity_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Productivity rate measured as units completed per labor hour or per day during the evaluation period.']
  productivity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the subcontractor\'s productivity and efficiency in completing assigned work during the evaluation period.']
  productivity_units_completed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total quantity of work units completed by the subcontractor during the evaluation period (e.g., cubic meters of concrete, linear meters of pipe, square meters of formwork).']
  quality_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Non-Conformance Reports issued against the subcontractor during the evaluation period.']
  quality_ncr_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Rate of Non-Conformance Reports per unit of work completed (e.g., NCRs per 1000 work hours or per million dollars of work).']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the subcontractor\'s overall quality conformance and workmanship during the evaluation period.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Recommended action based on the performance evaluation outcome: continue engagement, monitor closely, require improvement plan, place on watch-list, disqualify from future bids, or suspend from current work.']
  rfi_response_timeliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0-100) measuring the subcontractor\'s timeliness in responding to RFIs and providing requested information.']
  schedule_adherence_spi decimal(5,3) [note: 'Schedule Performance Index measuring the subcontractor\'s adherence to planned schedule. SPI = Earned Value / Planned Value. Values >1.0 indicate ahead of schedule, <1.0 indicate behind schedule.']
  schedule_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the subcontractor\'s overall schedule performance during the evaluation period.']
  strengths varchar [note: 'Description of the subcontractor\'s key strengths and positive performance aspects observed during the evaluation period.']
  subcontractor_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the subcontractor acknowledged receipt and review of the performance scorecard.']
  subcontractor_response varchar [note: 'Subcontractor\'s formal response or comments regarding the performance evaluation, including any disputes or corrective action commitments.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance evaluation of a subcontractor against defined KPIs including schedule adherence (SPI), quality conformance (NCR rate), HSE compliance (LTI/TRIR), productivity, and commercial conduct. Captures evaluation period, scoring by category, overall rating, evaluating manager, date of assessment, and recommended actions (continue, improve, watch-list, disqualify). Feeds prequalification renewal decisions and future bid eligibility.'
}

Table bid.subcontractor_bond {
  subcontractor_bond_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for subcontractor_bond']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this bond is issued.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Bond records are created by a responsible employee; linking provides accountability and audit trail.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontractor providing this bond.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subcontract or trade package that this bond secures.']
  alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiry at which an alert should be triggered for bond renewal or replacement (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Face value or penal sum of the bond in the contract currency. Represents the maximum liability of the surety.']
  bond_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bond amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  bond_form varchar [note: 'Standard bond form or template used: AIA A311 (performance and payment bond), AIA A312 (performance and payment bond - current), FIDIC (international standard), custom (project-specific), or statutory (government-mandated form).']
  bond_number varchar [note: 'Unique bond certificate number issued by the surety company.']
  bond_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Bond amount expressed as a percentage of the subcontract value (e.g., 10.00 for 10% performance bond).']
  bond_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost paid by the subcontractor to the surety company for issuing the bond.']
  bond_premium_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Premium rate expressed as a percentage of the bond amount (e.g., 0.0150 for 1.5% premium rate).']
  bond_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bond: active (in force), expired (past expiry date), called (claim made against bond), released (obligation discharged), cancelled (bond voided), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  bond_type varchar [note: 'Type of surety bond: performance bond (guarantees work completion), payment bond (guarantees payment to suppliers/workers), bid bond (guarantees bid acceptance), maintenance bond (guarantees defect-free period), supply bond (guarantees material delivery), or subdivision bond (guarantees infrastructure completion).']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount claimed against the bond. Null if no claim has been filed.']
  claim_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which a claim was filed against the bond by the obligee. Null if no claim has been filed.']
  claim_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the surety to settle the claim. Null if no payment made or no claim filed.']
  claim_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond claim was resolved (paid, settled, or denied). Null if claim is unresolved or no claim filed.']
  compliance_verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that last verified bond compliance.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond was last verified for compliance with contract requirements (amount, surety rating, expiry).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bond record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the scanned bond certificate or digital bond document stored in the document management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond becomes active and enforceable.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond expires or is scheduled to be released. May be extended if the subcontract is extended.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this bond record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bond record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the bond, including special conditions, amendments, or historical context.']
  obligee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party protected by the bond, typically the general contractor (GC) or project owner.']
  principal_name varchar [note: 'Name of the principal party (subcontractor) whose performance is guaranteed by the bond.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond was formally released by the obligee, discharging the surety\'s obligation.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for bond release: subcontract completion, defects liability period expiry, mutual agreement, or other contractual milestone.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the bond was last renewed or extended. Null if never renewed.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bond requires renewal or extension (True) or is a one-time issuance (False).']
  surety_company_am_best_rating varchar [note: 'AM Best financial strength rating of the surety company (e.g., A++, A+, A, A-, B++). Indicates the surety\'s ability to meet its obligations.']
  surety_company_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the surety company or insurance carrier issuing the bond.']
  surety_license_number varchar [note: 'State or jurisdiction license number of the surety company authorizing it to issue bonds.']

  Note: 'Surety bond records provided by subcontractors as financial security instruments, including performance bonds, payment bonds, and bid bonds. Captures bond type, surety company, bond number, bond amount, effective date, expiry date, bond status (active, called, released, expired), and the subcontract or trade package it secures. Tracks bonding compliance and triggers alerts for expiring or insufficient bonds.'
}

Table bid.bid_opportunity {
  bid_opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the bid opportunity record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the client organization that the opportunity targets.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Awarded project tracking – after winning a bid opportunity, the resulting construction project is recorded for performance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost Allocation Report: tracks which finance cost center funds bid preparation expenses, essential for budgeting and profitability analysis of each bid opportunity.']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: ESG compliance review during bid qualification requires linking each opportunity to the relevant ESG report per regulatory ESG reporting mandates.']
  jv_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.jv_partner. Business justification: Bid opportunity may involve a joint venture partner; replace string with FK to jv_partner for referential integrity.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial guarantee amount required to submit the bid.']
  bid_decision varchar [note: 'Decision on whether to submit a bid for the opportunity.']
  bid_due_date date [note: 'Final date by which the bid must be submitted.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the project location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid opportunity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated contract value.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the estimated contract value during bid preparation.']
  estimated_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total contract value before any adjustments.']
  expected_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date of the project if the bid is won.']
  expected_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the project if the bid is won.']
  gmp_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing model used in the bid (e.g., Guaranteed Maximum Price).']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bid is being submitted as a joint venture.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment or market category the opportunity belongs to.']
  net_estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated contract value after discounts and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the opportunity.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the bid opportunity, typically reflecting the project or client.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the opportunity, used in client communications and reporting.']
  pipeline_forecast_category varchar [note: 'Classification used for forecasting and reporting of the opportunity.']
  probability_of_win decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated likelihood of winning the opportunity expressed as a percentage.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project type for the opportunity.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the opportunity was generated.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the opportunity within the sales pipeline.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bid opportunity record.']
  win_loss_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the opportunity after the bid process concludes.']

  Note: 'Master record for each pre-award commercial opportunity tracked in Salesforce CRM. Captures the full pipeline entry from initial lead through bid submission, representing a potential project the business is pursuing. Stores opportunity name, client reference, market segment, project type, estimated contract value, probability of win, bid/no-bid decision, opportunity stage, source channel, geographic region, and pipeline forecast category. SSOT for commercial opportunity identity in the bid domain.'
}

Table bid.tender {
  tender_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the tender record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client/owner for whom the tender is prepared.']
  client_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_opportunity. Business justification: Needed for Tender-to-Opportunity mapping used in pipeline forecasting and compliance reporting of client‑driven tenders.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Tender management – each tender is issued for a specific construction project, needed for schedule and cost integration.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Tender Cost Tracking: associates tender expenses with a finance cost center for accurate cost reporting and compliance with internal budgeting policies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead estimator responsible for the tender package.']
  quality_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.plan. Business justification: Tender package requires a Quality Management Plan per client contract; linking ensures plan is attached to each tender.']
  sustainability_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_plan. Business justification: Tender evaluation criteria include sustainability plan compliance; linking tender to the applicable sustainability plan is required for award decisions.']
  award_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the client communicated the award decision.']
  award_status varchar [note: 'Final outcome status of the tender.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the required bid bond.']
  bid_bond_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the bid bond.']
  bid_bond_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a bid bond must be provided with the tender.']
  bid_bond_type varchar [note: 'Form of the bid bond provided.']
  bid_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tender is for a new contract, renewal, or extension.']
  compliance_requirements_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tender satisfies all mandatory compliance criteria.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tender information is marked as confidential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tender record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: many currencies — promote to reference product]']
  documents_attached varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents linked to the tender.']
  estimated_duration_months varchar [note: 'Projected duration of the project in months.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the contract if the tender is won.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used by the client to evaluate the tender.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score resulting from the client’s evaluation process.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tender is submitted as a joint venture.']
  joint_venture_partner varchar [note: 'Name of the partner organization in a joint‑venture tender.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks related to the tender.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the pre‑qualification review for the tendering entity.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to procure the project (open, selective, limited).']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date of the construction project.']
  project_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or address of the construction project.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the construction project.']
  project_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the construction project associated with the tender.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO region code for the project location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: many regions — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if external regulatory approval is needed for the tender.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approval.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating assigned to the tender.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Actual date the tender was submitted to the client.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Last calendar date by which the tender must be submitted.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tender submission.']
  tender_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the tender by the organization.']
  tender_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tender contract model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tender record.']
  validity_end date [note: 'Date when the tender expires if not awarded.']
  validity_start date [note: 'Date when the tender becomes officially valid for consideration.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a formal tender submission package prepared in response to an RFP or RFQ. Captures tender reference number, project title, client/owner identity, tender type (lump-sum, GMP, unit-rate, EPC), submission deadline, tender validity period, submission status, bid bond requirement flag, prequalification status, and the lead estimator assigned. Links to the parent opportunity and the resulting contract upon award. SSOT for tender identity and submission lifecycle.'
}

Table bid.estimate {
  estimate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the cost estimate record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client or owner for whom the estimate is prepared.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this estimate belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Ensures estimate preparation costs are charged to the appropriate finance cost center, supporting cost control and variance analysis between estimated and actual costs.']
  design_submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: Estimators rely on design submittals to verify scope and specifications; the estimate must reference the submittal it is based on.']
  embodied_carbon_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.embodied_carbon_assessment. Business justification: Cost estimating processes incorporate embodied carbon assessments to evaluate carbon impact, necessitating a direct FK from estimate to the assessment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the estimator or team responsible for the estimate.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: Cost estimate incorporates mitigation measures identified in the environmental impact assessment.']
  labor_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_rate. Business justification: Required for estimate cost calculations that use labor rates defined in workforce.labor_rate; estimators reference these rates to price labor components.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bid package or tender associated with the estimate.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the estimate was formally approved for submission.']
  base_pricing_date date [note: 'Date on which the unit rates and cost data were sourced for the estimate.']
  contingency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage added to cover unknowns and risks in the estimate.']
  cost_breakdown_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the detailed cost breakdown structure used in the estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the estimate record was first entered into the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the estimate.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or path to the electronic document containing the full estimate details.']
  escalation_allowance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowance expressed as a percentage to account for price escalation over time.']
  estimate_category varchar [note: 'Business category indicating the nature of the work the estimate covers.']
  estimate_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the estimate for easy identification.']
  estimate_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the estimate, used in bid documentation and communications.']
  estimate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the estimate within the bid process.']
  estimate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the estimate based on its level of detail and development stage.']
  estimating_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to develop the cost estimate.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the estimate is no longer valid for bid submission.']
  is_gmp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the estimate is prepared as a GMP (True) or not (False).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'True when the estimate is locked from further edits after approval.']
  is_lump_sum boolean [note: 'True if the estimate is a lump‑sum price; otherwise False.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks added by the estimator.']
  overhead_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overhead cost expressed as a percentage of direct costs.']
  profit_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired profit margin expressed as a percentage of total cost.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the estimate was created.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the version of the estimate.']
  risk_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric factor representing the overall risk level applied to the estimate.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated impact on project schedule expressed in days if the estimate is accepted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application that originated the estimate record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Procore).']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated cost of the estimate before taxes, including all cost components.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the estimate record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a project cost estimate prepared during the bid phase. Captures estimate number, estimate type (conceptual, schematic, detailed, definitive), base date of pricing, total estimated cost, contingency percentage, escalation allowance, overhead and profit margin, currency, estimate status, and the estimating method used (parametric, unit-rate, first-principles). Supports multiple estimate revisions per tender. SSOT for pre-award cost estimation data.'
}

Table bid.boq {
  boq_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each BOQ master record.']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: Required for Quantity Takeoff: BOQ creation uses BIM model geometry to derive quantities; the BOQ must reference the BIM model used.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign‑key hint referencing the overarching construction project for which the BOQ is prepared.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links BOQ preparation to a finance cost center, enabling detailed cost center reporting for bill of quantities and supporting budget approvals.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Required for Quantity Takeoff: BOQ lines are derived from construction drawings; linking each BOQ to its source drawing enables traceability.']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Needed for BOQ audit trail; the employee who prepares the Bill of Quantities must be recorded for compliance and accountability.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign‑key hint linking the BOQ to the tender (project bid) it supports.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the BOQ was signed off by the project controls authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who granted final approval to the BOQ.']
  boq_description varchar [note: 'Narrative field for additional context, special instructions, or remarks from the estimator.']
  boq_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the BOQ is still being edited, has been issued to bidders, approved, revised, or archived.']
  boq_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the BOQ as measured (based on take‑off), provisional (estimated), or approximate (high‑level).']
  contains_confidential_pricing boolean [note: 'True if the BOQ includes pricing that must be treated as confidential per contract and regulatory policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the BOQ master record entered the data lake.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier (e.g., USD, EUR) used for the total_value field.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the BOQ total_value to the company\'s reporting currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Effective date for the exchange_rate value.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Optional end‑date after which the BOQ cannot be used for pricing; null if open‑ended.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the BOQ was released to bidders as part of the tender package.']
  preparation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the BOQ document was initially compiled.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to identify the BOQ within the tender package.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Incremental integer indicating the version of the BOQ; higher numbers represent later revisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Indicates which operational system of record supplied the BOQ data.']
  specification_standard varchar [note: 'Industry standard governing the format and measurement rules of the BOQ (e.g., NRM, POMI, CESMM).']
  total_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate of the quantity field from all BOQ line items, expressed in the unit defined by the specification standard.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate price of all line items in the BOQ, expressed in the selected currency.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Login or employee identifier of the last person to modify the BOQ.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to any BOQ field.']
  version_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version identifier shown on the BOQ cover page.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Login or employee identifier of the estimator who initially created the BOQ.']

  Note: 'Bill of Quantities master record defining the structured pricing document attached to a tender. Captures BOQ reference, revision number, BOQ type (measured, provisional, approximate), total BOQ value, currency, preparation date, and the specification standard applied (NRM, POMI, CESMM). Each BOQ is linked to a tender and decomposed into BOQ line items. SSOT for BOQ document identity and header-level pricing data.'
}

Table bid.bid_boq_line {
  bid_boq_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BOQ line item.']
  boq_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent BOQ header.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: BOQ lines may specify sustainable materials; linking each line to the sustainable_material record enables tracking of green procurement.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the work was actually completed.']
  bid_boq_line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the work item.']
  bid_boq_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOQ line.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line originated from a change order.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated change order, if any.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of cost type for budgeting and reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the line for accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  estimated_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date for completing the work represented by the line.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Marks the line as part of the project critical path.']
  is_gmp_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line is included in a Guaranteed Maximum Price bid.']
  is_lump_sum boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line is part of a lump‑sum bid component.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line item is subject to tax.']
  item_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the work item as defined in the company\'s catalog.']
  labour_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to labour for the line item.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the BOQ.']
  material_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to materials.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks or clarifications.']
  overhead_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated overhead amount for the line item.']
  plant_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to plant/equipment usage.']
  profit_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target profit margin percentage applied to the line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item required as per take‑off.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the line item after changes.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the line item.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which the line originated (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  subcontract_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to subcontracted work.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated tax amount for the line item.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for taxable items.']
  total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated total cost for the line (quantity × unit_rate).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., meter, kilogram, piece). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unit|kg|m|m2|m3|l|pcs|hr — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for the item.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the line record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the line to the project WBS hierarchy.']
  work_section varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping of work items (e.g., civil, structural, MEP).']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a Bill of Quantities, representing a discrete work item priced during bid preparation. Captures item code, WBS reference, work section, item description, unit of measure, quantity, unit rate, total amount, labour component, plant component, material component, subcontract component, and overhead allocation. Supports MTO-driven quantity take-off and rate build-up analysis. Essential for bid-to-actual cost variance tracking post-award.'
}

Table bid.estimate_line {
  estimate_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the estimate line item.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of plant/equipment associated with the line.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the estimate belongs.']
  estimate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent estimate (transaction header) to which this line belongs.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subcontractor providing the service or material.']
  labor_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_rate. Business justification: Each estimate line may apply a specific labor rate; linking to workforce.labor_rate enables precise labor cost per line in bid estimates.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Estimating uses master material data for unit pricing, compliance and spec reference, essential for accurate bid cost calculations.']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the resource (item, equipment, labour) associated with this line.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the line.']
  baseline_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original cost captured at baseline creation.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a change order that modified this line.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Category of cost represented by the line (e.g., labour, material).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost centre responsible for the line expense.']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost code used for financial tracking of this line.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between revised and baseline cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the estimate line was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  estimate_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the estimate line.']
  estimate_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the estimate containing this line.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line has been soft‑deleted.']
  labor_grade varchar [note: 'Skill grade or classification of labour resources.']
  labor_rate_type varchar [note: 'Basis for labour cost calculation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the estimate.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the site or geographic location for the line.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of material used for the line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the line.']
  productivity_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier applied to account for expected productivity (e.g., 1.05).']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the resource required.']
  resource_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the resource.']
  retention_status varchar [note: 'Retention state of the line for contractual hold‑backs.']
  revised_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost after revisions or updates.']
  risk_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier reflecting risk or contingency applied to the line.']
  source_of_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Origin of the unit cost used for this line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the line.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated total cost (quantity × unit cost) before adjustments.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the resource in the selected currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the quantity (e.g., meters, kilograms).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the line.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the cost variance.']
  waste_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier to include anticipated material waste.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS code that groups this line within the project hierarchy.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the line.']

  Note: 'Detailed cost line item within a project estimate, capturing the granular cost build-up for a specific work package or activity. Stores cost code, WBS element, cost category (labour, material, plant, subcontract, indirect), resource description, quantity, unit, unit cost, total cost, productivity factor, waste factor, and source of rate (historical, vendor quote, published index). Enables detailed cost-to-complete analysis and estimate benchmarking.'
}

Table bid.submission {
  submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bid submission event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client/owner of the tender.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Bid Submission Compliance Checklist requires attaching the required permit; ensures bid proceeds only when permit is secured.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the tender.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns a cost center to bid submission expenses, required for post‑submission cost tracking and audit of bid‑related spend.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the submission.']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Bid submissions must include ESG reports for compliance; the FK records which ESG report accompanies each submission.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Required for the Bid Submission Tracking report, which records which subcontractor submitted each bid; essential for award decision and compliance audit.']
  itp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.itp. Business justification: Bid submission includes the Inspection Test Plan register for client review; linking ties each submission to its ITP.']
  jv_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.jv_partner. Business justification: Bid submission can be part of a joint venture; replace string with FK to jv_partner.']
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_baseline. Business justification: Bid submissions reference the baseline schedule for time‑based pricing and risk assessment, required for Bid Evaluation Dashboard.']
  acknowledgement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number received from the client confirming receipt.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the bid bond required for this submission.']
  bid_bond_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the bid bond.']
  bid_bond_type varchar [note: 'Type of bid bond provided.']
  bid_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount offered in the bid at submission.']
  bid_price_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment (e.g., discount, fee) applied to the base bid price.']
  bid_type varchar [note: 'Contractual pricing structure of the bid.']
  commercial_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned to the commercial portion of the bid.']
  compliance_requirements_met boolean [note: 'True if all mandatory compliance items were satisfied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid submission record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the bid price.']
  deadline date [note: 'Official deadline date for the tender submission.']
  documents_attached_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the submission.']
  estimated_duration_months varchar [note: 'Projected duration of the project in months as estimated in the bid.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate the bid (e.g., two‑envelope).']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'True if the bid is submitted as a joint venture.']
  late_submission_flag boolean [note: 'True if the submission was received after the deadline.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the submitter at time of submission.']
  number_of_copies varchar [note: 'Count of physical copies submitted, if applicable.']
  project_location varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the project site location.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the bid submission.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the project.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the bid based on evaluation criteria.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the bid submission.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bid submission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bid was formally submitted.']
  technical_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned to the technical portion of the bid (if two‑envelope).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bid submission record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal act of submitting a tender to a client or owner. Records submission timestamp, submission method (electronic portal, hard copy, email), submitted by (person), submission reference number, number of copies submitted, bid price at submission, technical score (if two-envelope), commercial score, submission acknowledgement reference, and late submission flag. Represents the definitive bid submission event for audit and compliance purposes.'
}

Table bid.clarification {
  clarification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each bid clarification record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Clarification log – clarifications raised during a tender are tied to the underlying project for compliance audit.']
  originating_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Tracks which internal employee originated a clarification request, essential for the Clarification Log and responsibility reporting.']
  originating_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party that originated the communication.']
  related_document_document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document (e.g., drawing, specification) associated with the clarification.']
  rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: Bid clarifications often arise from RFIs; linking a clarification to its originating RFI tracks the source of the question.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient has formally acknowledged receipt.']
  addendum_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the addendum referenced, if the communication is an addendum or amendment.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting files are attached to the clarification.']
  bid_revision_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clarification caused a formal bid revision.']
  clarification_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the clarification by the tendering authority.']
  clarification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clarification.']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Category of the communication exchanged during the tender period.']
  content varchar [note: 'Full text of the original communication request or notice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clarification record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the impact amount.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication originated from the client or the contractor.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the communication was originally issued.']
  impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the clarification on the bid.']
  impact_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the clarification affects bid parameters.']
  impact_flag_boq boolean [note: 'True if the clarification affects the Bill of Quantities.']
  impact_flag_price boolean [note: 'True if the clarification alters the bid price.']
  impact_flag_schedule boolean [note: 'True if the clarification changes the project schedule.']
  impact_flag_scope boolean [note: 'True if the clarification modifies the project scope.']
  impact_flag_specifications boolean [note: 'True if the clarification changes technical specifications or drawings.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Marks the clarification as critical to bid success.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Entity that issued the communication (e.g., client, architect, engineer).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by users.']
  response_content varchar [note: 'Full text of the response provided to the communication.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the recipient must respond to the communication.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the response process.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response was received or recorded.']
  revised_submission_deadline date [note: 'New deadline for bid submission if the clarification triggers a deadline change.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the clarification record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system (e.g., Procore, Aconex).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the communication subject.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the clarification record.']

  Note: 'Record of all formal tender-period communications and document modifications including RFIs (Requests for Information), clarifications, client-issued addenda, and amendments that modify the RFP/RFQ documents. Captures communication reference, type (RFI_issued, RFI_received, addendum, amendment, clarification), direction, addendum number (where applicable), issuing authority, subject, content, response, impact flags (bid price, schedule, scope, BOQ, specifications, drawings), revised submission deadline (if applicable), acknowledgement status, bid revision triggered, and status (open, answered, closed, incorporated). SSOT for all tender-period correspondence, addenda tracking, and document amendment audit trail ensuring tender compliance.'
}

Table bid.bond {
  bond_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bid bond record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project associated with the bid.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps bid bond liability to a GL account for proper accounting entry, essential for financial statements and bond guarantee reporting.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tender to which this bid bond is attached.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bid bond.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the bid bond.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid bond was formally approved by the authorized authority.']
  beneficiary varchar [note: 'Client or project owner that is the beneficiary of the bid bond.']
  bond_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the bid bond by the issuing entity.']
  bond_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bid bond.']
  bond_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bond instrument (e.g., bank guarantee, surety bond, insurance bond).']
  compliance_requirements_met boolean [note: 'True if the bond satisfies all regulatory and client‑specific compliance requirements.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bond details are marked as confidential for internal handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid bond record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the bond amount is expressed.']
  documents_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting bond documents have been attached to the record.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the bid bond expires or must be returned.']
  expiry_place varchar [note: 'Geographic location where the bond is to be returned or expires.']
  extension_count varchar [note: 'Total count of times the bond expiry has been extended.']
  guarantee_extension_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bond terms permit extensions.']
  guarantee_extension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for the most recent bond extension.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the bid bond was formally issued.']
  issue_place varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city/country) where the bond was issued.']
  issuer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the issuing entity: bank, surety, or insurance.']
  issuing_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the bank, surety, or insurance company that issued the bid bond.']
  last_extension_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent expiry extension.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update to the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the bid bond.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Bond amount expressed as a percentage of the total tender value.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating assigned to the bond based on financial and contractual factors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that supplied the bond data (e.g., SAP, Procore).']
  status_date date [note: 'Date on which the current status became effective.']
  total_extension_days varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of days added to the original expiry date through extensions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bid bond record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a bid bond (tender guarantee) instrument required as part of a tender submission. Captures bond reference number, issuing bank or surety, bond amount, bond currency, bond percentage of tender value, issue date, expiry date, bond type (bank guarantee, surety bond, insurance bond), beneficiary (client), bond status (issued, submitted, returned, forfeited, extended), and extension history. Tracks bond lifecycle from issuance through return or forfeiture.'
}

Table bid.win_loss_record {
  win_loss_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the win/loss record.']
  carbon_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_target. Business justification: After award, the project commits to a carbon reduction target; linking win/loss record to carbon_target captures this commitment.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project for which the tender was issued.']
  jv_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.jv_partner. Business justification: Win/loss record may reference the JV partner involved in the bid; replace string with FK to jv_partner.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bid associated with this win/loss outcome.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tender (RFP/RFQ) that generated the bid.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitor that won the tender when this record represents a loss.']
  awarded_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract awarded to the winning bidder.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the bid bond required for the tender.']
  bid_bond_type varchar [note: 'Form of security provided as a bid bond.']
  bid_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bid pricing strategy.']
  competitor_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct bidders that participated in the tender.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled completion date of the awarded contract.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date of the awarded contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the win/loss record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the awarded contract value.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the final decision for the tender was recorded.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate bids (e.g., technical, combined, price‑only).']
  evaluation_score_commercial decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned to the bid for commercial criteria (0‑100 scale).']
  evaluation_score_hsse decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for Health, Safety, Security, and Environment criteria (0‑100 scale).']
  evaluation_score_technical decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned to the bid for technical criteria (0‑100 scale).']
  is_award_confirmed boolean [note: 'True when the award has been formally confirmed by the client.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'True if the bid was submitted as a joint venture.']
  lessons_learned_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the document containing lessons learned from this bid.']
  loss_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of why the bid was not successful.']
  loss_reason_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text explanation of the loss reason.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current outcome of the tender competition (won, lost, withdrawn, or cancelled).']
  price_gap_to_winner decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between this bid\'s price and the winning bid price.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the win/loss record.']
  win_loss_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the win/loss record, used in reporting and communications.']
  winning_bid_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the winning bid, if disclosed by the client.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the outcome of a tender competition, recording whether the bid was won, lost, withdrawn, or cancelled. Stores outcome status, award date, awarded contract value, competitor count, winning bidder (if lost), winning bid price (if disclosed), price gap to winner, evaluation criteria scores (technical, commercial, HSSE), loss reason category, loss reason narrative, and lessons-learned reference. Feeds bid-to-award conversion rate analytics and competitive intelligence.'
}

Table bid.vendor_quote {
  vendor_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor_quote']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Vendor quotes for sustainable materials must reference the material record to verify sustainability credentials.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor providing the quote.']
  compliance_requirements_met varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quote satisfies required regulatory or project compliance criteria.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quote contains confidential or proprietary information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the quoted amount.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days required by the vendor to deliver the quoted items.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Standard Incoterms defining delivery responsibilities and costs.']
  documents_attached varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document identifiers linked to the quote.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score assigned during bid evaluation for this quote.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the quote.']
  qualifications_and_deviations varchar [note: 'Vendor qualifications, assumptions, or deviations from the specification.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of items, units, or work measured in the quote.']
  quote_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote was received from the vendor.']
  quote_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the vendor quote by the vendor or system.']
  quote_validity_date date [note: 'Date until which the quoted prices and terms remain valid.']
  quoted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount quoted by the vendor before taxes or fees.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if regulatory approval is needed for the quoted work or material.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approval.']
  scope_exclusions varchar [note: 'Items, services, or materials explicitly excluded from the quote.']
  scope_inclusions varchar [note: 'Items, services, or materials explicitly included in the quote.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work or material scope covered by the quote.']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical specification or drawing that the quote addresses.']
  total_quoted_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated total (quantity × unit price) before any discounts or taxes.']
  trade_or_material_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the trade, service, or material being quoted.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quoted quantity (e.g., m3, ton, pcs).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of the quoted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the quote record.']
  vendor_quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quote.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor providing the quote.']

  Note: 'Record of an external vendor quotation (subcontractor, material supplier, or specialist trade) received during bid preparation for incorporation into the project estimate. Captures quote reference, vendor name, vendor type (subcontractor, material_supplier, specialist_trade, plant_hire), trade or material description, specification reference, scope of work or material list, quoted amount, currency, quote validity date, quantity and unit of measure, unit price, total quoted value, scope inclusions, scope exclusions, qualifications and deviations, delivery lead time, delivery terms (Incoterms where applicable), and quote status (received, evaluated, accepted, rejected, expired). Supports cost build-up within the main tender estimate, vendor comparison analysis, and procurement handover upon award. SSOT for all external vendor pricing obtained during the bid phase.'
}

Table bid.bid_risk {
  bid_risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the bid risk record.']
  bid_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  climate_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.climate_risk_assessment. Business justification: Bid risk registers include climate risk assessments; the FK ties each risk entry to its detailed climate risk assessment.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Risk register – risks identified in a bid are tracked against the associated construction project for integrated risk management.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Risk register links each risk to a specific permit condition that may trigger compliance penalties.']
  primary_bid_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or organisation responsible for managing the risk.']
  related_bid_bid_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bid to which this risk is attached.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tender associated with the risk.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to evaluate the risk.']
  attached_documents varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document identifiers linked to the risk.']
  bid_risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date the risk was formally closed.']
  contingency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial reserve allocated to cover the risk in the bid price.']
  contingency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contingency expressed as a percentage of the estimated cost impact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk record was created in the system.']
  exposure_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary impact fields.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date the risk was first recorded.']
  impact_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact if the risk materialises, expressed in the project currency.']
  impact_quality varchar [note: 'Qualitative effect of the risk on project quality standards.']
  impact_schedule_days varchar [note: 'Estimated schedule delay in days caused by the risk.']
  is_key_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the risk is considered a key risk for the bid.']
  mitigation_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date for mitigation.']
  mitigation_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for mitigation activities.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned actions to reduce probability or impact of the risk.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by risk owners.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk, indicating whether it arises from internal processes or external factors.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Prioritisation level used for reporting and escalation.']
  probability_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood of the risk occurring, expressed as a qualitative rating.']
  residual_impact_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact remaining after mitigation.']
  residual_probability varchar [note: 'Likelihood of the risk after mitigation actions are applied.']
  residual_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score after mitigation, used for ongoing monitoring.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the risk is reviewed and re‑assessed.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the risk affecting the bid phase.']
  risk_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used by project teams to identify the risk in reports and documents.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature, cause and potential consequences of the risk.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score derived from probability and impact, used for prioritisation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the risk data (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief human‑readable title that summarises the risk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk record.']

  Note: 'Risk register entry specific to the bid phase, capturing identified risks and opportunities that influence bid pricing strategy and contingency allocation. Stores risk ID, risk category (commercial, technical, geotechnical, regulatory, supply chain, force majeure), risk description, probability rating, impact rating (cost, schedule, quality), risk score, mitigation strategy, contingency amount allocated, risk owner, and residual risk rating. Feeds bid pricing decisions and GMP contingency calculations.'
}

Table bid.bid_prequalification {
  bid_prequalification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each prequalification record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client for which the prequalification is held.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project or framework linked to the prequalification.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the last review.']
  jv_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.jv_partner. Business justification: Prequalification can be for a joint venture; replace string with FK to jv_partner.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Prequalification process validates that the contractor meets required regulatory obligations for the project.']
  sustainability_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_plan. Business justification: Pre‑qualification checks require a sustainability plan; linking prequalification to the plan records compliance.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the required bid bond.']
  bid_bond_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the bid bond amount.']
  bid_bond_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the bid bond must be released or returned.']
  bid_bond_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a bid bond must be posted for this prequalification.']
  bid_prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prequalification.']
  confidentiality_flag varchar [note: 'Classification of the prequalification record for data handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prequalification record was first created in the system.']
  documents_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents have been uploaded.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the prequalification becomes effective for tender participation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the prequalification ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) from the client’s evaluation of the prequalification.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the prequalification expires if not renewed.']
  financial_capacity_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the financial capacity threshold.']
  financial_capacity_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum financial capacity (in currency) required to be eligible.']
  hsse_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) summarising health, safety, security and environmental performance.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'True if the prequalification is submitted as part of a joint‑venture partnership.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the prequalification was last reviewed for renewal or amendment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the prequalification.']
  prequalification_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the prequalification based on scope and partnership type.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of goods or services the prequalification covers.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the prequalification (e.g., PQ-2023-001).']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if external regulatory approval is needed for the prequalification.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approval.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the prequalification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the prequalification was submitted to the client.']
  technical_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting technical capability to deliver the work.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the prequalification record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the prequalification record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking the prequalification (PQ) status of the business for a specific client, project, or procurement category. Captures PQ reference, client name, project or framework name, PQ submission date, PQ expiry date, PQ status (submitted, approved, conditionally approved, rejected, expired), financial capacity threshold, technical capacity score, HSSE performance score, and prequalification category. Ensures the business maintains current PQ standing to participate in tenders.'
}

Table bid.jv_partner {
  jv_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the JV partner record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the partner\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the partner\'s mailing address.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the partner\'s address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the partner\'s headquarters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the JV partner record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for financial capacity amount.']
  equity_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of equity ownership contributed by the partner.']
  financial_capacity_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount representing the partner\'s financial capacity contribution.']
