  jv_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the JV agreement document.']
  jv_partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the partner organization participating in the joint venture.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional information about the partner.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the partner in the JV process.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner\'s role in the JV (lead, junior, specialist).']
  past_project_references varchar [note: 'List or description of past projects the partner has provided as references.']
  prequalification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the partner\'s prequalification for the project.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary individual contact for the JV partner.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary contact.']
  role_in_jv varchar [note: 'Specific role the partner plays within the joint venture.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Description of the work scope the partner is responsible for in the bid.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the partner\'s address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the JV partner record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a Joint Venture (JV) or consortium partner engaged for a specific bid. Captures partner company name, partner role (lead partner, junior partner, specialist partner), equity share percentage, scope of work contribution, prequalification status, financial capacity contribution, country of origin, past project references provided, JV agreement reference, and partner status (active, withdrawn). Tracks JV formation and partner contributions specific to the bid phase.'
}

Table bid.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the bid approval record.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_assessment. Business justification: Bid approval decisions incorporate results of a compliance assessment report to ensure regulatory fit.']
  bid_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.bid_opportunity. Business justification: Bid approval should be linked to the originating bid opportunity for traceability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the individual or role that made the approval decision.']
  sustainability_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_plan. Business justification: Bid approval process validates alignment with the sustainability plan; the FK captures the specific plan reviewed.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the approval event per internal governance policy.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the approval record.']
  approved_bid_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final approved monetary amount for the bid.']
  approved_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved profit margin expressed as a percentage of the bid price.']
  bonding_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the company\'s ability to provide required bid bonds.']
  client_relationship_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the strength of the relationship with the client.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Any contractual or operational conditions attached to the approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid approval record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the approved bid price.']
  deadline date [note: 'Date by which the approved bid must be submitted.']
  decision_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or entity authorizing the decision.']
  decision_authority_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role or title of the decision authority.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the approval process (e.g., bid, no_bid, conditional_bid, approved, approved_with_conditions, rejected, deferred). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bid|no_bid|conditional_bid|approved|approved_with_conditions|rejected|deferred — promote to reference product]']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the reasoning behind the decision.']
  decision_stage varchar [note: 'Governance stage at which the decision was taken.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  delegation_of_authority_level varchar [note: 'Authorized delegation tier that permitted the decision.']
  is_conditional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval is subject to conditions.']
  margin_potential_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected profitability score based on estimated margins.']
  pursuit_investment_justification varchar [note: 'Business case narrative justifying the investment of resources into the bid pursuit.']
  resource_availability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the availability of required resources for the project.']
  risk_profile_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing the risk exposure of the bid.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the bid pursuit.']
  strategic_fit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring alignment of the bid with corporate strategy.']
  total_governance_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregated score from all governance criteria used to evaluate the bid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bid approval record.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Expiration date of the approval if not acted upon.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal bid governance decision event within the internal authorization workflow, capturing the complete pursuit lifecycle from initial bid/no-bid decision through estimating review, commercial review, risk review, and executive sign-off. Stores approval stage (bid_no_bid, estimate_review, commercial_review, risk_review, executive_signoff), decision date, decision outcome (bid, no-bid, conditional_bid, approved, approved_with_conditions, rejected, deferred), decision authority (approver name and role), scoring against governance criteria (client relationship, risk profile, resource availability, margin potential, strategic fit, bonding capacity), total governance score, risk rating, approved bid price, approved margin percentage, conditions imposed, decision rationale narrative, delegation of authority level, and pursuit investment justification. SSOT for all bid governance decisions including pursuit authorization, resource allocation, and complete audit trail from opportunity pursuit through final submission authority.'
}

Table bid.response {
  response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for response']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subcontractor submitting the bid response.']
  revised_response_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on response (revised_response_id)']
  trade_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trade package (RFQ) to which this bid response relates.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments to the gross bid amount (e.g., taxes, fees, discounts).']
  award_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the awarded contract value.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date on which the bid was formally awarded to the subcontractor.']
  award_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the contract awarded based on the winning bid.']
  bid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount requested by the subcontractor in the bid (gross).']
  bid_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code assigned to the bid response, used for tracking and communication.']
  bid_type varchar [note: 'Method used to price the bid: lump‑sum, line‑item breakdown, or unit‑price schedule.']
  bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the required performance bond.']
  bond_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a performance bond is required for this bid.']
  clarification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any clarifications requested for the bid.']
  commercial_ranking varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the bid based on commercial criteria such as price and payment terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid response record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the bid amount.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum insurance coverage amount required for the bid.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether insurance coverage is required for the subcontractor.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after adjustments, representing the amount the subcontractor expects to be paid.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional comments or special conditions attached to the bid.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the bid evaluation process.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice receipt that payment is due.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bid response. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|clarified|evaluated|shortlisted|awarded|unsuccessful|withdrawn|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the contract value retained until final acceptance.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bid response was submitted by the subcontractor.']
  technical_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting how well the bid meets technical specifications and requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bid response record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the bid amount is valid; after this date the bid expires.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the bid amount is considered valid.']

  Note: 'Bid/quotation submissions received from subcontractors in response to trade package RFQs. Captures bid reference, responding subcontractor, trade package, bid amount, bid breakdown (by BOQ line or lump-sum), bid validity period, submission date, clarification status, technical compliance score, commercial ranking, and bid outcome (shortlisted, awarded, unsuccessful, withdrawn). Enables competitive analysis and audit trail of the subcontractor selection process from RFQ to award.'
}

Table bid.lien_waiver {
  lien_waiver_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the lien waiver record.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subcontractor that issued the lien waiver.']
  payment_application_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment application to which this lien waiver is linked.']
  superseded_lien_waiver_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lien_waiver (superseded_lien_waiver_id)']
  compliance_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waiver satisfies all statutory and contractual compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lien waiver record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the waiver amount.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the lien waiver document.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date the lien waiver was signed and became effective.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the lien waiver was filed with the jurisdictional authority.']
  filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the filing of the lien waiver with the appropriate authority.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state or jurisdiction where the lien waiver is legally effective.']
  notarization_date date [note: 'Date the notarization of the lien waiver was completed.']
  notarization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the waiver has been notarized.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the lien waiver.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lien waiver record.']
  waiver_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount covered by the lien waiver.']
  waiver_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the lien waiver by the contractor or project system.']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lien waiver.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the waiver is conditional, unconditional, progress, or final.']

  Note: 'Lien waiver and statutory declaration records obtained from subcontractors as a condition of progress and final payments. Captures waiver type (conditional, unconditional, progress, final), subcontract reference, payment application reference, waiver amount, execution date, notarization status, and filing reference. Required in most US jurisdictions and analogous instruments (statutory declarations) in other markets to protect the GC from mechanics lien exposure.'
}

Table bid.bid_team_assignment {
  bid_team_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the BidTeamAssignment association']
  bid_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the bid opportunity']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee']
  allocation_percentage decimal [note: 'Percentage of the employee\'s time allocated to the bid opportunity']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s assignment to the bid opportunity ends']
  role varchar [note: 'The role of the employee in the bid team (e.g., Bid Manager, Cost Estimator)']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s assignment to the bid opportunity begins']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of employees to bid opportunities, capturing each employee\'s role, allocation percentage, and the start and end dates of the assignment. Each record links one bid opportunity to one employee.. Existence Justification: Bid opportunities are staffed by multiple employees, each with a defined role, allocation percentage, and assignment dates. Employees can be assigned to many bid opportunities over time. The assignment itself is a managed business entity that captures these details.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_permit {
  compliance_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the permit.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Permit issuance requires linking each permit to the client company that holds it for reporting and compliance tracking.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the permit.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Permit tracking requires linking each permit to the responsible subcontractor firm for compliance monitoring.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Required for Permit Management process where a designated employee oversees permit issuance and compliance, enabling tracking of responsible staff.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.regulatory_authority']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Required for Permit Management to record which vendor holds the permit, enabling compliance tracking of contractor responsibilities.']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to stored evidence satisfying permit conditions.']
  compliance_permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated compliance status of all permit conditions.']
  condition_count varchar [note: 'Number of conditions attached to the permit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the permit record was first created.']
  documents_attached_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the permit application.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the permit becomes effective (may differ from issue date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the permit remains in effect (nullable for open-ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the permit expires or must be renewed.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee associated with the permit.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fee.']
  fee_paid_date date [note: 'Date the permit fee was paid.']
  fee_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit fee has been paid.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the permit is currently active (not expired, revoked, or suspended).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the permit was officially issued.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the permit record.']
  next_condition_due_date date [note: 'Earliest due date among pending permit conditions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal comments or observations.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'Category of the permit based on ownership or project type.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number assigned by issuing authority.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the kind of permit.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which renewal must be submitted.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit requires renewal.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date the permit was revoked, if applicable.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level associated with the permit.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the permit application was submitted to the authority.']
  suspension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the permit is currently suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for permit suspension.']

  Note: 'Master register of all construction permits across the full lifecycle from application through expiry, including building permits, excavation permits, environmental permits, utility connection permits, demolition permits, and occupancy permits. Tracks application submissions (applicant details, supporting documents, authority response, resubmissions, appeals), authority review and approval/rejection, issuance, individual conditions attached (each with due date, responsible party, fulfilment status, and compliance evidence), condition compliance monitoring, renewal, expiry, revocation, and suspension. Captures permit number, issuing authority, permit type, project association, submission date, issue date, expiry date, renewal requirements, and current status. Serves as the SSOT for permit identity, permit application history, and permit condition compliance across all project sites.'
}

Table compliance.permit_application {
  permit_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the permit application record.']
  applicant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (person or organization) submitting the permit application.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Permit applications result in a compliance permit; linking them avoids data duplication. Overlapping columns are removed from permit_application.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project associated with the permit.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated environmental impact assessment record.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Permit applications are often submitted by subcontractors; linking to firm_profile enables audit of applicant compliance.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date on which an appeal against a decision can be filed.']
  appeal_filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when an appeal was submitted.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the permit application by the submitting organization.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the authority approved the permit.']
  compliance_requirements_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the application satisfies all regulatory compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the permit application record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the fee.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the authority on the application.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification provided for the authority\'s decision.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approved permit expires.']
  fee_adjustment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional adjustments to the base fee such as taxes, surcharges, or discounts.']
  fee_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount payable after applying adjustments.']
  is_resubmission boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the application is a resubmission after a prior rejection or withdrawal.']
  leeds_certification_level varchar [note: 'LEED sustainability certification level applicable to the project.']
  permit_application_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work or activity for which the permit is sought.']
  permit_application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the permit application.']
  resubmission_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided for why the application is being resubmitted.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the application was formally submitted to the authority.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the application.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the permit application record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last day the permit remains valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First day the permit becomes effective.']
  work_scope varchar [note: 'Brief description of the work scope covered by the permit.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each permit application submitted to a regulatory or municipal authority, capturing application reference, submission date, applicant details, supporting documents submitted, application status, authority response, and approval or rejection outcome. Tracks the full lifecycle from initial submission through approval, including resubmissions and appeals.'
}

Table compliance.permit_condition {
  permit_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the permit condition record.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Each permit condition belongs to a specific permit; adding permit_id creates the required parent link and eliminates the silo.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Managing permit conditions assigns a specific client contact as the responsible party, needed for condition monitoring and notifications.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Needed for Condition Enforcement process; assigns a specific employee to monitor and report on each permit condition, replacing denormalized name fields.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the condition must be satisfied.']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to digital evidence supporting compliance.']
  compliance_status_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the current compliance status.']
  condition_action_required varchar [note: 'Specific action(s) required to satisfy the condition.']
  condition_category varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping of the condition (e.g., environmental, safety).']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'External code or identifier assigned to the condition by the regulatory authority.']
  condition_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the official document where the condition is recorded.']
  condition_fine_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary fine associated with non‑compliance, if applicable.']
  condition_fine_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fine amount.']
  condition_is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the condition is mandatory (cannot be waived).']
  condition_is_waivable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the condition can be waived under certain circumstances.']
  condition_last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  condition_last_updated_by_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user or system that performed the most recent update (e.g., compliance_officer, system).']
  condition_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating the importance of the condition.']
  condition_reference_number varchar [note: 'Alternate reference number for the condition, if used by the authority.']
  condition_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the condition was resolved or closed.']
  condition_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing how the condition was resolved.']
  condition_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review of the condition.']
  condition_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status of the condition.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Category of the condition, such as environmental, safety, financial, schedule, or quality.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the condition becomes effective.']
  evidence_required varchar [note: 'Description of documentation or evidence needed to prove compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the condition expires or is no longer applicable (nullable).']
  inspection_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection related to the condition.']
  inspection_next_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical inspection is required for this condition.']
  last_report_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance report submitted.']
  next_report_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance report.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes related to the condition.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount if the condition is not met.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  permit_condition_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the condition and its obligations.']
  permit_condition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compliance evidence must be reported.']
  source_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issued the condition (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the condition record.']

  Note: 'Records individual conditions, restrictions, and obligations attached to an issued permit by the regulatory authority. Each condition has its own compliance obligation, due date, responsible party, and fulfilment status. Enables tracking of permit condition compliance separately from the permit itself, supporting audit readiness and authority inspections.'
}

Table compliance.env_impact_assessment {
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for env_impact_assessment']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Supports Environmental Impact Oversight where an internal compliance officer is accountable for assessment outcomes and regulatory follow‑up.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which this assessment belongs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the assessment received formal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the assessment with the regulator.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the environmental impact assessment was formally conducted.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the assessment by the organization or regulator.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment (e.g., baseline, impact, post‑construction, ongoing monitoring).']
  assessment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assessment document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  consultant_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the environmental consultant for correspondence.']
  consultant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the external environmental consultant or firm responsible for the assessment.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action triggered by the last exceedance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  environmental_category varchar [note: 'Primary environmental domain(s) addressed by the assessment.']
  epa_report_submitted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the required EPA report for this assessment has been submitted.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the key findings and conclusions of the assessment.']
  iso_14001_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 14001 environmental management system standards.']
  leeds_certified boolean [note: 'True if the project has achieved LEED certification related to the assessment.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'List of required mitigation actions to address identified environmental impacts.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of the assessment.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the assessment record.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the construction project associated with the assessment.']
  regulatory_submission_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the submission made to the regulatory authority (e.g., EPA filing number).']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory review cycles for the assessment.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the assessment based on potential environmental impact.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the geographic and functional scope covered by the assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA), Environmental Management Plans (EMP), and ongoing environmental monitoring programs for construction projects. Captures assessment type, scope, environmental consultant, assessment date, findings summary, mitigation measures required, regulatory submission reference, approval status, and review cycle. Includes full environmental monitoring history: air quality measurements, noise level readings, water discharge sampling, soil contamination checks, dust suppression monitoring, with monitoring date, location, parameter measured, recorded value, regulatory threshold, exceedance flag, and corrective action triggered for each monitoring event. Supports EPA reporting, ISO 14001 surveillance, and demonstrates ongoing environmental compliance through continuous monitoring evidence.'
}

Table compliance.env_monitoring {
  env_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for env_monitoring']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the monitoring activity.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: Environmental monitoring events are performed against a specific impact assessment. Adding env_impact_assessment_id to env_monitoring captures this link.']
  recorded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who logged the monitoring record.']
  site_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site where the monitoring activity occurred.']
  work_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work package or WBS element linked to the monitoring event.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'Employee identifier who initially created the record.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch of samples or readings, if applicable.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or observations related to the monitoring event.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination for the measurement.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action triggered by an exceedance.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the monitoring record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the quality assessment of the recorded data.']
  env_monitoring_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the monitoring record.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the equipment used for the measurement.']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value exceeds the regulatory threshold.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the measurement location.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the monitoring location (e.g., "North perimeter, Zone A").']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the measurement location.']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the measurement.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to obtain the measurement.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the recorded value (e.g., µg/m³, dB, pH).']
  monitoring_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the environmental measurement was taken.']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Category of environmental parameter being monitored.']
  parameter varchar [note: 'Specific environmental parameter measured (e.g., PM2.5, dB, pH).']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Authority whose regulations apply to the measurement.']
  sample_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual sample taken.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor or instrument that captured the measurement.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold value.']
  threshold_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regulatory or project-specific limit for the parameter.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the monitoring record.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Brief description of weather at the time of measurement (e.g., sunny, windy).']

  Note: 'Transactional records of environmental monitoring activities conducted on construction sites, including air quality measurements, noise level readings, water discharge sampling, soil contamination checks, and dust suppression monitoring. Captures monitoring date, location, parameter measured, recorded value, regulatory threshold, exceedance flag, and corrective action triggered. Supports EPA reporting and ISO 14001 surveillance.'
}

Table compliance.leed_certification {
  leed_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the LEED certification record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Links LEED certification to the employee responsible for ensuring compliance with green building criteria, required for audit trails.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with this LEED certification pursuit.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date the LEED certification was officially granted.']
  certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issued LEED certificate document.']
  certification_level_awarded varchar [note: 'Certification level actually awarded after final review; \'None\' if not awarded.']
  certification_level_target varchar [note: 'Desired certification level the project aims to achieve (e.g., Certified, Silver, Gold, Platinum).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Specific LEED rating system applicable to the project (e.g., New Construction, Existing Buildings).']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issues the LEED certification, typically the U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC).']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance verification performed internally.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the project with LEED requirements.']
  evidence_submitted_date date [note: 'Date when supporting evidence for credit claims was uploaded to the certification portal.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the awarded LEED certification expires, if applicable.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last review action performed by the certifying body.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the certification process.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes related to the certification process.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the project relevant to the certification effort.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the LEED certification record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the LEED certification record.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the certifying body when the project is entered into the LEED system.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the project\'s registration in the LEED system (e.g., registered, withdrawn, expired).']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Status of the certification renewal process.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the certifying body\'s review of the submitted evidence.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the reviewer during the certification assessment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the LEED certification pursuit officially begins.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the certification application and supporting documentation were submitted to the certifying body.']
  submission_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or code used by the certifying body for the submission package.']
  target_award_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the certification is expected to be awarded.']
  total_points_available decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of points that can be earned across all credit categories for this project.']
  total_points_awarded decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual number of points earned after the certifying body review.']
  total_points_targeted decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of points the project plans to achieve based on credit selection.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the certification record.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification pursuits for construction projects with full credit-level scorecard management. Captures certification level targeted (Certified, Silver, Gold, Platinum), certifying body, project registration number, and manages individual credits within each credit category (Energy & Atmosphere, Water Efficiency, Indoor Environmental Quality, Materials & Resources, Sustainable Sites, Innovation). Each credit tracks points available, points targeted, points awarded, evidence submitted, review status, and reviewer comments. Also captures submission date, certification award date, and certificate reference. Serves as the SSOT for LEED certification status and granular credit scorecard progress. Supports U.S. Green Building Council reporting.'
}

Table compliance.leed_credit {
  leed_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the LEED credit record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with this credit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the LEED reviewer who performed the assessment.']
  leed_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.leed_certification. Business justification: LEED credits are defined within a LEED certification. Adding leed_certification_id to leed_credit models the parent‑child relationship.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or LEED prerequisite references applicable to this credit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit record was first created.']
  credit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level LEED credit category (e.g., Energy & Atmosphere, Water Efficiency).']
  credit_code varchar [note: 'Standard LEED credit identifier code (e.g., EQ‑1, WE‑2).']
  credit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the credit requirements and intent.']
  credit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the specific LEED credit.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation or files stored in the document management system.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual description of the criteria that must be satisfied to be eligible for the credit.']
  evidence_submission_date date [note: 'Date the evidence was submitted to the LEED reviewer.']
  evidence_submitted varchar [note: 'Reference or description of the evidence package submitted for this credit.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project meets the basic eligibility criteria for this credit.']
  leed_credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit record.']
  points_available decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum points that can be earned for this credit.']
  points_awarded decimal(5,2) [note: 'Points actually awarded after review.']
  points_targeted decimal(5,2) [note: 'Points the project aims to achieve for this credit.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the review decision was recorded.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent review of the submitted evidence.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Comments provided by the LEED reviewer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system where the credit data originated (e.g., Procore, BIM 360).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit evidence for the credit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit record.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual LEED credit categories and specific credits being pursued within a LEED certification effort. Records credit category (e.g., Energy & Atmosphere, Water Efficiency, Indoor Environmental Quality), credit name, points available, points targeted, evidence submitted, review status, points awarded, and reviewer comments. Enables granular tracking of LEED scorecard progress.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_submission {
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory submission record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or organization that prepared the submission.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Regulatory filing fees are recorded in the general ledger; linking submission to a GL account enables proper financial posting.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Regulatory Incident Reporting: each safety incident triggers a regulatory submission record filed with authorities.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: Regulatory submissions are made to a specific authority; adding regulatory_authority_id captures this relationship and allows removal of duplicated authority fields.']
  related_contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract associated with the submission.']
  related_project_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the submission relates.']
  acknowledgement_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the acknowledgement receipt, if any.']
  acknowledgement_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an acknowledgement was received from the regulator.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if any supporting documents are attached.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level compliance domain the submission addresses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the fee amount.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the submission.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the regulatory body.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged by the regulatory body for processing the submission.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission contains confidential information.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the submission.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the period covered by the submission.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'First day of the period covered by the submission.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., reason for rejection.']
  submission_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission was sent to the regulatory body.']
  submission_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the submission content.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the submission.']
  submission_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured at submission time.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the submission by the originating system.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory filing, such as annual report or incident notification.']
  submitter_role varchar [note: 'Role of the submitter within the project organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the submission record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal regulatory submissions made to governing bodies including OSHA, EPA, local building authorities, and environmental agencies. Captures submission type (annual report, incident notification, permit renewal, compliance certificate), submission date, regulatory body, submission reference number, reporting period, submission method, acknowledgement received, and submission status. Serves as the SSOT for all outbound regulatory filings.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory obligation record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Regulatory reporting tracks which client accounts are subject to each obligation, enabling account‑level compliance dashboards.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Obligation‑related expenses are allocated to a cost center, supporting cost‑center level compliance cost analysis.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Regulatory obligations are assigned to subcontractors; the FK tracks which firm must satisfy each obligation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Penalties or costs arising from obligations are posted to a GL account for accurate financial impact tracking.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Assigns each regulatory obligation to a specific organizational unit for accountability and reporting in compliance governance.']
  primary_regulatory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party responsible for ensuring compliance.']
  related_contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract or agreement that references this regulatory obligation.']
  related_project_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this regulatory obligation is linked.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Obligations are assigned to specific vendors; linking allows monitoring vendor compliance with regulatory requirements.']
  compliance_evidence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compliance evidence submission.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the obligation record was created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of the obligation data per organizational policy.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation becomes effective.']
  evidence_required varchar [note: 'Description of documentation or evidence needed to demonstrate compliance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation expires or is no longer applicable, if applicable.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the obligation is currently active (true) or retired (false).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the obligation is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction (country or region) where the obligation applies.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was last reviewed for compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations about the obligation.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'External reference code or number assigned by the regulatory body or contract.']
  obligation_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the regulatory requirement.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount for non-compliance, if applicable.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty associated with the obligation.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority or standard that issued the obligation.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory compliance reviews.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with non-compliance of the obligation.']
  scope_business_units varchar [note: 'Business units or divisions affected by the obligation.']
  scope_projects varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of project identifiers to which the obligation applies.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original legal or contractual document that defines the obligation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the obligation record.']

  Note: 'Master register of all regulatory obligations applicable to the organization and its construction projects, derived from applicable laws, standards, permits, and contractual requirements. Captures obligation source (OSHA 29 CFR, EPA Clean Water Act, IBC/IRC, ISO 45001, GDPR, PCI DSS, FIDIC, local building codes), obligation description, applicable jurisdiction, effective date, review frequency, responsible owner, compliance evidence required, current compliance status, and organizational scope mapping (projects, business units, jurisdictions). Serves as the single compliance obligation inventory underpinning all compliance activities, assessments, calendar deadlines, and regulatory change impact analysis.'
}

Table compliance.assessment {
  assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance assessment record.']
  audit_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the detailed audit report linked to this assessment.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the assessment applies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or consultant who performed the assessment.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the assessment (e.g., CA‑2023‑001).']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance assessment was conducted.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of compliance assessment performed.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the assessor.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the assessment.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit trail information.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of compliance (e.g., environmental, safety).']
  compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall compliance result based on the assessment.']
  compliance_status_overall varchar [note: 'Aggregated compliance status across all obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary values.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for supporting documentation (e.g., file ID).']
  external_audit_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the external firm that conducted the audit.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of key findings from the assessment.']
  gaps_identified varchar [note: 'Specific compliance gaps or deficiencies discovered.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment result is considered critical for the project.']
  is_external_audit boolean [note: 'True if the assessment was performed by an external auditor.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the jurisdiction.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party who performed the most recent review of the assessment.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to mitigate identified compliance risks.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent compliance assessment.']
  obligations_assessed varchar [note: 'List or description of regulatory obligations evaluated during the assessment.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed for non‑compliance, if applicable.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the project.']
  rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the degree of compliance.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Suggested remediation steps to close identified gaps.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or standard governing the assessment.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the assessment findings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the scope and boundaries of the compliance assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Periodic compliance assessment records evaluating the organization\'s or a project\'s adherence to regulatory obligations, internal policies, and permit conditions. Captures assessment date, scope, assessor, obligations assessed, findings, compliance rating, gaps identified, and recommended actions. Distinct from quality NCRs (owned by quality domain) and safety audits (owned by safety domain) — this covers regulatory and legal compliance only.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_action {
  compliance_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance action record.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Compliance actions (e.g., corrective measures) are assigned to the specific scheduled activity they address, supporting traceability of remediation.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the permit whose condition was breached, if applicable.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Compliance Action Tracking Report requires linking each corrective action to the project it addresses.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Expenses from compliance actions are charged to a specific cost center, enabling cost‑center based reporting of compliance spend.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document or file that provides proof of action completion.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Compliance actions (e.g., corrective measures) are owned by a subcontractor; linking records ownership and accountability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Compliance actions incur costs that must be posted to a GL account for financial reporting; linking ensures accurate expense accounting.']
  owner_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Maps compliance actions to the owning org unit, replacing free‑text department field to enable unit‑level performance metrics.']
  primary_compliance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or external party accountable for executing the action.']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: Budgeting of compliance actions is tracked within the project budget to control allocated compliance funds.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Compliance actions (e.g., corrective measures) are often triggered by a particular purchase order that caused a non‑conformance.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Compliance actions address regulatory obligations; many actions can belong to one obligation. Adding regulatory_obligation_id to compliance_action enables this relationship.']
  related_audit_iso_audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit or inspection that uncovered the issue.']
  source_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific finding, observation, or notice that originated the action.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the corrective or preventive action, often used in reports and communications.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the action as corrective, preventive, or improvement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the action was formally approved.']
  closure_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Web link or path to the evidence confirming action closure.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks added by stakeholders.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective or preventive work was finished.']
  compliance_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective or preventive work to be performed.']
  compliance_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compliance action.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Domain of compliance impacted (e.g., safety, environment, quality).']
  corrective_measure varchar [note: 'Specific steps or measures that will address the identified non‑conformance.']
  cost_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual monetary cost incurred after action completion.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost to implement the action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the compliance action was initially recorded.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the action must be completed to satisfy the compliance requirement.']
  external_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external regulatory body or agency.']
  external_notice_date date [note: 'Date the external authority issued the notice or citation.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if the action is driven by an external regulator or agency.']
  is_repeat_action boolean [note: 'True if this action is a repeat of a previously recorded issue.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the action record was last reviewed for relevance or accuracy.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Long‑term plan to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  monitoring_end_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring period ends.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the post‑action monitoring should occur.']
  monitoring_required boolean [note: 'True if ongoing monitoring is required after closure.']
  monitoring_start_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring period begins.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level indicating urgency.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulation, standard, or permit clause that triggered the action (e.g., OSHA 1910.120).']
  repeat_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the same issue has been recorded.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk severity associated with the non‑compliance.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the underlying cause that triggered the compliance action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the action record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the completed action was verified as effective and compliant.']

  Note: 'Corrective and preventive actions raised as a result of compliance assessments, regulatory findings, permit condition breaches, or authority notices. Tracks action description, root cause, responsible party, due date, priority, completion status, evidence of closure, and verification date. Distinct from safety corrective actions (owned by safety domain via Intelex) — this covers regulatory compliance remediation only.'
}

Table compliance.authority_notice {
  authority_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the authority notice record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project affected by the notice.']
  daily_log_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site/location where the notice applies.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a stored document (e.g., scanned notice) linked to this record.']
  appeal_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal was lodged.']
  appeal_lodged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal against the notice has been filed.']
  authority_notice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notice.']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the issuing authority by jurisdiction level.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the compliance issue.']
  compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name of the internal officer responsible for managing the notice response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notice record was first captured in the system.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body that issued the notice (e.g., OSHA, EPA, local council).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  notice_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory authority issued the notice.']
  notice_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the issuing authority to the notice.']
  notice_type varchar [note: 'Category of the notice describing its nature.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty imposed by the authority, if any.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the penalty amount.']
  penalty_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the penalty must be paid.']
  regulation_breached varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, code, or standard that was violated.']
  remediation_required varchar [note: 'Actions required by the authority to remediate the violation.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the notice was formally resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome after the notice was addressed.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'Date by which a formal response to the notice must be submitted.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the response was submitted.']
  response_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a response has been submitted to the authority.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the notice by the authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the notice record.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the regulatory violation cited in the notice.']

  Note: 'Records formal notices, orders, infringement notices, improvement notices, prohibition notices, stop-work orders, and directives received from regulatory authorities including OSHA, EPA, local councils, fire marshals, and building inspectors. Captures notice type, severity level, issuing authority, notice date, project or site affected, violation described, applicable regulation breached, required remediation, response deadline, response submitted, appeal lodged flag, and resolution outcome. Critical for legal risk management, regulatory relationship tracking, and insurance notification obligations.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_obligation {
  privacy_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the privacy obligation record.']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated breach incident record.']
  breach_date date [note: 'Date on which the personal data breach occurred.']
  breach_discovery_method varchar [note: 'How the breach was discovered.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the breach incident.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when consent was obtained.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which consent was obtained.']
  consent_required boolean [note: 'True if explicit consent is required for the processing activity.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of consent for the data subject.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent form or policy.']
  consent_withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the data subject withdrew consent, if applicable.']
  cross_border_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes where data is transferred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|DEU|FRA|... — promote to reference product]']
  cross_border_transfer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA.']
  data_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the personal data (e.g., personal, special category, pseudonymised).']
  data_controller varchar [note: 'Internal department or entity responsible for determining the purposes of processing.']
  data_processor varchar [note: 'External party that processes personal data on behalf of the controller.']
  data_subject_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the individuals whose personal data is covered (e.g., employee, subcontractor worker, client contact).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the privacy obligation becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the privacy obligation expires or is no longer applicable (null if open‑ended).']
  individuals_notified boolean [note: 'True if affected data subjects were notified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was last reviewed for compliance.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Lawful basis for processing personal data as defined by GDPR.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information related to the privacy obligation.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which notifications were sent to authorities or individuals.']
  notification_obligation_triggered boolean [note: 'True if the 72‑hour breach notification obligation was triggered.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'External reference code or number assigned to the privacy obligation for tracking and reporting.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the privacy obligation, such as consent management, data retention, breach handling, legal basis documentation, cross‑border transfer, or other.']
  privacy_obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the obligation.']
  processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which the personal data is processed under this obligation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy obligation record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the privacy obligation record.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions taken after the breach.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data must be retained before deletion or anonymisation.']
  supervisory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supervisory authority that was notified.']
  supervisory_authority_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relevant supervisory authority was notified.']

  Note: 'Master register of GDPR and privacy obligations for the organizations processing of construction workforce and client personal data, with integrated consent management and incident tracking. Captures data subject categories (employee, subcontractor worker, client contact), processing purposes, legal basis, data retention periods, cross-border transfer requirements. Manages consent records (data subject, consent purpose, date, method, version, withdrawal, processing activities covered) and privacy incident/breach records (incident date, breach type, data subjects affected, categories of personal data involved, discovery method, notification obligations triggered, supervisory authority notification, affected individuals notified, remediation actions). Supports GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA), 72-hour breach notification obligations, lawful basis documentation, and consent management for construction workforce data processing.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_incident {
  privacy_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the privacy incident record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Privacy Incident Management records the project where the incident occurred for reporting and remediation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reported the incident.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the privacy incident.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the privacy breach.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to prevent recurrence.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated fine.']
  data_category varchar [note: 'Category of personal data involved in the breach.']
  data_retention_action varchar [note: 'Action taken regarding retained data after the breach.']
  data_subject_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the affected individual.']
  data_subject_reference varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the affected data subject.']
  data_subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual whose personal data was affected.']
  data_volume_bytes varchar [note: 'Total size in bytes of the data affected.']
  data_volume_records varchar [note: 'Number of individual records affected by the breach.']
  discovery_method varchar [note: 'How the privacy incident was discovered.']
  estimated_fine_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary penalty or fine associated with the breach.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of business and regulatory impact resulting from the breach.']
  incident_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the incident was formally closed.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the privacy incident.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the privacy incident.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the privacy breach occurred or was discovered.']
  individuals_notified_flag boolean [note: 'True if affected individuals were notified.']
  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident is under legal hold.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when affected individuals were notified.']
  notification_obligation_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory notification obligations (e.g., GDPR 72‑hour rule) were triggered.']
  privacy_incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the privacy incident.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incident record.']
  regulatory_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory report was submitted.']
  regulatory_report_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal regulatory report has been filed.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to remediate the breach.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort.']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who reported the incident.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) assigned after assessment.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis of why the breach occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the incident was initially captured.']
  supervisory_authority_notified_date date [note: 'Date the supervisory authority was notified, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of personal data breaches, privacy incidents, and unauthorized disclosures involving workforce or client data. Captures incident date, data subjects affected, categories of personal data involved, breach type, discovery method, notification obligations triggered, supervisory authority notification date, affected individuals notified flag, and remediation actions taken. Supports GDPR 72-hour breach notification obligations.'
}

Table compliance.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the consent record.']
  privacy_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_obligation. Business justification: Consent records are tied to a privacy obligation; adding privacy_obligation_id links consent to its governing obligation.']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the consent (e.g., personal data, marketing, analytics).']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'How the consent was obtained (written, electronic, or verbal).']
  consent_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent record.']
  consent_reference varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference code for the consent record, used in audits and communications.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the consent was obtained.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent form or policy at the time of capture.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_subject_reference varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the person or entity providing consent.']
  data_subject_type varchar [note: 'Category of the data subject (e.g., employee, subcontractor, client contact).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the consent becomes effective for processing activities.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Optional expiry date of the consent; null if consent is open‑ended.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the legal jurisdiction governing the consent.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Lawful basis under GDPR or other regulations supporting the processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the consent.']
  processing_activities varchar [note: 'List or description of processing activities (e.g., HR payroll, project reporting) covered by the consent.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Specific business purpose(s) for which the data subject\'s personal data may be processed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the consent record.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the data subject withdrew consent, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Records explicit consent obtained from data subjects (employees, subcontractor workers, client contacts) for specific personal data processing activities. Captures data subject reference, consent purpose, consent date, consent method (written, electronic, verbal), consent version, withdrawal date if applicable, and processing activities covered. Supports GDPR lawful basis documentation and consent management obligations.'
}

Table compliance.pci_control {
  pci_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PCI DSS control record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: PCI Control compliance tracking associates each control with the construction project handling sensitive data.']
  pci_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_assessment. Business justification: PCI controls are evaluated within a PCI assessment; linking each control to its assessment provides traceability and removes the silo.']
  assessment_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the ROC, AOC, or other assessment documentation.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the most recent assessment.']
  assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent assessment.']
  assessment_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard against which the control was assessed (e.g., v4.0).']
  assessor_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the assessor for follow‑up.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or organization that performed the latest assessment.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'Overall compliance level derived from assessments.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing compliance maturity.']
  control_audit_trail varchar [note: 'Append‑only log of changes to the control record for audit purposes.']
  control_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping the control (e.g., Access Control, Monitoring).']
  control_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created in the system.']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of what the control addresses and how it is applied.']
