  control_family varchar [note: 'One of the six PCI DSS control families to which this control belongs.']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is expected to be performed or verified.']
  control_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique code or number identifying the PCI DSS control (e.g., 1.1, 2.3.4).']
  control_last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last formal review of the control.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the PCI DSS control.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for the control\'s implementation and upkeep.']
  control_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level (1‑5) for remediation or review, with 1 being highest.']
  control_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific PCI DSS requirement number (e.g., Req 1.2.3).']
  control_retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the control was retired or superseded.']
  control_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the control based on impact and likelihood.']
  control_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control originates from internal policy or external regulation.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  control_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of user‑defined tags for categorization and search.']
  control_testing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the control is tested for effectiveness.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control is technical, procedural, or administrative.']
  control_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  control_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the control definition (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  evidence_location varchar [note: 'Path or URL where supporting evidence for the control is stored.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key findings from the latest assessment.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control has been fully implemented, is planned, or not implemented.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent assessment of this control.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next assessment of this control.']
  related_systems varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of systems or applications in scope for this control.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing remediation actions.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Textual description of the remediation steps and responsibilities.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any remediation activities for the control.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control applies to the cardholder data environment.']

  Note: 'Master register of PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) controls and assessment history for the organizations project payment processing systems, subcontractor payment portals, and client billing platforms. Captures PCI DSS requirement reference, control description, control owner, applicable system or process, implementation status, and full assessment history including SAQ (Self-Assessment Questionnaire) completions, QSA (Qualified Security Assessor) audits, penetration test results, assessment dates, assessors, PCI DSS version assessed against, cardholder data environment scope, findings, compliance level achieved, ROC (Report on Compliance), and AOC (Attestation of Compliance) references. Supports PCI DSS compliance program management for construction financial transaction security including progress payment systems and online payment portals.'
}

Table compliance.pci_assessment {
  pci_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the PCI DSS assessment record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the assessment applies.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) undergoing the PCI assessment.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the PCI DSS assessment was conducted.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the assessment by the organization.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of PCI DSS assessment performed.']
  assessor_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the assessor for follow‑up communication.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity performing the assessment.']
  cardholder_data_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder data environment (CDE) is fully in scope, out of scope, or partially in scope for the assessment.']
  compliance_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with performing the PCI DSS assessment.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliance level achieved (Level 1‑4) based on the assessment results.']
  control_failed varchar [note: 'Count of controls that did not meet compliance requirements.']
  control_passed varchar [note: 'Count of controls that met compliance requirements.']
  control_total varchar [note: 'Number of PCI DSS controls evaluated in the assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting evidence files (e.g., screenshots, logs) stored in the document repository.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of documented exceptions or compensating controls.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of compliance findings identified during the assessment.']
  last_assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent assessment activity (e.g., final sign‑off).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the assessor or compliance team.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the assessment indicating full compliance, non‑compliance, or partial compliance.']
  pci_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment record.']
  pci_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard against which the assessment was performed (e.g., 4.0).']
  remediation_actions varchar [note: 'Planned or executed remediation steps to address identified gaps.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation actions must be completed.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ROC or AOC document generated for the assessment.']
  report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored ROC/AOC document.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score (0‑100) derived from assessment findings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the assessment scope, including systems, networks, and processes covered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of PCI DSS compliance assessments and Self-Assessment Questionnaires (SAQ) conducted for financial transaction processing environments. Captures assessment type (SAQ, QSA audit, penetration test), assessment date, assessor, PCI DSS version assessed against, scope of cardholder data environment, findings, compliance level achieved, and Report on Compliance (ROC) or Attestation of Compliance (AOC) reference.'
}

Table compliance.iso_certification {
  iso_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each ISO certification record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: ISO Certification Management ties certifications to the specific construction project for audit scope.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that holds the certification.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments from the audit team.']
  audit_schedule_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which surveillance audits are required.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official number assigned by the certifying body to the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'ISO standard for which the organization is certified.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the ISO certification.']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting evidence (e.g., audit reports, corrective action plans).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the certification document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the certification becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the certification expires or is scheduled to end (null for open‑ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certificate is scheduled to expire.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Count of audit findings identified in the last audit.']
  iso_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certificate was originally issued.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the jurisdiction governing the certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit (internal, surveillance, or recertification).']
  last_audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent audit.']
  last_audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the most recent audit.']
  next_surveillance_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next surveillance audit required by the standard.']
  non_conformances_count varchar [note: 'Count of non‑conformances raised during the last audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks.']
  observations varchar [note: 'Narrative observations recorded by the auditor.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the renewal process.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal action is required before expiry.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the certification (e.g., impact of non‑compliance).']
  scope varchar [note: 'Description of the business processes, sites, or functions covered by the certification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking ISO management system certifications held or pursued by the organization — ISO 9001 (Quality), ISO 14001 (Environmental), and ISO 45001 (OH&S) — with integrated audit history. Captures certification standard, certifying body, certificate number, scope of certification, initial certification date, current certificate expiry, surveillance audit schedule, and certification status. Manages full audit trail including internal audits, surveillance audits, and recertification audits with audit type, date, auditor/certification body, standard audited, scope, findings, non-conformances raised, observations, and audit outcome. Distinct from LEED certification (project-specific) — this covers organizational management system certifications and their ongoing maintenance through the complete audit lifecycle.'
}

Table compliance.iso_audit {
  iso_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ISO audit record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the audit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the auditor in the master party registry.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: ISO audits are performed on subcontractor firms; linking audit records to the firm enables compliance reporting.']
  iso_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.iso_certification. Business justification: Each ISO audit evaluates a specific ISO certification. Adding iso_certification_id to iso_audit captures this relationship.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the certification body in the master party registry.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Category of the audit based on focus area.']
  audit_comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments recorded by the auditor.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time spent on the audit, expressed in hours.']
  audit_findings_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed findings document.']
  audit_location varchar [note: 'Physical site or facility where the audit was performed.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the audit.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the audit by the certification body.']
  audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit: passed, failed, or conditional (requires corrective actions).']
  audit_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number used by external parties (e.g., client or regulator).']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit report document.']
  audit_scope_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the audit scope for reporting.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the audit based on the scoring methodology.']
  audit_score_scale varchar [note: 'Scale used for the audit score (e.g., 0‑100 or 1‑5).']
  audit_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the audit record originated (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  audit_team_size varchar [note: 'Number of auditors and support staff involved.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit: internal, surveillance, or recertification.']
  audit_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the audit record, incremented on revisions.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the lead auditor or certification body representative.']
  auditor_role varchar [note: 'Role of the auditor in the audit team.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external certification organization conducting the audit.']
  certification_body_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact information (email or phone) for the certification body.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination after the audit.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which all corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_plan_url varchar [note: 'Link to the corrective action plan associated with the audit.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions are required based on audit findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting evidence documents attached to the audit.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of audit findings and observations.']
  follow_up_audit_date date [note: 'Planned date for the follow‑up audit, if any.']
  follow_up_audit_scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up audit has been scheduled.']
  iso_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the audit record.']
  non_conformances_count varchar [note: 'Number of non‑conformances identified during the audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the auditor or audit manager.']
  observations_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations (non‑critical findings) recorded.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, including sites, processes, and functions covered.']
  standard_audited varchar [note: 'ISO management system standard that was audited.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of ISO management system surveillance audits, recertification audits, and internal audits conducted against ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 standards. Captures audit type (internal, surveillance, recertification), audit date, auditor or certification body, standard audited, scope, findings, non-conformances raised, observations, and audit outcome. Supports ongoing ISO certification maintenance.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_authority {
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the regulatory authority.']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Common short name or acronym of the authority.']
  address varchar [note: 'Physical mailing address of the authority.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category type of the authority.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the organization with respect to this authority.']
  contact_person varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the authority.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Role of the primary contact person within the authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authority record was created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the authority record became effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the authority record is no longer effective, if applicable.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the authority.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage of the authority\'s regulatory reach.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this authority is the primary regulator for the organization.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Level of jurisdiction the authority operates under.']
  last_contact_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent communication with the authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the authority.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the authority.']
  portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the electronic portal used for submissions to the authority.']
  regulatory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_authority_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the authority record.']
  regulatory_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory frameworks the authority enforces (e.g., OSHA, EPA, ISO 45001).']
  relationship_owner varchar [note: 'Internal department or owner responsible for managing the relationship with the authority.']
  submission_system varchar [note: 'Name of the electronic submission system or platform used by the authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authority record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL for the authority.']

  Note: 'Reference master of regulatory and governing bodies with whom the organization interacts, including OSHA, EPA, local building departments, environmental agencies, fire marshals, and utility authorities. Captures authority name, jurisdiction type, geographic scope, contact details, applicable regulatory frameworks, portal or submission system reference, and relationship owner. Enables consistent authority identification across permit applications, submissions, and notices.'
}

Table compliance.waiver_exemption {
  waiver_exemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the waiver or exemption record.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related permit that the waiver modifies or supersedes.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the waiver applies.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Waivers are granted to specific subcontractors; the FK identifies the beneficiary firm for tracking.']
  related_contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a contract (e.g., EPC, JV) associated with the waiver.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents linked to the waiver record.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Standard code or identifier for the granting authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the repository where ongoing compliance evidence is stored.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the organization is meeting the waiver’s conditions.']
  conditions_summary varchar [note: 'Brief list of key conditions the organization must satisfy while the waiver is in effect.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code used for financial tracking of the waiver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the waiver record was first entered into the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact estimate.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the waiver becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the waiver expires or is scheduled to terminate (nullable for open‑ended waivers).']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to digital evidence demonstrating ongoing compliance with waiver conditions.']
  financial_impact_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the financial impact (savings or cost) resulting from the waiver.']
  granting_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or agency that issued the waiver.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates if the waiver contains confidential or commercially sensitive information.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or jurisdiction governing the waiver (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code).']
  last_monitoring_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance monitoring activity.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the waiver’s conditions must be monitored or reported.']
  monitoring_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of monitoring, reporting, or audit activities required to maintain compliance.']
  next_monitoring_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next required monitoring activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured by compliance staff.']
  regulation_section varchar [note: 'Specific code or clause of the regulation that the waiver modifies (e.g., 40 CFR 60.10).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for renewal submission, if renewal is required.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waiver must be renewed before its expiry.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the waiver based on condition severity and exposure.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the activities, locations, or systems covered by the waiver.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for revocation).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the waiver record.']
  waiver_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the waiver is temporary, permanent, or conditional on specific actions.']
  waiver_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the waiver.']
  waiver_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference number assigned by the granting authority.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Category of the waiver indicating the regulatory domain it addresses.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the waiver record.']

  Note: 'Records regulatory waivers, exemptions, variances, and alternative compliance methods granted to the organization or specific projects by regulatory authorities, allowing deviation from standard regulatory requirements under defined conditions. Captures waiver type, granting authority, applicable regulation or code section, scope of exemption, conditions attached, monitoring requirements, effective date, expiry date, renewal status, and evidence of ongoing condition compliance. Critical for demonstrating authorized non-standard compliance positions during audits and for ensuring waiver conditions are actively monitored.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_calendar {
  compliance_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance calendar entry.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team accountable for meeting the deadline.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Compliance calendars often contain deadlines specific to a subcontractor\'s obligations; linking ensures timely alerts.']
  parent_compliance_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compliance_calendar (parent_compliance_calendar_id)']
  related_contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract associated with the compliance requirement.']
  related_project_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the compliance deadline applies.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the calendar entry.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance action was completed.']
  compliance_calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar entry.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of compliance (e.g., Safety, Environment, Quality).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calendar entry record was first created.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the compliance obligation must be satisfied.']
  deadline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deadline, indicating the nature of the compliance requirement.']
  entry_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the calendar entry, used for external reference.']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Textual description of the escalation procedure if the deadline is missed.']
  escalation_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalation process has been activated.']
  escalation_triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the escalation was triggered.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the jurisdiction governing the obligation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the calendar entry.']
  overdue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deadline is past due and not yet completed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the deadline for resource planning.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or body that issued the obligation.']
  reminder_lead_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the deadline when a reminder should be issued.']
  reminder_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pre‑deadline reminder has been sent.']
  reminder_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reminder was sent to the responsible owner.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with missing the deadline.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier of the originating regulatory obligation, permit, or certification.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title describing the compliance deadline or event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calendar entry.']

  Note: 'Operational calendar of compliance deadlines, renewal dates, submission due dates, permit expiry dates, ISO audit schedules, LEED submission windows, and regulatory filing deadlines. Aggregates key dates from permits, certifications, regulatory obligations, and authority notices into a single actionable timeline. Captures deadline date, deadline type, source obligation or permit reference, responsible owner, reminder lead time, escalation path, completion status, and overdue flag. The primary daily operational tool for construction compliance managers ensuring no regulatory deadline is missed across multiple project sites and jurisdictions.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_change {
  regulatory_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory change record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Regulatory Change Impact Assessment links each change to affected projects to update compliance plans.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or owner responsible for overseeing implementation.']
  firm_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Regulatory changes can impact particular subcontractors; the FK records which firms must implement the change.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: Regulatory changes are issued by a governing authority; linking them records the source authority.']
  superseded_regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulatory_change (superseded_regulatory_change_id)']
  affected_jurisdictions varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country or region codes where the change applies (comma‑separated).']
  affected_obligations varchar [note: 'List or description of specific compliance obligations impacted by the change.']
  affected_projects varchar [note: 'Identifiers of projects that are impacted by the regulatory change (comma‑separated).']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the regulatory change record.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Notes capturing audit trail information for compliance verification.']
  change_authority varchar [note: 'Specific agency or department that authorized the regulatory change.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the regulatory change and its provisions.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number assigned to the regulatory change.']
  change_review_status varchar [note: 'Current review status of the regulatory change within internal governance.']
  change_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the regulatory change (e.g., Federal Register, State Agency).']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory change: new, amended, or repealed.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Broad category of the regulatory change (e.g., environmental, safety, building code).']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level category grouping the change (e.g., regulatory, policy, standard).']
  compliance_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to external evidence or documentation proving compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory change record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory change becomes effective and enforceable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory change expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the regulatory change on the organization.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD).']
  impact_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of the business impact caused by the regulatory change.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which the organization must implement the regulatory change.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current progress of implementing the regulatory change.']
  implementation_status_date date [note: 'Date when the implementation status was last updated.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the regulatory change is currently active in the system.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the change is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Level of jurisdiction for the change (federal, state, local).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the regulatory change.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory change was officially published.']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Official code or identifier of the regulation affected by the change (e.g., IBC-2023-01).']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority that issued the regulation (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  regulatory_change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory change within the organization.']
  required_actions varchar [note: 'Specific actions the organization must take to comply with the change.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory change was last reviewed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the regulatory change based on impact and likelihood.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Document or reference number linking to the original regulatory text.']
  stakeholder_impact varchar [note: 'Description of how key stakeholders (clients, subcontractors, regulators) are affected.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the regulatory change record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the regulatory change record.']

  Note: 'Tracks changes to applicable laws, regulations, building codes, environmental standards, and safety requirements that affect the organizations compliance obligations across construction jurisdictions. Captures regulation reference, change type (new, amended, repealed), publication date, effective date, impact assessment summary, affected obligations, affected projects and jurisdictions, required actions, implementation deadline, implementation status, and responsible owner. Supports proactive compliance management as building codes (IBC/IRC), environmental regulations (EPA/Clean Water Act), OSHA safety standards, and local authority requirements evolve.'
}

Table compliance.finding {
  finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for finding']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the auditor who logged the finding.']
  source_finding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on finding (source_finding_id)']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination for the finding.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action taken to address the finding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the impact amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the issue, cause, and context.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date for corrective action completion.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the finding.']
  impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the finding.']
  is_financial_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding has financial implications.']
  is_privacy_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding involves privacy or data protection concerns.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the site or facility where the finding originated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information or comments.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulation, standard, or law applicable to the finding.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who reported the finding.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date the finding was initially reported.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the finding was resolved or closed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on severity and impact.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric severity rating (0.00‑10.00) assigned by the auditor.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original record in the source system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the finding.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the finding.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise title summarizing the finding.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the finding (e.g., safety, environmental).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for finding. Referenced by source_finding_id.'
}

Table compliance.audit_report {

  audit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the period covered by the audit.']
  audit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by the audit.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the scope and boundaries of the audit.']
  auditor_email varchar [note: 'Contact email of the primary auditor.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary auditor responsible for the report.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status determined by the audit.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the audit report content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit report record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical.']
  document_path varchar [note: 'File system or storage path to the full audit report document.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of audit findings.']
  noncritical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as non‑critical.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score summarizing audit performance (0‑100).']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Date by which identified issues must be remediated.']
  report_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the audit report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the audit report.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Category of audit performed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned based on audit findings.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit report.']
  total_findings varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified in the audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the audit report record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the audit report when revisions occur.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_report. Referenced by audit_report_id.'
}

Table hr.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each employee record.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.job_profile. Business justification: Job profile centralizes job title and grade; employee.job_profile_id FK replaces redundant job_title and job_grade columns.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or supervisor.']
  base_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base monetary compensation before bonuses or allowances.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Method of compensation for the employee.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center associated with the employee\'s labor expenses.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for salary payments.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s birth date, used for age‑based benefits and compliance.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department to which the employee belongs.']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest level of formal education attained.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address for the employee.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Company‑assigned employee number used for internal HR processes.']
  employee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment state of the employee.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the employee as recorded on official documents.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the employee.']
  health_insurance_plan varchar [note: 'Name of the health insurance plan the employee is enrolled in.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment.']
  hse_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has completed mandatory Health, Safety, and Environment training.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name (surname) of the employee.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance evaluation.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Primary site or office location where the employee works.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Current marital status of the employee.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued personal identifier (e.g., SSN, NIN).']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular interval at which the employee is paid.']
  performance_rating_last_year varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating for the most recent review year.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number listed for the employee.']
  professional_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of professional certifications held by the employee.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  retirement_plan varchar [note: 'Retirement or pension plan assigned to the employee.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Level of security clearance granted to the employee.']
  social_security_number varchar [note: 'U.S. Social Security Number for payroll and tax reporting.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identifier used for payroll reporting and statutory filings.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for employee separation (e.g., resignation, layoff, retirement).']
  visa_status varchar [note: 'Visa or residency status of the employee.']
  work_permit_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the work permit.']
  work_permit_number varchar [note: 'Number of the work permit authorizing employment, if required.']

  Note: 'Master record for all salaried, office, and management staff employed by the construction enterprise. Captures full employee identity, employment classification, job title, grade, department, cost center, employment type (permanent, fixed-term, secondment), hire date, termination date, work location, manager, and employment status. SSOT for corporate HR personnel distinct from field craft labor (owned by workforce domain). Integrates with payroll, benefits, performance, and organizational hierarchy.'
}

Table hr.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the position record.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.job_profile. Business justification: Linking position to its job profile consolidates overlapping descriptive attributes and eliminates redundancy, ensuring each position references a single authoritative job profile.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Positions are scoped to an org unit; position.org_unit_id FK provides hierarchy and allows removal of location_code which is stored in org unit.']
  position_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the creation of the position.']
  position_employee_id varchar [note: 'HR employee responsible for sourcing candidates for this position.']
  reporting_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate supervisory position.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on position (reports_to_position_id)']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position was approved for creation.']
  benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position participates in company benefit programs.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for performance bonuses.']
  budgeted_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Salary amount allocated in the project or department budget.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the recruitment posting closed.']
  competency_required varchar [note: 'Key competency or skill set required for the position.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the position\'s expenses are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the position.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is scheduled to be retired or closed (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active and can be filled.']
  fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allocated full‑time equivalent headcount for the position.']
  grade_band varchar [note: 'Compensation grade band assigned to the position (e.g., G5, G6).']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if the position is exempt from overtime regulations.']
  is_manager boolean [note: 'True if the position includes managerial responsibilities.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level within the job family (e.g., Level 3, Manager).']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages and proficiency levels required for the position.']
  last_filled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position was most recently filled by an employee.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy code used to reference the position in HR systems.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of duties, responsibilities, and expectations.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current occupancy status of the position.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the position was posted for recruitment.']
  remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position can be performed remotely.']
  salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range for the position, annualized in USD.']
  salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range for the position, annualized in USD.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Required security clearance for the role.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Work shift pattern associated with the position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the authorized position (e.g., Senior Project Engineer).']
  union_member boolean [note: 'True if the position is covered by a labor union.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']
  vacancy_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the position is vacant or being created.']
  work_schedule varchar [note: 'Standard weekly schedule for the position.']

  Note: 'Authorized position (headcount slot) within the organizational structure. Defines the approved role, job family, grade band, FTE allocation, reporting line, department, cost center, and whether the position is filled or vacant. Supports headcount planning, org design, and recruitment requisition. Each employee is linked to a position; positions exist independently of incumbents to support vacancy management.'
}

Table hr.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the organizational unit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the employee who is the primary manager of the unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent unit in the hierarchy.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the unit, expressed in the corporate currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the organizational unit record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit ceased operation or was retired; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the unit became operational.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the unit.']
  gl_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Finance cost‑center code associated with the unit for budgeting and reporting.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of employees assigned to the unit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Primary physical location or office address of the unit.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to identify the unit in ERP and reporting systems.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the unit’s purpose and responsibilities.']
  org_unit_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the unit within the organization tree (root = 1).']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the organizational unit (e.g., "East Coast Construction Division").']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit within the corporate hierarchy.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Main telephone contact for the unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the organizational unit record.']

  Note: 'Organizational unit master defining the hierarchical structure of the construction enterprise — divisions, business units, departments, and cost centers. Captures unit name, unit type, parent unit, effective dates, responsible manager, and associated GL cost center. Supports org chart rendering, reporting hierarchies, and cost allocation. Distinct from project WBS (owned by project domain) and field crew structures (owned by workforce domain).'
}

Table hr.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the job profile record.']
  parent_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_profile (parent_job_profile_id)']
  award_classification varchar [note: 'Industrial award or enterprise agreement governing employment terms for the role.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Method of remuneration for the role (e.g., salaried, hourly, contract, commission).']
  competency_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the competency model or framework applied to assess the role.']
  competency_level varchar [note: 'Expected proficiency tier for the role within the competency framework.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the job profile record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the job profile definition becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the job profile definition is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the employment relationship for the role.']
  health_and_safety_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory HSE training is required before an employee can assume the role.']
  is_field_facing boolean [note: 'True if the role primarily works on construction sites or field locations.']
  is_office_based boolean [note: 'True if the role is primarily performed in an office environment.']
  is_project_based boolean [note: 'True if the role is assigned to specific projects rather than a functional department.']
  is_union_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role is covered by a labor union or award.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional grouping of the role for talent management and reporting.']
  job_family_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the job family for integration with external taxonomy.']
  job_grade varchar [note: 'Internal grade or level assigned to the role for compensation and career progression.']
  job_profile_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used in HR systems and recruitment tools.']
  job_profile_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing responsibilities, scope, and key duties of the role.']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Primary work location category for the role.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the role is eligible for overtime compensation under applicable awards.']
  reporting_manager_role varchar [note: 'Standardized title of the role to which this position reports.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of professional certifications mandatory for the role.']
  required_education varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the role.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience needed to perform the role.']
  salary_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range applicable to the role, expressed in local currency.']
  salary_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range applicable to the role, expressed in local currency.']
  title varchar [note: 'Standardized name of the job role as used across the enterprise.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires regular travel between sites or offices.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the job profile record.']

  Note: 'Job profile and role definition master capturing the standardized job title, job family, job level/grade, required qualifications, competency framework, and salary band for each role in the construction enterprise. Serves as the template for position creation and recruitment. Includes whether the role is field-facing, office-based, or project-based, and the applicable industrial award or enterprise agreement classification.'
}

Table hr.employment_contract {
  employment_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the employment contract record.']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salaried employee to whom the contract applies.']
  superseded_employment_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on employment_contract (superseded_employment_contract_id)']
  agreement_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on its governing agreement or award.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the latest amendment was executed.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of contract amendment.']
  award_code varchar [note: 'Industrial award or collective bargaining agreement code applicable.']
  benefits_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of employee benefits provided under the contract.']
  bonus_entitlement decimal(12,2) [note: 'Potential annual bonus amount payable under the contract.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or remarks about the contract.']
  confidentiality_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a confidentiality clause.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic contract document.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the employment contract.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was signed by all parties.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of employment contract indicating the nature of the engagement.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department where the employee works.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract ends or expires; null for open-ended contracts.']
  enterprise_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise agreement governing the contract.']
  health_insurance_plan varchar [note: 'Name of the health insurance plan provided.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was last reviewed for compliance or renewal.']
  leave_accrual_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual leave accrual rate in days per year.']
  leave_balance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current accrued leave balance in days.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the contract in its lifecycle.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary work location.']
  non_compete_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a non-compete clause.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of notice required for termination by either party.']
  overtime_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary rate applied for overtime hours.']
  pension_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Employee pension contribution percentage.']
  position_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position held by the employee.']
  probation_period_days varchar [note: 'Length of the probationary period in days.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is set for automatic renewal.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary agreed in the contract.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the salary amount.']
  severance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary severance payable upon termination.']
  tax_withholding_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of salary withheld for tax purposes.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for contract termination.']
  working_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of working hours per week.']

  Note: 'Employment contract record for each salaried employee, capturing contract type (permanent, fixed-term, casual, secondment), contract start and end dates, notice period, probation period, agreed salary, bonus entitlement, working hours, and applicable enterprise agreement or award. Tracks contract amendments and renewals. Distinct from subcontract agreements (subcontractor domain) and project contracts (contract domain).'
}

Table hr.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salaried employee to whom this payroll record applies.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll run batch that generated this record.']
  reversal_payroll_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_record (reversal_payroll_record_id)']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment applied to the payroll (positive or negative).']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the payroll adjustment.']
  allowance_project decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allowance tied to specific project assignments for the period.']
  allowance_site decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allowance for site-specific duties during the pay period.']
  allowance_travel decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allowance for travel expenses incurred during the pay period.']
  bank_account_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the employee\'s bank account used for payroll deposit.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number for payroll direct deposit.']
  bonus_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional bonus payment included in this payroll.']
  bonus_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bonus payment.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll was processed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks related to the payroll record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the payroll amounts.']
  deduction_other_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of any additional deduction not covered by standard categories.']
  deduction_other_description varchar [note: 'Description of the additional deduction.']
  deduction_salary_sacrifice decimal(12,2) [note: 'Voluntary salary sacrifice amount (e.g., for retirement or benefits).']
  deduction_superannuation decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑mandated superannuation (pension) contribution deducted.']
  deduction_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Statutory tax withheld from gross salary.']
  gross_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross salary earned for the pay period before any deductions.']
  is_off_cycle boolean [note: 'True if the payroll entry is an off‑cycle or supplemental payment.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payroll record.']
  net_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after all allowances and deductions.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked in the pay period.']
  overtime_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Hourly rate applied to overtime hours.']
  pay_period_end date [note: 'End date of the pay period covered by this payroll.']
  pay_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the pay period covered by this payroll.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the net pay was transferred or issued.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the net pay to the employee.']
  payroll_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the payroll record.']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payroll record.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payroll record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this payroll record.']
  superannuation_fund varchar [note: 'Name of the superannuation fund receiving contributions.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Internal tax code used for calculating employee tax.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the tax calculations apply.']
  year_to_date_deductions decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all deductions (tax, superannuation, etc.) for the fiscal year.']
  year_to_date_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross earnings for the employee in the current fiscal year.']
  year_to_date_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative net earnings for the employee in the current fiscal year.']
  year_to_date_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax withheld for the employee in the current fiscal year.']

  Note: 'Periodic payroll processing record for each salaried employee capturing pay period, gross salary, allowances (site allowance, travel allowance, project allowance), deductions (tax, superannuation/pension, salary sacrifice), net pay, payment method, and payroll run status. Tracks payroll adjustments, off-cycle payments, and year-to-date accumulators. SSOT for corporate payroll distinct from field craft labor payroll (workforce domain).'
}

Table hr.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the benefit plan record.']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  benefit_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  co_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed amount the employee pays per service encounter.']
  confidential_benefit boolean [note: 'True if the benefit details are treated as confidential within the organization.']
  coverage_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of what the plan covers, including limits and exclusions.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Scope of coverage (e.g., individual, employee+spouse, family).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the benefit plan record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of monetary values.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount the employee must pay before the plan starts covering expenses.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan terminates or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes active for eligible employees.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Rules defining which employees are eligible (e.g., full‑time, tenure, job grade).']
  employee_contribution_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium deducted from employee payroll.']
  employer_contribution_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium paid by the employer.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last date employees may enroll in the plan for the current period.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First date employees may enroll in the plan for the current period.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is currently active (true) or not (false).']
  max_coverage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit of benefits payable per claim or per year.']
  out_of_pocket_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount an employee will pay in a benefit year.']
  plan_admin_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plan administrator.']
  plan_admin_name varchar [note: 'Internal contact responsible for plan administration.']
  plan_admin_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the plan administrator.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to reference the plan in HR systems.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the PDF or web page containing the full plan document.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the benefit plan as used in employee communications.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit (e.g., health, dental, vision, life, disability, pension, EAP, vehicle allowance, professional membership). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|dental|vision|life|disability|pension|eap|vehicle|membership — promote to reference product]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the plan (e.g., v2024.1).']
  provider_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the benefit provider contact.']
  provider_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the benefit provider contact.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or insurer delivering the benefit.']
  taxable_benefit boolean [note: 'True if the benefit is considered taxable income for the employee.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the benefit plan record.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after enrollment before benefits become payable.']

  Note: 'Master record defining each employee benefit plan offered by the construction enterprise — health insurance, dental, vision, life insurance, income protection, superannuation/pension, EAP (Employee Assistance Program), vehicle allowance, and professional membership. Captures plan name, plan type, provider, coverage details, eligibility criteria, employer contribution rate, employee contribution rate, and effective dates.'
}

Table hr.benefit_enrollment {
  benefit_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the benefit enrollment record.']
  benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the benefit plan to which the employee is enrolling.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who is enrolling in the benefit plan.']
  prior_benefit_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_enrollment (prior_benefit_enrollment_id)']
  benefit_cost_share decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the benefit coverage split between employee and employer.']
  contribution_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the contribution amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit coverage ends or is scheduled to terminate (nullable for open‑ended coverage).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when the selected benefit coverage begins.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage selected: employee only, employee + spouse, or family.']
  dependent_count varchar [note: 'Number of dependents covered under the enrollment (spouse and/or children).']
  elected_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount the employee elects to contribute per pay period for the selected benefit.']
  employee_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium the employee is responsible for.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount contributed by the employer for the employee\'s coverage.']
  employer_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium paid by the employer.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment transaction was recorded in the system.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment record.']
  mid_year_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment was a change made outside the open enrollment period due to a qualifying event.']
  open_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment occurred during the annual open enrollment window.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used to identify the benefit plan in payroll and HR systems.']
  plan_group varchar [note: 'Grouping identifier for related benefit plans (e.g., "Medical Benefits Group").']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit plan (e.g., health, dental, vision).']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the benefit plan (e.g., v2024).']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the benefit plan version applies.']
  qualifying_event_date date [note: 'Date the qualifying life event occurred.']
  qualifying_life_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of life event that permits a mid‑year enrollment change.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has waived the benefit (true) or elected coverage (false).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an employee\'s enrollment in a specific benefit plan. Captures enrollment date, coverage tier (employee only, employee + spouse, family), elected contribution amount, effective start and end dates, enrollment status, and any waiver declarations. Tracks open enrollment events, qualifying life events, and mid-year changes. Links employee to benefit plan.'
}

Table hr.leave_request {
  leave_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the leave request record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved or rejected the request.']
  original_leave_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on leave_request (original_leave_request_id)']
  primary_leave_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who submitted the leave request.']
  accrual_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the leave accrual policy that governs this request.']
  approval_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the approval process.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved or rejected.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documentation (e.g., medical certificate) is attached.']
  carryover_allowed boolean [note: 'True if unused leave days may be carried over to the next year.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional comments from the employee or approver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the leave request record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the requested leave period.']
  hr_notified boolean [note: 'True when the HR department has been notified of the request.']
  is_paid_leave boolean [note: 'True if the leave is paid; false if unpaid.']
  leave_balance_after decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accrued leave balance for the employee after this request is processed.']
  leave_balance_before decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accrued leave balance for the employee prior to this request.']
  leave_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leave request.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave requested (e.g., annual, sick, parental, long service, FMLA, compassionate, study).']
  leave_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar year to which the leave request applies.']
  payroll_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the leave will affect payroll processing.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation provided by the employee for the leave request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the leave request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the employee submitted the leave request.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the requested leave period.']
  total_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of leave days requested, including fractional days.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the leave request record.']

  Note: 'Employee leave request and approval record capturing leave type (annual leave, sick leave, parental leave, long service leave, FMLA, compassionate leave, study leave), requested start and end dates, number of days, approval status, approver, approval date, and leave balance impact. Tracks leave accruals, carryover balances, and leave liability for financial reporting. Applies to salaried staff distinct from field craft labor attendance (workforce domain).'
}

Table hr.leave_balance {
  leave_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the leave balance record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee to whom this leave balance applies.']
  prior_leave_balance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on leave_balance (prior_leave_balance_id)']
  accrual_cap decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum leave that can be accrued for this type.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate leave accruals.']
  accrual_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which leave accrues.']
  accrual_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which leave accrues per accrual period (e.g., days per month).']
  accrued_balance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total leave accrued to date.']
  as_of_date date [note: 'Date for which the balance snapshot is recorded.']
  available_balance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Leave currently available for use.']
  balance_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the leave balance entry.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the leave balance record.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the payroll run that generated this balance snapshot.']
  carryover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if unused leave can be carried over to the next period.']
  carryover_limit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of leave that can be carried over.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave balance record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this leave balance ceases to be effective.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this leave balance becomes effective.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions under which the employee is eligible for this leave type.']
  forfeited_balance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Leave that has been forfeited.']
  last_accrual_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accrual calculation.']
  leave_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code of the leave policy governing accrual and usage.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave (e.g., Annual, Sick, Parental).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the balance record.']
  next_accrual_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next accrual.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the leave balance.']
  pending_balance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Leave approved but not yet taken.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the balance originated (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors).']
  taken_balance decimal(6,2) [note: 'Leave already taken.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the leave balance record.']

  Note: 'Current leave balance ledger for each employee by leave type, capturing accrued balance, taken balance, pending balance, forfeited balance, and available balance as of the last payroll run. Tracks leave accrual rates, maximum accrual caps, and carryover rules per employment contract and applicable award. Supports leave liability reporting for financial statements.'
}

Table hr.recruitment_requisition {
  recruitment_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the recruitment requisition record.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work site or office location for the role.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational department owning the vacancy.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master position record that defines the role and responsibilities.']
  primary_recruitment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager responsible for the hire.']
  replacement_recruitment_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recruitment_requisition (replacement_recruitment_requisition_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the requisition received formal approval.']
  approved_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of positions approved for this requisition.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Financial budget identifier linked to the requisition\'s cost allocation.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date after which the requisition will no longer accept applications.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for funding the new hire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the salary range.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship (e.g., full‑time, part‑time, contract).']
  expected_fte double [note: 'Projected full‑time equivalent effort the new hire will contribute.']
  external_job_posting boolean [note: 'True if the requisition is posted on external job boards or agencies.']
  internal_job_posting boolean [note: 'True if the requisition is posted on internal employee portals.']
  interview_process varchar [note: 'Outline of interview stages and assessment methods for the role.']
  job_grade varchar [note: 'Internal grade or level assigned to the position for compensation and hierarchy.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business reason for creating the requisition, including project or operational need.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was first posted to internal/external channels.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification of the requisition for resource planning.']
  recruitment_requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition (e.g., draft, approved, active, on‑hold, filled, cancelled).']
  recruitment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the candidate pipeline for this requisition.']
  remote_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position can be performed remotely (true) or requires on‑site presence (false).']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the role.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience candidates must possess.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the requisition, used in communications and tracking.']
  requisition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition was formally created or issued.']
  salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the annual salary range offered for the position.']
  salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the annual salary range offered for the position.']
  skill_set varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of key skills and competencies needed.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Approach used to source candidates (e.g., internal posting, external recruiter).']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the new hire to begin work.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the requisition record.']
