  Note: 'Recruitment requisition record initiated when a position vacancy is approved for filling. Captures requisition number, position, department, hiring manager, approved headcount, employment type, target start date, salary range, requisition status (draft, approved, active, on-hold, filled, cancelled), sourcing strategy, and justification. Links to the approved position and org unit. Drives the end-to-end talent acquisition workflow.'
}

Table hr.applicant {
  applicant_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the applicant record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter managing this applicant.']
  referred_by_applicant_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on applicant (referred_by_applicant_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the applicant\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the applicant\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  applicant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the applicant within the recruitment process.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date on which the applicant submitted the application.']
  availability_date date [note: 'Earliest date the applicant is available to start work.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the applicant\'s background screening process.']
  certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of professional certifications held by the applicant.']
  citizenship_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO country code representing the applicant\'s citizenship.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the applicant\'s mailing address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the applicant\'s country of residence.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the expected salary.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the organization where the applicant is currently employed.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Applicant\'s birth date for eligibility and compliance checks.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for communication with the applicant.']
  expected_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Salary amount the applicant expects or requests for the role.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the applicant as provided in the application.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the applicant\'s consent for processing personal data under GDPR.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the applicant provided or updated GDPR consent.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the applicant.']
  highest_qualification varchar [note: 'Highest academic degree or certification attained by the applicant.']
  languages varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of languages the applicant can speak, with proficiency levels if known.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'URL to the applicant\'s LinkedIn professional profile.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued national identification number of the applicant.']
  passport_number varchar [note: 'Passport number of the applicant for international travel verification.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the applicant.']
  position_applied_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the position for which the applicant applied.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the applicant\'s mailing address.']
  qualification_field varchar [note: 'Field of study or professional discipline of the highest qualification (e.g., civil engineering).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the applicant record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the applicant record.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or entity that referred the applicant, if applicable.']
  resume_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file path or storage key) to the applicant\'s uploaded resume/CV.']
  skills varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of key skills and competencies reported by the applicant.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the applicant learned about or applied for the position.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the applicant\'s mailing address.']
  visa_status varchar [note: 'Current visa or work‑authorization status of the applicant.']
  willingness_to_relocate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is willing to relocate for the position.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years of relevant professional experience reported by the applicant.']

  Note: 'Candidate/applicant master record for individuals who have applied for a position within the construction enterprise. Captures applicant name, contact details, source channel (LinkedIn, referral, agency, careers portal), CV/resume reference, current employer, years of experience, highest qualification, and GDPR/privacy consent status. Maintains applicant identity across multiple applications and recruitment cycles.'
}

Table hr.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the job application record.']
  applicant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the candidate who submitted the application.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter or hiring manager responsible for this application.']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruitment requisition to which this application belongs.']
  resubmitted_application_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on application (resubmitted_application_id)']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the candidate submitted the application.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing reference number for the application (e.g., APP-2023-00123).']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the application within the recruitment pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: applied|screening|interview|offer|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of any pre‑screening assessments or tests.']
  cover_letter_url varchar [note: 'Link to the candidate\'s uploaded cover letter.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the application record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the salary amounts.']
  interview_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated numeric score from interview assessments (scale 0‑10).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by recruiters or hiring managers.']
  offer_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has accepted the job offer.']
  offer_salary_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base salary amount offered to the candidate before deductions.']
  offer_salary_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Take‑home salary after standard deductions.']
  offer_start_date date [note: 'Proposed first day of employment if the offer is accepted.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual reason provided when the application is rejected.']
  resume_url varchar [note: 'Link to the candidate\'s uploaded resume document.']
  salary_adjustment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional monetary adjustments to the offer (e.g., signing bonus, relocation allowance).']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the candidate applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the application record.']

  Note: 'Job application transactional record linking an applicant to a specific recruitment requisition. Captures application date, application status (applied, screening, interview, offer, hired, rejected, withdrawn), stage in the recruitment pipeline, interview scores, assessment results, offer details (offered salary, start date), offer acceptance status, and rejection reason. Tracks the full candidate journey from application to hire or rejection.'
}

Table hr.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the performance review record.']
  primary_performance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salaried employee being reviewed.']
  prior_performance_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_review (prior_performance_review_id)']
  tertiary_performance_hr_approval_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR representative who approved the review.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the employee qualifies for a performance bonus.']
  calibration_comments varchar [note: 'Comments from the calibration committee.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Status of the calibration process across the organization.']
  compensation_change_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review resulted in a compensation change.']
  competency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated competency rating across all competency areas.']
  employee_self_assessment varchar [note: 'Free‑text self‑evaluation entered by the employee.']
  final_approved_rating varchar [note: 'Rating after calibration and final approval.']
  goal_achievement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted score reflecting achievement of pre‑set goals.']
  hr_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status from HR.']
  hr_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when HR completed the approval.']
  leadership_competency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for leadership and people‑management competencies.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the review record.']
  manager_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text feedback provided by the manager.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the employee\'s next performance review.']
  overall_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Executive summary rating of employee performance.']
  promotion_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for promotion based on the review.']
  rating_explanation varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of how the rating was derived.']
  rating_scale varchar [note: 'Definition of the rating scale used (e.g., 1‑5).']
  rating_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 4.5) corresponding to the overall rating.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The scheduled cycle of the review (annual, mid‑year, probation, or project‑end).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the review was formally completed.']
  review_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the site or office where the review took place.']
  review_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to collect review data.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the review (e.g., PR‑2024‑001).']
  review_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_period_start date [note: 'First day of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review (performance, project, or probation).']
  review_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the review record to support revisions.']
  safety_competency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for safety‑related competencies, important in construction.']
  salary_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of salary increase or decrease linked to the review.']
  succession_plan_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee is identified for succession planning.']
  technical_competency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for technical knowledge and skill competencies.']

  Note: 'Annual or mid-year performance review record for a salaried employee. Captures review cycle (annual, mid-year, probation, project-end), review period, overall performance rating, goal achievement score, competency ratings, manager comments, employee self-assessment, calibration status, and final approved rating. Supports performance-linked pay decisions, promotion eligibility, and succession planning for construction management and engineering staff.'
}

Table hr.goal {
  goal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the performance goal.']
  alignment_kpi_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate KPI that this goal aligns with.']
  goal_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who approved the goal.']
  goal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who owns the goal.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  parent_goal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on goal (parent_goal_id)']
  achievement_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating assigned at review time.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goal was approved by the manager.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes regarding the goal.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the goal was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goal record was created.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the goal is expected to be achieved.']
  goal_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code for the goal, used in reporting and communication.']
  goal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the goal, including expectations and scope.']
  goal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goal.']
  goal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the goal indicating its scope and ownership.']
  is_key_goal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goal is a key strategic goal.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review for the goal.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure progress toward the target.']
  progress_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current progress expressed as a percentage of the target.']
  rating_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric score associated with the achievement rating.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Performance review cycle identifier (e.g., FY2024 Q1).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the goal period begins.']
  supporting_documents varchar [note: 'Link to documents that provide context or evidence for the goal.']
  target_metric varchar [note: 'Metric or key performance indicator that the goal aims to influence.']
  target_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric target value for the metric.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the performance goal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the goal record.']
  weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative weight of the goal expressed as a percentage of total performance evaluation.']

  Note: 'Individual performance goal record set for an employee within a performance cycle. Captures goal title, description, goal type (individual, team, corporate, project-specific), weight/percentage, target metric, measurement method, due date, progress status, and achievement rating at review time. Supports cascaded goal-setting from corporate KPIs down to individual construction managers and engineers.'
}

Table hr.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each training course record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate identifier for the instructor (links to HR personnel record).']
  prerequisite_training_course_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_course (prerequisite_training_course_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external body that accredits the course, if any.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the accreditation body.']
  applicable_job_profile_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job profile codes for which the course is relevant or required.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to evaluate participant competence.']
  assessment_pass_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target pass percentage required for participants to receive credit.']
  certification_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion results in a formal certification.']
  cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost to the organization for delivering the course (e.g., external provider fees).']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the training course, used in catalogs and enrollment systems.']
  course_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the course by business purpose or subject area.']
  cpd_points decimal(5,2) [note: 'Accredited CPD points awarded upon successful completion of the course.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the course record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the course cost.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where classroom sessions are held (null for virtual).']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Primary method by which the course is delivered to participants.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned total instructional time for the course expressed in hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the course is retired or no longer offered (null if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the course becomes available for enrollment.']
  enrollment_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants allowed for a single offering of the course.']
  enrollment_current varchar [note: 'Number of participants currently enrolled in the upcoming offering.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary instructor or facilitator.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the course material is delivered.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the course content was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course is required for a specific job profile or compliance purpose.']
  material_url varchar [note: 'Link to downloadable course materials, slides, or handouts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks, special instructions, or administrative comments.']
  online_url varchar [note: 'Web address for accessing the e‑learning version of the course.']
  prerequisite_course_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of course codes that must be completed before enrolling.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between mandatory content reviews.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary employee segment for which the course is intended.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the training course.']
  training_course_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the content, objectives, and outcomes of the training course.']
  training_course_level varchar [note: 'Skill level targeted by the course.']
  training_course_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the course.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the course record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for the course content.']

  Note: 'Master record for each training course or learning program available to corporate and management staff. Captures course code, course title, course type (technical, leadership, compliance, HSE awareness, project management, BIM/digital tools), delivery mode (classroom, e-learning, on-the-job, external), duration, provider, CPD points, mandatory flag, and applicable job profiles. Distinct from field safety inductions (safety domain) and craft certifications (workforce domain).'
}

Table hr.training_enrollment {
  training_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the training enrollment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who is enrolled in the training.']
  rescheduled_training_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_enrollment (rescheduled_training_enrollment_id)']
  training_course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the training course or program.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score achieved on the training assessment, typically out of 100.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the issued certificate expires, if applicable.']
  certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certificate was issued after successful completion.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issued certificate.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the employee completed the training.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the training is required for regulatory or safety compliance.']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of the training for the organization.']
  cpd_points varchar [note: 'Number of CPD points awarded for completing the training.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the training cost.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode in which the training is delivered.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible unique enrollment code assigned to the record.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment was recorded in the system.']
  hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Total number of hours allocated for the training.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the site or facility where the training takes place (if in‑person).']
  pass_fail_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether the employee passed or failed.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the training is to be delivered.']
  training_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the enrollment lifecycle.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the external or internal organization delivering the training.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training (e.g., mandatory, optional, refresher).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an employee\'s enrollment in and completion of a training course. Captures enrollment date, scheduled delivery date, completion date, completion status (enrolled, in-progress, completed, failed, cancelled), assessment score, pass/fail outcome, certificate issued flag, certificate expiry date, and CPD points earned. Tracks mandatory compliance training completion rates for corporate staff.'
}

Table hr.disciplinary_case {
  disciplinary_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the disciplinary case record.']
  primary_disciplinary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee subject to the disciplinary case.']
  related_disciplinary_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on disciplinary_case (related_disciplinary_case_id)']
  tertiary_disciplinary_hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner assigned to the case.']
  appeal_date date [note: 'Date when the appeal decision was rendered.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee filed an appeal against the outcome.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process, if an appeal was filed.']
  case_closed_date date [note: 'Date when the disciplinary case was formally closed.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disciplinary case.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case for handling urgency.']
  case_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the case report.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the disciplinary case indicating the nature of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured in the system.']
  disciplinary_action_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the disciplinary action (e.g., warning) takes effect.']
  disciplinary_case_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the incident or issue leading to the case.']
  disciplinary_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disciplinary case.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case is treated as confidential per HR policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary notes or comments added by HR or investigators.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final disciplinary action taken as a result of the case.']
  outcome_date date [note: 'Date when the disciplinary outcome became effective.']
  raised_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disciplinary case was initially created.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control and audit.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑5) reflecting the seriousness of the outcome.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Effective date of employment termination, if the outcome is termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the case record.']

  Note: 'Formal disciplinary case record for a salaried employee, capturing case number, case type (misconduct, performance, attendance, policy breach, code of conduct violation), date raised, investigating manager, HR business partner assigned, case status (open, under investigation, outcome issued, appealed, closed), outcome (verbal warning, written warning, final warning, termination), and appeal outcome. Maintains confidential HR case management records.'
}

Table hr.grievance {
  grievance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grievance record.']
  escalated_grievance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on grievance (escalated_grievance_id)']
  grievance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who raised the grievance.']
  grievance_hr_case_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'HR staff member responsible for managing the grievance case.']
  grievance_respondent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager against whom the grievance is filed.']
  compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation awarded as part of the grievance resolution, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grievance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for any monetary compensation.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the grievance was escalated to an external body.']
  escalation_external_body varchar [note: 'External authority to which the grievance was escalated, if any.']
  grievance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the grievance type.']
  grievance_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the grievance submitted by the complainant.']
  grievance_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the grievance, used for tracking and reference.']
  grievance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grievance.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date on which the incident that gave rise to the grievance occurred.']
  investigation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation of the grievance concluded.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation of the grievance began.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the grievance investigation.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site or location where the grievance incident occurred.']
  lodged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grievance was formally lodged.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary notes added by HR during case handling.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the grievance was formally resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the grievance after investigation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the grievance record.']

  Note: 'Employee grievance record capturing formal complaints raised by salaried staff regarding workplace issues — harassment, discrimination, unfair treatment, unsafe conditions, or management conduct. Captures grievance number, complainant, respondent, grievance category, date lodged, HR case owner, investigation status, resolution outcome, and escalation to external body (Fair Work Commission, EEOC, employment tribunal). Supports labor compliance and ER case management.'
}

Table hr.compensation_review {
  compensation_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each compensation review record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR officer who approved the review.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s position at the time of review.']
  primary_compensation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee whose compensation is being reviewed.']
  prior_compensation_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_review (prior_compensation_review_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the compensation review.']
  budget_consumed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of the budget pool consumed by this salary increase.']
  budget_pool_code varchar [note: 'Code of the compensation budget pool from which the increase is drawn.']
  budget_remaining_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Remaining amount in the budget pool after this review.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the reviewer.']
  compensation_component varchar [note: 'Component of compensation being adjusted (e.g., base salary, bonus, equity).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for salary amounts.']
  current_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employee\'s base salary amount before the review.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department to which the employee belongs.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the approved compensation changes become effective.']
  grade_level varchar [note: 'Grade level associated with the employee\'s position.']
  increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change applied to the current salary.']
  increase_type varchar [note: 'Reason for salary change (e.g., merit, market adjustment, promotion, equity, bonus).']
  internal_equity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s pay equity relative to peers.']
  is_equity_granted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether equity compensation was granted.']
  is_promoted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the employee received a promotion during the review.']
  is_salary_adjusted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the salary was changed as a result of the review.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale supporting the proposed salary change.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic location identifier for the employee\'s primary work site.']
  market_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'External market salary benchmark for the employee\'s role.']
  new_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final salary amount after the review is approved.']
  pay_band varchar [note: 'Pay band or salary range classification for the employee.']
  proposed_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Salary amount proposed by the review before final approval.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The scheduled cycle of the compensation review (e.g., annual, mid‑year, off‑cycle).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the compensation review was conducted.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the review cycle, used for tracking and audit.']
  review_period_end date [note: 'End date of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the compensation review record.']
  review_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the review takes place.']
  salary_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the salary figure represents base, total, or target compensation.']

  Note: 'Annual or off-cycle compensation review record capturing the salary review cycle, employee current salary, proposed salary increase, increase type (merit, market adjustment, promotion, equity), percentage increase, new salary, effective date, approval status, approver, and budget pool consumed. Supports remuneration benchmarking, pay equity analysis, and compensation governance for construction management and professional staff.'
}

Table hr.succession_plan {
  succession_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the succession plan record.']
  primary_succession_incumbent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee currently holding the target position.']
  superseded_succession_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on succession_plan (superseded_succession_plan_id)']
  target_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the critical position for which the succession plan is created.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the succession plan was formally approved by senior leadership.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the succession plan record was first created in the system.']
  development_actions varchar [note: 'Planned development activities, training, or experiences required for the successor.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the succession plan expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the succession plan becomes active.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the plan in HR systems and reports.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the succession plan, typically indicating the critical role or business unit.']
  plan_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for formal review and update of the succession plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., critical leadership, technical specialist).']
  readiness_level varchar [note: 'Readiness assessment of the successor (immediate, 1‑2 years, 3‑5 years).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the succession gap (e.g., flight risk, retirement risk).']
  succession_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the succession plan record.']

  Note: 'Succession planning record identifying critical positions and their designated successors within the construction enterprise. Captures the target position, incumbent employee, successor candidate, readiness level (ready now, ready in 1-2 years, ready in 3-5 years), development actions required, risk rating (flight risk, retirement risk), and plan review date. Supports talent pipeline management for senior construction leadership and project director roles.'
}

Table hr.onboarding_checklist {
  onboarding_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the onboarding checklist instance.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site associated with the onboarding.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  onboarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  onboarding_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the onboarding checklist template used for this employee.']
  parent_onboarding_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on onboarding_checklist (parent_onboarding_checklist_id)']
  primary_onboarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the new hire employee.']
  tertiary_onboarding_buddy_assigned_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned as the onboarding buddy.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when the employee acknowledged the required policies.']
  acknowledgment_received boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the employee has acknowledged required policies.']
  background_check_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee\'s background check is completed.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date when the background check was completed.']
  benefits_enrollment_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if benefits enrollment is completed.']
  benefits_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when benefits enrollment was completed.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of tasks completed (0‑100).']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if compliance training (e.g., OSHA) is completed.']
  compliance_training_date date [note: 'Date when compliance training was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding checklist record was created.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date of the onboarding process.']
  equipment_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates if required equipment has been assigned to the employee.']
  equipment_assigned_date date [note: 'Date when equipment was assigned to the employee.']
  health_safety_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if health and safety training required by OSHA/ISO 45001 is completed.']
  health_safety_training_date date [note: 'Date when health and safety training was completed.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the onboarding process is mandatory for the employee\'s role.']
  last_task_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent onboarding task was completed.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary work location or site for the employee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or comments related to the onboarding process.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current overall status of the onboarding checklist.']
  payroll_setup_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if payroll setup is completed for the employee.']
  payroll_setup_date date [note: 'Date when payroll setup was completed.']
  policy_acknowledged varchar [note: 'Specific policy that was acknowledged by the employee.']
  requires_security_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires a security clearance.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Required security clearance level for the employee.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the onboarding process begins for the employee.']
  task_completed varchar [note: 'Number of onboarding tasks that have been marked as completed.']
  task_total varchar [note: 'Total number of tasks defined in the selected onboarding template.']
  training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates if mandatory training has been completed.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when mandatory training was completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding checklist.']
  visa_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s visa.']
  visa_status varchar [note: 'Current visa status of the employee, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Structured onboarding task checklist instance created for each new hire joining the construction enterprise. Captures the employee, start date, onboarding template used, assigned HR coordinator, list of onboarding tasks (IT setup, security access, site induction scheduling, policy acknowledgements, payroll setup, benefits enrollment, buddy assignment), task completion status, and overall onboarding completion percentage. Ensures consistent onboarding experience for new construction management and office staff.'
}

Table hr.separation {
  separation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the employee separation event.']
  rehire_separation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on separation (rehire_separation_id)']
  separation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who is separating.']
  separation_exit_interview_conducted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR representative who conducted the exit interview.']
  separation_processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR staff member who processed the separation.']
  benefits_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which employee benefits coverage ends.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date the compliance review was performed.']
  compliance_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance (e.g., HSE, legal) review was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the separation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_separation_date date [note: 'Date the separation becomes legally effective (often same as last working day).']
  equipment_return_date date [note: 'Date on which all required equipment was returned.']
  equipment_return_status varchar [note: 'Status of company equipment returned by the employee.']
  exit_interview_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee completed an exit interview (True/False).']
  exit_interview_feedback varchar [note: 'Free‑text field capturing themes or comments from the exit interview.']
  exit_interview_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑5) summarizing the employee\'s exit interview responses.']
  exit_interview_theme varchar [note: 'Key thematic area identified from the exit interview (e.g., compensation, culture).']
  final_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount to be paid to the employee on separation.']
  final_pay_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the employee\'s final paycheck.']
  last_working_day date [note: 'Final day the employee is scheduled to work for the organization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by HR staff.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date the employee submitted their notice of separation.']
  payroll_cutoff_date date [note: 'Final payroll processing date for the employee.']
  rehire_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for future rehire.']
  separation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier or reference number for the separation.']
  separation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the separation process.']
  separation_type varchar [note: 'Category of separation such as resignation, retirement, termination, etc.']
  severance_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount paid as severance, if applicable.']
  severance_pay_status varchar [note: 'Current status of severance payment processing.']
  system_access_revocation_date date [note: 'Date when all system access rights were revoked.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the reason for termination, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the separation record.']

  Note: 'Employee separation (offboarding) record capturing the full exit lifecycle for departing salaried staff. Captures separation type (resignation, redundancy, retirement, termination, end of contract, death in service), notice date, last working day, exit interview completion flag, exit interview feedback themes, final pay calculation status, equipment return status, system access revocation date, and rehire eligibility flag. Supports workforce attrition analysis and compliance with termination obligations.'
}

Table hr.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the HR policy record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Associating each HR policy with an org unit provides clear scope and eliminates the generic department list column, ensuring policies are tied to the organizational hierarchy.']
  superseded_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on policy (superseded_policy_id)']
  applicable_jurisdictions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the policy applies.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or committee that approved the policy.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or business driver for the most recent policy change.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or internal compliance obligations addressed by the policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was initially created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the authoritative policy document stored in the document management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy becomes effective and binding.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Degree to which compliance with the policy is required.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the policy expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended policies).']
  mandatory_acknowledgement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees must formally acknowledge the policy (true) or not (false).']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'Category of the policy (e.g., leave, code of conduct, anti-harassment, remuneration, flexible work, travel).']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the policy, used in external references and documentation.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the policy content and purpose.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp capturing when the record was first persisted for audit purposes.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp capturing the last modification of the record for audit purposes.']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next policy review to ensure relevance and compliance.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the HR policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the policy record.']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing changes made in this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the policy (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']

  Note: 'Master register of all HR policies and procedures applicable to the construction enterprise\'s salaried workforce. Captures policy code, policy title, policy category (leave, code of conduct, anti-harassment, remuneration, flexible work, travel, expense, health and wellbeing), version number, effective date, review date, approving authority, applicable jurisdictions, and mandatory acknowledgement flag. Supports labor compliance and audit readiness.'
}

Table hr.workforce_headcount_plan {
  workforce_headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each workforce headcount plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner or manager who approved the plan.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the org unit (department, division, or cost centre) to which the plan applies.']
  prior_workforce_headcount_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on workforce_headcount_plan (prior_workforce_headcount_plan_id)']
  actual_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed headcount in full‑time equivalents at the end of the period.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the plan received formal approval.']
  approved_backfills varchar [note: 'Number of backfill positions approved to replace attrition.']
  approved_new_hires varchar [note: 'Number of new hires authorized in the plan for the period.']
  attrition_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed attrition percentage used for forecasting headcount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the headcount plan record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the headcount plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the headcount plan becomes effective.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional grouping of positions (e.g., Engineering, Finance, HR).']
  job_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or level within the job family used for compensation and hierarchy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, assumptions, or rationale associated with the plan.']
  period varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar period the plan covers (e.g., 2024_Q1). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2023_Q1|2023_Q2|2023_Q3|2023_Q4|2024_Q1|2024_Q2|2024_Q3|2024_Q4 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable plan number or code used for external reference and reporting.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the headcount plan indicating its planning horizon.']
  planned_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved budgeted headcount expressed in full‑time equivalents for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the headcount plan record.']
  variance_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between planned and actual FTE (planned_fte - actual_fte).']
  workforce_headcount_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the headcount plan.']

  Note: 'Corporate workforce headcount planning record defining the approved FTE budget by org unit, job family, grade, and period (monthly/quarterly/annual). Captures planned headcount, actual headcount, variance, approved new hires, approved backfills, and attrition assumptions. Supports annual budgeting, project mobilization planning for office staff, and HR business partner reporting. Distinct from field craft labor staffing plans (workforce domain).'
}

Table hr.kpi {
  kpi_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kpi']
  alignment_kpi_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kpi (alignment_kpi_id)']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Historical baseline against which current performance is compared.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the formula or logic used to compute the KPI.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the KPI.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance rule linked to the KPI.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification for the KPI definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KPI record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) indicating the quality of the source data.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or process that provides the raw data used for the KPI.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business definition and purpose of the KPI.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the KPI becomes effective for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the KPI is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the KPI is measured or reported.']
  is_key_performance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this metric is a core KPI for the organization.']
  kpi_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the KPI is leading, lagging, or both.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the KPI definition was last reviewed.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for the KPI value (e.g., percent, days, dollars).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the key performance indicator.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the KPI.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the KPI.']
  related_process varchar [note: 'Name of the business process that the KPI measures.']
  reporting_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level at which the KPI is reported.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the KPI definition is retained before archival.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the KPI definition is reviewed for relevance.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KPI definition.']
  target_direction varchar [note: 'Desired direction of change for the KPI (e.g., increase).']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Desired numeric target for the KPI.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Acceptable lower or upper bound for the KPI before it is considered out of range.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the KPI record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the KPI definition for change management.']
  visualization_type varchar [note: 'Preferred visual representation for the KPI in reports.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for kpi. Referenced by alignment_kpi_id.'
}

Table hr.payroll_run {
  payroll_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_run']
  payroll_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee group or cost center to which this payroll run applies.']
  prior_payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_run (prior_payroll_run_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the payroll amounts.']
  deductions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all statutory and voluntary deductions for the payroll run.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special notes for the payroll run.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct employees included in this payroll run.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Quarter of the fiscal year for reporting purposes.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross earnings before any deductions for the payroll run.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the payroll run was initiated manually rather than by an automated schedule.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount payable to employees after deductions.']
  pay_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the payroll run is generated.']
  pay_date date [note: 'Date on which employees receive their net pay for this run.']
  payroll_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this run.']
  payroll_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this run.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run (e.g., regular salary, bonus, overtime, severance).']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll run completed processing and was ready for posting.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the payroll run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the payroll run was executed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating HR/Payroll system that generated the payroll run record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payroll run.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the payroll taxes are reported.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payroll run record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the payroll run record for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll group.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_group. Referenced by payroll_group_id.'
